# Create Sealed Hold worktree

## User
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-21T15:32:22.792Z


# Files mentioned by the user:

## # PROJECT SEALED HOLD ## Phase 1: Lock the Chronicle ### Private Content Bounda…: C:\Users\kgray\.codex/attachments/7f049257-a65f-432f-bb70-f1d75e8505a4/pasted-text.txt

## My request for Codex:
# ADDENDUM: PARALLEL EXECUTION WITH PROJECT WAYFARER

## Independent Worktree, Shared Baseline, Branch Isolation, and Future Reconciliation

Project Sealed Hold Phase 1 will run **at the same time as Project Wayfarer Stage/Phase 1**.

The two programs must proceed independently without sharing a working directory, modifying each other’s branches, or assuming the other program has already finished.

Project Sealed Hold must therefore create its own branch and worktree from the newest approved repository commit before implementation begins.

---

# 1. Parallel-Execution Rule

Treat Project Wayfarer and Project Sealed Hold as two independent feature programs:

```text
Project Wayfarer
  Identity, accounts, profiles, privacy, personal history,
  linked identities, memberships, achievements, and related systems

Project Sealed Hold
  Private Chronicle packages, encrypted content boundaries,
  private asset storage, import/export, backup, and secure delivery
```

They may share architectural concepts, but they must not share a mutable checkout.

Neither program may:

* commit directly to the other program’s branch;
* use the other program’s worktree;
* merge the other branch while work is ongoing;
* cherry-pick incomplete commits from the other branch;
* rebase the other branch;
* force-update the other branch;
* assume uncommitted work in another checkout is available;
* use the canonical dirty working tree for implementation.

Both programs should begin from the same newest approved remote baseline whenever practical.

---

# 2. Determine the Authoritative Starting Commit

Before creating the Sealed Hold branch:

1. Locate the canonical repository.
2. Record the current local branch and commit.
3. Preserve all staged, unstaged and untracked work.
4. Run a non-destructive remote update:

```bash
git fetch --all --prune
```

5. Determine the repository’s authoritative integration branch.

Use:

```text
origin/main
```

unless repository documentation, current project-status metadata, or an explicitly accepted integration branch identifies another branch as authoritative.

Do not guess based on whichever local branch happens to be checked out.

6. Record:

```text
authoritative branch
authoritative remote ref
authoritative commit SHA
commit date
commit subject
```

7. Verify that the selected commit is available locally.
8. Verify that it includes the newest work already accepted and pushed before this task began.
9. Do not include uncommitted changes from any checkout in the baseline.
10. Do not wait for Project Wayfarer Phase 1 to finish.

The Sealed Hold branch must be created from the latest accepted remote commit available **at the moment Sealed Hold begins**.

If Project Wayfarer has already created its branch from that same commit, record that both projects share a common base SHA.

If Wayfarer began from an older commit, do not move or modify the Wayfarer branch. Record the baseline difference for later reconciliation.

---

# 3. Create a Dedicated Sealed Hold Worktree

Create a sibling worktree outside the canonical checkout.

Recommended branch:

```text
codex/project-sealed-hold-phase1
```

Recommended worktree directory:

```text
../treasurehuntSoT-sealed-hold-phase1
```

Use the exact repository name and safe local path appropriate to the environment.

A representative command is:

```bash
git worktree add \
  -b codex/project-sealed-hold-phase1 \
  ../treasurehuntSoT-sealed-hold-phase1 \
  <AUTHORITATIVE_REMOTE_REF>
```

If the branch already exists:

1. Do not delete or overwrite it.
2. Inspect its current commit.
3. Inspect its upstream.
4. Determine whether it belongs to an earlier Sealed Hold attempt.
5. Resume it only if its provenance is clear and it is based on an acceptable commit.
6. Otherwise create a uniquely named recovery branch such as:

```text
codex/project-sealed-hold-phase1-2
```

Do not force-reset an existing branch merely to reuse the preferred name.

After creating the worktree, verify:

```bash
git -C ../treasurehuntSoT-sealed-hold-phase1 status
git -C ../treasurehuntSoT-sealed-hold-phase1 branch --show-current
git -C ../treasurehuntSoT-sealed-hold-phase1 rev-parse HEAD
git -C ../treasurehuntSoT-sealed-hold-phase1 rev-parse --show-toplevel
```

The new worktree must be:

* on the dedicated Sealed Hold branch;
* clean before implementation;
* based on the recorded authoritative commit;
* physically separate from the canonical checkout;
* physically separate from the Wayfarer worktree.

Do not implement Sealed Hold inside the original dirty checkout.

---

# 4. Discover the Concurrent Wayfarer Branch

Inspect local and remote branches for the active Wayfarer implementation branch.

Likely names may include:

```text
codex/project-wayfarer-phase1
codex/wayfarer-phase1
feature/project-wayfarer-phase1
```

Do not assume one of these names exists.

Use branch inspection and recent commit metadata to identify the real branch.

Record:

```text
Wayfarer branch name
Wayfarer worktree path, if local and visible
Wayfarer starting SHA
Wayfarer current SHA
shared base SHA with Sealed Hold
```

This inspection is read-only.

Do not:

* check out the Wayfarer branch in the Sealed Hold worktree;
* modify Wayfarer files;
* commit to Wayfarer;
* merge Wayfarer;
* rebase Wayfarer;
* rewrite Wayfarer history.

If the Wayfarer branch cannot be identified, continue Sealed Hold independently and record that the concurrent branch was not discoverable.

Do not block implementation merely because another Codex conversation did not leave perfect breadcrumbs. Software agents, like pirates, are occasionally poor cartographers.

---

# 5. Create a Parallel-Work Coordination Record

Before broad implementation, create:

```text
Development_Docs/Project_Sealed_Hold_Parallel_Work_Record.md
```

Record:

* Sealed Hold branch;
* Sealed Hold worktree;
* authoritative base ref;
* authoritative base SHA;
* Wayfarer branch if discoverable;
* Wayfarer base SHA if discoverable;
* shared base SHA;
* known overlapping subsystems;
* files Sealed Hold expects to own;
* files likely to require reconciliation;
* files Sealed Hold must avoid where possible;
* decisions deferred until merge;
* current date and responsible implementation thread.

Update this record during implementation when actual overlap is discovered.

Do not use the coordination record to copy private content or secrets.

---

# 6. Expected Collision Areas

Project Wayfarer and Project Sealed Hold may both need to touch:

```text
prisma/schema.prisma
Prisma migrations
authentication helpers
authorization helpers
invitation models
Player and Creator identity references
privacy settings
asset ownership
artifact ownership
audit events
Studio navigation
Studio settings
API route guards
environment documentation
SECURITY.md
README.md
package.json
project-status metadata
```

These are high-conflict files.

Before changing any of them:

1. Inspect the current Sealed Hold baseline.
2. Inspect the concurrent Wayfarer branch version if available.
3. Determine whether Sealed Hold truly requires the change.
4. Prefer a new isolated module or adapter when possible.
5. Record the reason for touching the shared file.
6. Keep the patch narrow.
7. Do not reproduce or partially imitate Wayfarer’s unmerged implementation.
8. Add focused tests around the Sealed Hold behavior.

---

# 7. Ownership Boundaries

## 7.1 Sealed Hold owns

Project Sealed Hold should primarily own new files under the equivalent of:

```text
src/private-content/
scripts/private-content/
tests/private-content/
Development_Docs/Project_Sealed_Hold_*
```

It also owns narrowly scoped additions for:

* encrypted package handling;
* package schemas;
* archive validation;
* private asset storage;
* private asset authorization;
* import/export services;
* package audit receipts;
* private-content backup and restore;
* repository leak scanning;
* Studio private-content administration;
* synthetic private-content fixtures.

## 7.2 Wayfarer owns

Project Sealed Hold must not independently redesign:

* canonical person identity;
* account creation;
* guest account claiming;
* email verification;
* password reset;
* linked Xbox, Steam or Discord identity;
* public profiles;
* Chronicle Passports;
* global privacy-preference schemas;
* unified Player/Captain/Creator identity;
* achievement systems;
* personal Chronicle history;
* general profile statistics;
* general account/session management.

Where Sealed Hold requires identity or authorization, consume the current stable interfaces or introduce a narrow compatibility adapter.

Do not implement a competing identity system.

## 7.3 Shared-domain rule

If Sealed Hold requires a durable reference to an authenticated person, use the smallest currently stable identity abstraction.

Do not tightly bind the private-content package format to legacy identity tables that Wayfarer is expected to replace.

Prefer conceptual references such as:

```text
ownerPersonId
createdByPersonId
authorizedActorId
```

only when supported by the current schema or through an adapter.

If the canonical Wayfarer identity model is still unmerged, document the expected future mapping rather than copying unfinished Wayfarer models.

---

# 8. Prisma and Migration Protocol

Prisma is the most likely collision point.

Humanity created relational databases, then invented concurrent feature branches so that two people could disagree about the same foreign key from different directories.

Follow these rules:

1. Only modify Prisma if Sealed Hold cannot satisfy durable import, asset or idempotency requirements with current models.
2. Keep Sealed Hold models narrowly scoped to private-content operations.
3. Do not modify Wayfarer-owned account/profile models.
4. Do not rename shared identity columns during this branch.
5. Do not alter invitation identity semantics unless strictly required.
6. Create a uniquely named migration.
7. Do not reuse a Wayfarer migration timestamp or identifier.
8. Do not edit a migration already committed on another branch.
9. Document every expected merge conflict.
10. Generate the Prisma client only from the Sealed Hold worktree.
11. Run migrations only against an isolated Sealed Hold test database.
12. Never point Sealed Hold mutation tests at the Wayfarer test database.
13. Never point either program at the canonical database.

Create a migration reconciliation note containing:

```text
Sealed Hold migration identifier
base schema SHA
models added
columns added
indexes added
constraints added
expected Wayfarer interaction
required post-merge regeneration
```

At later integration, migrations from both branches must remain separate and ordered.

Do not squash them into one improvised migration without reviewing production compatibility.

---

# 9. Package Format Must Remain Identity-Neutral

The encrypted private package must not hardcode unstable internal account IDs as its only ownership representation.

Package content should describe:

* Tale identity;
* package identity;
* content identity;
* asset identity;
* authorship metadata where appropriate;
* import ownership intent.

It should not require that the destination installation already contains the exact originating database user ID.

Use import-time ownership binding.

For example:

```text
Package contains creator metadata or an external creator reference.
Import operation binds imported content to the authenticated importing Creator.
```

Do not export:

* password hashes;
* login sessions;
* email verification secrets;
* password-reset records;
* linked-platform tokens;
* invitation tokens;
* internal session IDs;
* Wayfarer private-profile internals unrelated to the Tale.

This prevents Sealed Hold from becoming coupled to whichever identity schema happens to exist before Wayfarer merges.

---

# 10. Authorization Compatibility Layer

Create a narrow authorization boundary for private-content operations.

Conceptually, Sealed Hold should ask questions such as:

```text
May this actor administer private Chronicle packages?
May this actor inspect this private Tale?
May this actor export this Tale?
May this actor access this protected asset?
May this Player access this revealed asset in this playthrough?
```

Do not scatter assumptions about current PlayerProfile or Game Master tables through package code.

If necessary, create an adapter such as:

```ts
interface PrivateContentAuthorization {
  canAdministerPackages(actor: AuthenticatedActor): Promise<boolean>;
  canImportForOwner(
    actor: AuthenticatedActor,
    ownerId: string,
  ): Promise<boolean>;
  canExportTale(
    actor: AuthenticatedActor,
    taleId: string,
  ): Promise<boolean>;
  canReadPrivateAsset(
    actor: AuthenticatedActor | null,
    assetId: string,
    context: PrivateAssetAccessContext,
  ): Promise<boolean>;
}
```

The exact interface may differ.

The important requirement is that Wayfarer can later replace the identity source without forcing changes throughout encryption, archive and storage modules.

Add adapter contract tests.

---

# 11. Studio Integration Boundary

Project Sealed Hold may add its private-content administration screens.

However:

* do not redesign the entire Studio account/profile area;
* do not replace Wayfarer navigation concepts;
* do not introduce a second profile switcher;
* do not add independent account settings;
* do not add another global privacy settings model;
* do not create a new Creator identity system.

Prefer:

* a dedicated private-content route;
* a small isolated navigation registration;
* feature-level authorization;
* universal language keys;
* no broad rewrite of shared Studio shells.

If Wayfarer changes Studio navigation concurrently, record the expected reconciliation rather than repeatedly rebasing.

---

# 12. Runtime and Port Isolation

The two programs must not share runtime resources.

Sealed Hold must use:

* its own server port;
* its own test database;
* its own asset root;
* its own private staging root;
* its own temporary package directory;
* its own Playwright output directory where configurable.

Recommended example:

```text
PORT=3117
DATABASE_URL=<isolated-sealed-hold-database>
PRIVATE_CONTENT_ROOT=<isolated-sealed-hold-assets>
PRIVATE_CONTENT_STAGING_ROOT=<isolated-sealed-hold-staging>
```

Do not assume these exact values are free.

Verify ownership before use.

Do not stop:

* the Wayfarer development server;
* the Wayfarer test server;
* another Codex process;
* the canonical local application;
* an unknown process on port 3000.

Record the Sealed Hold runtime identity before mutating tests.

---

# 13. Dependency Changes

If Sealed Hold requires new packages:

1. Inspect concurrent Wayfarer dependency changes if visible.
2. Modify `package.json` narrowly.
3. Regenerate the lockfile only in the Sealed Hold worktree.
4. Record added packages and reasons.
5. Avoid upgrading unrelated dependencies.
6. Avoid formatting the entire lockfile through a different package-manager version.
7. Use the repository’s existing package manager.
8. Do not resolve anticipated Wayfarer lockfile conflicts by importing its unfinished lockfile.

Lockfile reconciliation happens during integration.

---

# 14. Do Not Continuously Rebase During Parallel Work

Once Sealed Hold begins implementation from the recorded base:

* do not repeatedly rebase onto changing `main`;
* do not repeatedly merge Wayfarer;
* do not chase every new remote commit;
* do not mix unrelated concurrent changes into the branch.

Finish Sealed Hold against its stable base.

A stable base makes later comparison and reconciliation deterministic.

If an urgent security or repository-wide fix lands on `main` during implementation:

1. Identify the exact commit.
2. Determine whether Sealed Hold depends on it.
3. Cherry-pick only that isolated accepted commit if necessary.
4. Record the cherry-pick.
5. Do not use this exception as permission to absorb unrelated work.

---

# 15. Completion Behavior

At the end of Sealed Hold Phase 1:

1. Run all Sealed Hold focused validations.
2. Run the repository’s full validation only from the Sealed Hold-owned isolated environment.
3. Run the private-content leak scanner.
4. Inspect the staged diff.
5. Confirm no private content or secrets exist.
6. Commit only Sealed Hold work.
7. Push:

```text
origin/codex/project-sealed-hold-phase1
```

or the actual dedicated branch name.

8. Do not merge into `main`.
9. Do not merge Wayfarer into Sealed Hold.
10. Do not merge Sealed Hold into Wayfarer.
11. Do not delete either worktree.
12. Stop for coordinated integration.

The final report must include:

```text
Sealed Hold branch
Sealed Hold commit
Sealed Hold starting SHA
authoritative base ref
Wayfarer branch, if discovered
Wayfarer current SHA, if discovered
shared base SHA
ahead/behind relationships
shared files changed
expected merge conflicts
migration-order requirements
lockfile reconciliation requirements
Studio reconciliation requirements
authorization-adapter reconciliation requirements
recommended integration order
```

---

# 16. Recommended Later Integration Order

Do not perform this integration during the current task.

Document the recommended order as follows unless implementation evidence supports a different order:

1. Complete and accept Wayfarer Stage/Phase 1.
2. Complete and accept Sealed Hold Phase 1 independently.
3. Create a fresh integration branch from the newest accepted `main`.
4. Merge or cherry-pick Wayfarer using repository policy.
5. Resolve and validate Wayfarer completely.
6. Merge or cherry-pick Sealed Hold.
7. Reconcile identity adapters.
8. Reconcile Prisma schema and retain both migration histories.
9. Regenerate Prisma client.
10. Reconcile `package.json` and lockfile.
11. Reconcile Studio navigation.
12. Reconcile universal-language resources.
13. Run private-content leak scanning.
14. Run focused Wayfarer tests.
15. Run focused Sealed Hold tests.
16. Run combined authorization tests.
17. Run isolated migration tests.
18. Run production build.
19. Run the full validation gate once.
20. Create an integration manifest and hash.
21. Commit and push the integration branch.
22. Merge only after explicit review and acceptance.

Do not assume that a clean Git merge means the systems are behaviorally compatible.

A merge without conflicts merely means Git did not notice the argument.

---

# 17. Additional Completion Gate

Project Sealed Hold Phase 1 must not be declared complete unless:

1. It was implemented in a separate worktree.
2. It was implemented on a dedicated branch.
3. Its base commit was recorded.
4. The branch was created from the newest approved remote baseline available when work began.
5. The canonical dirty checkout remained untouched.
6. The Wayfarer branch remained untouched.
7. Sealed Hold used separate runtime resources.
8. Shared-file changes were minimized and documented.
9. No competing identity architecture was introduced.
10. Package ownership remains compatible with future Wayfarer identity migration.
11. Prisma changes are independently migratable.
12. The Sealed Hold branch is committed and pushed.
13. No automatic merge occurred.
14. A detailed reconciliation report exists.
15. No real private Chronicle content entered either branch.

Proceed immediately with creating the Sealed Hold worktree and branch, then implement the full Project Sealed Hold Phase 1 prompt from that isolated environment.

Mission\r\n\r\nImplement **Phase 1 of Project Sealed Hold**, the private-content architecture for the Forever Treasure / Tall Tale platform.\r\n\r\nThis phase is called:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nLock the Chronicle\r\n```\r\n\r\nIts formal engineering scope is:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPrivate Content Boundaries, Encrypted Chronicle Packages,\r\nSecure Asset Storage, Import/Export Safety, and Deployment Isolation\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe repository is intended to remain a reusable, generic application engine.\r\n\r\nThe real Chronicle may eventually contain:\r\n\r\n* private story text;\r\n* personal dedications;\r\n* unpublished riddles and accepted answers;\r\n* photographs;\r\n* artwork;\r\n* audio;\r\n* video;\r\n* 2D artifact imagery;\r\n* future 3D artifact models;\r\n* creator-only notes;\r\n* surprise reveals;\r\n* private invitation wording;\r\n* finale material;\r\n* content intended for only one Player or one crew.\r\n\r\nThat material must not be treated like ordinary source code.\r\n\r\nThe goal of this phase is to create a secure, versioned architecture in which:\r\n\r\n1. The public engine repository remains generic and safe to share.\r\n2. Private Chronicle content exists outside Git history.\r\n3. Private content can be packaged, encrypted, validated, imported, backed up, restored and deployed.\r\n4. Importing content does not automatically publish it.\r\n5. Publishing content does not automatically expose every asset.\r\n6. Player access remains controlled by existing Tale, session, invitation and visibility rules.\r\n7. Private assets are never stored under a public static directory.\r\n8. Package decryption credentials are never committed, logged or persisted insecurely.\r\n9. Tampered, malformed or incompatible packages fail closed.\r\n10. Failed imports do not leave partially created Tales or orphaned private assets.\r\n11. Re-importing the same package is deterministic and idempotent.\r\n12. Existing generic seed content and current public behavior remain intact.\r\n13. Future 2D and 3D artifact assets can be carried safely without implementing the full artifact renderer during this phase.\r\n\r\nThe core rule is:\r\n\r\n> The repository contains the engine. The sealed package contains the Chronicle.\r\n\r\nAnother governing rule is:\r\n\r\n> Gitignore is a convenience, not a security boundary.\r\n\r\nA file being ignored does not make it safe. The implementation must include active validation, scanning, access control, storage isolation and operational documentation.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 2. Current Repository Context\r\n\r\nImplementation must target the **current checked-out canonical repository state**.\r\n\r\nDo not assume that the repository still matches an earlier audit commit.\r\n\r\nBefore editing:\r\n\r\n1. Determine the repository root.\r\n2. Record:\r\n\r\n   * current branch;\r\n   * current commit;\r\n   * default branch;\r\n   * upstream;\r\n   * ahead/behind state;\r\n   * staged changes;\r\n   * unstaged changes;\r\n   * untracked files;\r\n   * linked worktrees.\r\n3. Determine whether:\r\n\r\n   * Lanternwake Phase 5 has been merged;\r\n   * the universal-language overhaul has been merged;\r\n   * any private-content or artifact work already exists;\r\n   * another worktree is modifying Prisma, Studio, assets, security or publishing.\r\n4. Revalidate current models, routes, authorization helpers, asset storage and publishing logic.\r\n5. Preserve all existing work.\r\n6. Do not overwrite newer changes with older documentation or remembered architecture.\r\n7. Do not merge unrelated branches.\r\n8. Do not automatically merge this work into `main`.\r\n\r\nIf concurrent integration work is still occurring, create or use a dedicated worktree and branch such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ncodex/project-sealed-hold-phase-1\r\n```\r\n\r\nBranch from the current approved canonical base.\r\n\r\nDo not decide independently that an unmerged feature branch is authoritative.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 3. Mandatory Governing Review\r\n\r\nBefore writing code, inspect the current versions of all relevant files and systems, including equivalents if paths have changed:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nREADME.md\r\nSECURITY.md\r\n.env.example\r\npackage.json\r\nnext.config.ts\r\nprisma/schema.prisma\r\nsrc/domain/\r\nsrc/lib/\r\nsrc/app/api/\r\nsrc/components/studio/\r\nsrc/components/player/\r\nsrc/components/captain/\r\nsrc/animation/\r\nscripts/\r\ntests/\r\nDevelopment_Docs/\r\n```\r\n\r\nSpecifically identify:\r\n\r\n* Tall Tale models;\r\n* Tale drafts;\r\n* immutable published versions;\r\n* published snapshots;\r\n* assets and asset variants;\r\n* artifacts and artifact references;\r\n* story blocks;\r\n* location references;\r\n* invitation authorization;\r\n* Player memberships;\r\n* Captain/Creator authorization;\r\n* asset upload routes;\r\n* asset-serving routes;\r\n* visibility projection;\r\n* audit-event infrastructure;\r\n* database transaction patterns;\r\n* current local asset root;\r\n* any S3/object-storage abstraction;\r\n* current logging and redaction;\r\n* current background-job capabilities;\r\n* current backup scripts;\r\n* current secret-scanning or CI systems.\r\n\r\nSearch the repository for possible private material.\r\n\r\nThe scan must be read-only.\r\n\r\nDo not print potentially private prose into logs or the final report.\r\n\r\nReport only:\r\n\r\n* number of suspicious files;\r\n* paths;\r\n* classification;\r\n* whether content appears synthetic, generic or potentially private.\r\n\r\nIf actual private content appears to have already entered Git history:\r\n\r\n1. Do not delete it automatically.\r\n2. Do not rewrite history.\r\n3. Do not quote it in reports.\r\n4. Mark it as a security finding.\r\n5. Stop any action that would replicate it.\r\n6. Recommend a separately authorized history-remediation operation.\r\n\r\nHistory rewriting is explicitly outside this implementation phase unless separately approved.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 4. Repository Safety\r\n\r\nNever use destructive repository commands.\r\n\r\nDo not use:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ngit reset --hard\r\ngit clean\r\ngit checkout -- .\r\ngit restore .\r\ngit restore --staged .\r\ngit stash --all\r\nforce push\r\nbroad git add .\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not:\r\n\r\n* modify unrelated user files;\r\n* delete unknown untracked files;\r\n* replace current files with baseline copies;\r\n* stage the entire repository;\r\n* commit private package fixtures containing real content;\r\n* commit decrypted package output;\r\n* commit real keys;\r\n* commit real passwords;\r\n* commit real photos, recordings, riddles or finale material.\r\n\r\nUse targeted staging only.\r\n\r\nPreserve concurrent edits.\r\n\r\nBefore modifying a high-conflict file such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nprisma/schema.prisma\r\npackage.json\r\nSECURITY.md\r\n.env.example\r\nnext.config.ts\r\nStudio navigation files\r\npublished snapshot schemas\r\nasset service modules\r\nauthorization helpers\r\n```\r\n\r\ninspect the current file and recent changes carefully.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 5. Required Phase Design Documents\r\n\r\nBefore broad source implementation, create:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Sealed_Hold_Phase_1_Design_Record.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Private_Content_Threat_Model.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Private_Chronicle_Package_Format.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Private_Content_Operations_Runbook.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Private_Content_Test_Plan.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe design record must freeze the Phase 1 architecture before multiple implementation lanes modify consumers.\r\n\r\nIt must define:\r\n\r\n1. Private-content trust boundaries\r\n2. Package envelope format\r\n3. Decrypted payload format\r\n4. Package manifest schema\r\n5. Package compatibility rules\r\n6. Encryption and key-derivation policy\r\n7. Secret and passphrase handling\r\n8. Temporary-file policy\r\n9. Private asset-storage interface\r\n10. Import planning\r\n11. Conflict resolution\r\n12. Import execution\r\n13. Import rollback\r\n14. Import idempotency\r\n15. Export collection\r\n16. Export validation\r\n17. Package integrity verification\r\n18. Studio authorization policy\r\n19. Player asset-delivery policy\r\n20. Logging and redaction policy\r\n21. Backup and restore policy\r\n22. Future object-storage compatibility\r\n23. Future 3D-asset compatibility\r\n24. Migration strategy\r\n25. Explicit non-goals\r\n\r\nDo not begin parallel changes to imports, exports, routes and Studio until the central interfaces are stable.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 6. Threat Model\r\n\r\nThe threat model must explicitly cover at least the following.\r\n\r\n## 6.1 Accidental Git exposure\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* private files copied into the repository;\r\n* exports written inside the repository;\r\n* decrypted temporary files left under the project root;\r\n* a developer using `git add .`;\r\n* private assets placed under `public/`;\r\n* generated thumbnails copied into tracked directories;\r\n* package keys placed in `.env.example`;\r\n* real content included in test fixtures;\r\n* client-side bundles containing unpublished story text.\r\n\r\n## 6.2 Unauthorized HTTP access\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* guessing an asset URL;\r\n* accessing a private asset without membership;\r\n* using an expired invitation;\r\n* accessing Creator-only content as a Player;\r\n* requesting an unpublished draft through a public route;\r\n* accessing thumbnails when the original asset is protected;\r\n* using stale signed URLs after access revocation;\r\n* cross-Tale asset-reference confusion.\r\n\r\n## 6.3 Package tampering\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* changed encrypted payload;\r\n* changed manifest;\r\n* corrupted authentication tag;\r\n* checksum mismatch;\r\n* missing asset;\r\n* replaced asset;\r\n* unsupported package version;\r\n* invalid schema;\r\n* conflicting IDs;\r\n* altered published version;\r\n* package claiming a different source Tale.\r\n\r\n## 6.4 Malicious archives\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* path traversal;\r\n* absolute paths;\r\n* drive-letter paths;\r\n* symbolic links;\r\n* hard links;\r\n* duplicate paths;\r\n* decompression bombs;\r\n* excessive entry count;\r\n* excessive total extracted size;\r\n* unexpected executable files;\r\n* MIME spoofing;\r\n* nested archives;\r\n* malformed Unicode filenames;\r\n* case-folding collisions;\r\n* reserved Windows filenames.\r\n\r\n## 6.5 Secret leakage through operations\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* logs;\r\n* stack traces;\r\n* audit events;\r\n* analytics;\r\n* error-monitoring payloads;\r\n* shell history;\r\n* process arguments;\r\n* temporary files;\r\n* CI artifacts;\r\n* test screenshots;\r\n* Playwright traces;\r\n* browser local storage;\r\n* browser source maps;\r\n* database dumps;\r\n* backup manifests.\r\n\r\n## 6.6 Partial import corruption\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* database transaction succeeds but asset move fails;\r\n* asset staging succeeds but database transaction fails;\r\n* process crashes during commit;\r\n* duplicate package import;\r\n* concurrent import of the same package;\r\n* two packages contain conflicting Tales;\r\n* imported asset becomes visible before authorization state exists.\r\n\r\n## 6.7 Loss and recovery\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* private package lost;\r\n* key lost;\r\n* database restored without assets;\r\n* assets restored without matching database;\r\n* stale backup;\r\n* package version no longer readable;\r\n* backup tampering;\r\n* incomplete restore.\r\n\r\nFor each threat, document:\r\n\r\n* asset at risk;\r\n* attacker or failure mode;\r\n* current control;\r\n* new Phase 1 control;\r\n* remaining limitation;\r\n* test or operational verification.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 7. Explicit Non-Goals\r\n\r\nDo not implement the following during Phase 1 unless an exact dependency requires a narrow interface:\r\n\r\n* the full 2D/3D artifact inspection experience;\r\n* Three.js or React Three Fiber rendering;\r\n* final GLB presentation;\r\n* artifact assembly animation;\r\n* public Tale marketplace;\r\n* public content sharing;\r\n* multi-tenant billing;\r\n* DRM;\r\n* external KMS integration;\r\n* hardware security modules;\r\n* complete object-storage migration;\r\n* antivirus vendor integration;\r\n* production media transcoding;\r\n* facial recognition;\r\n* private-content history rewriting;\r\n* broad account-system redesign;\r\n* unrelated Lanternwake animation work;\r\n* broad language rewrites;\r\n* a replacement Tale publishing system;\r\n* automatic publishing after import;\r\n* automatic session creation after import;\r\n* automatic invitation delivery after import;\r\n* automatic deployment after import.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 may define interfaces and metadata needed by future 2D/3D assets, but it must not implement Project #3â€™s renderer or reveal system.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 8. Required Architecture\r\n\r\nCreate a cohesive private-content subsystem rather than distributing cryptographic and archive behavior across API routes.\r\n\r\nRecommended organization:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nsrc/private-content/\r\n  core/\r\n    types.ts\r\n    errors.ts\r\n    constants.ts\r\n    redaction.ts\r\n\r\n  package/\r\n    envelope.ts\r\n    manifest.ts\r\n    manifest.schema.ts\r\n    compatibility.ts\r\n    encrypt.ts\r\n    decrypt.ts\r\n    archive-reader.ts\r\n    archive-writer.ts\r\n    integrity.ts\r\n    limits.ts\r\n\r\n  storage/\r\n    PrivateAssetStore.ts\r\n    LocalPrivateAssetStore.ts\r\n    private-path-policy.ts\r\n    asset-access.ts\r\n\r\n  import/\r\n    import-types.ts\r\n    inspect-package.ts\r\n    build-import-plan.ts\r\n    conflict-policy.ts\r\n    stage-assets.ts\r\n    execute-import.ts\r\n    rollback-import.ts\r\n    import-audit.ts\r\n\r\n  export/\r\n    collect-export-content.ts\r\n    build-export-manifest.ts\r\n    stage-export.ts\r\n    write-package.ts\r\n    verify-round-trip.ts\r\n\r\n  backup/\r\n    backup-types.ts\r\n    create-private-backup.ts\r\n    verify-private-backup.ts\r\n    restore-private-backup.ts\r\n\r\n  security/\r\n    secret-policy.ts\r\n    repository-scan.ts\r\n    build-output-scan.ts\r\n    temp-file-policy.ts\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe exact structure may change if current repository conventions suggest a better shape.\r\n\r\nDo not place cryptographic primitives directly in React components or route handlers.\r\n\r\nAPI routes must call domain services.\r\n\r\nCLI scripts must call the same domain services.\r\n\r\nStudio must call typed API routes backed by the same validation and authorization.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 9. Core Types\r\n\r\nDefine typed interfaces or discriminated unions for at least:\r\n\r\n```ts\r\ntype PrivatePackageFormatVersion = 1;\r\n\r\ntype PrivatePackageEnvelope = {\r\n  format: \"forever-treasure-private-package\";\r\n  envelopeVersion: 1;\r\n  payloadFormatVersion: number;\r\n  cipher: string;\r\n  keyDerivation: string;\r\n  salt: string;\r\n  nonce: string;\r\n  authenticationTag: string;\r\n  encryptedPayloadBytes: number;\r\n  encryptedPayloadSha256: string;\r\n  createdAt: string;\r\n};\r\n\r\ntype PrivatePackageManifest = {\r\n  packageId: string;\r\n  packageRevision: number;\r\n  formatVersion: number;\r\n  createdAt: string;\r\n  sourceApplicationVersion: string;\r\n  minimumApplicationVersion: string;\r\n  maximumApplicationVersion?: string;\r\n  classification: \"private\";\r\n  contentType: \"tale-draft\" | \"published-tale\" | \"tale-archive\";\r\n  tales: PrivatePackageTaleEntry[];\r\n  assets: PrivatePackageAssetEntry[];\r\n  dependencies: PrivatePackageDependency[];\r\n  totals: {\r\n    files: number;\r\n    assets: number;\r\n    plaintextBytes: number;\r\n  };\r\n};\r\n\r\ntype PrivatePackageAssetEntry = {\r\n  logicalId: string;\r\n  sha256: string;\r\n  relativePath: string;\r\n  mediaType: string;\r\n  byteLength: number;\r\n  originalFilename?: string;\r\n  representation:\r\n    | \"image\"\r\n    | \"audio\"\r\n    | \"video\"\r\n    | \"document\"\r\n    | \"model-3d\"\r\n    | \"binary\";\r\n  role?: string;\r\n  source?: string;\r\n  license?: string;\r\n  accessibilityDescription?: string;\r\n};\r\n\r\ntype PrivatePackageInspection =\r\n  | {\r\n      status: \"valid\";\r\n      envelope: SanitizedEnvelopeSummary;\r\n      manifest: SanitizedManifestSummary;\r\n      importPlan: PrivateImportPlan;\r\n    }\r\n  | {\r\n      status: \"invalid\";\r\n      code: PrivatePackageFailureCode;\r\n      safeMessage: string;\r\n    };\r\n\r\ntype PrivateImportStatus =\r\n  | \"CREATED\"\r\n  | \"VALIDATING\"\r\n  | \"PLANNED\"\r\n  | \"STAGING_ASSETS\"\r\n  | \"COMMITTING_DATABASE\"\r\n  | \"FINALIZING_ASSETS\"\r\n  | \"COMPLETED\"\r\n  | \"FAILED\"\r\n  | \"ROLLED_BACK\";\r\n\r\ntype PrivateImportConflict =\r\n  | \"PACKAGE_ALREADY_IMPORTED\"\r\n  | \"TALE_ID_CONFLICT\"\r\n  | \"TALE_SLUG_CONFLICT\"\r\n  | \"PUBLISHED_VERSION_CONFLICT\"\r\n  | \"ASSET_LOGICAL_ID_CONFLICT\"\r\n  | \"ASSET_HASH_REUSE\"\r\n  | \"UNSUPPORTED_SCHEMA_VERSION\"\r\n  | \"MISSING_DEPENDENCY\";\r\n\r\ntype PrivateImportReceipt = {\r\n  importId: string;\r\n  packageId: string;\r\n  packageRevision: number;\r\n  packageSha256: string;\r\n  status: PrivateImportStatus;\r\n  importedTaleIds: string[];\r\n  importedAssetIds: string[];\r\n  reusedAssetIds: string[];\r\n  idMappings: Record<string, string>;\r\n  warnings: SafePrivateImportWarning[];\r\n  startedAt: string;\r\n  completedAt?: string;\r\n};\r\n```\r\n\r\nExact names may differ, but do not replace typed outcomes with generic thrown strings.\r\n\r\nAll externally visible errors must have:\r\n\r\n* stable machine-readable code;\r\n* safe human-readable message;\r\n* server-side correlation ID;\r\n* no private content;\r\n* no decrypted filenames unless authorized and necessary.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 10. Private Chronicle Package Format\r\n\r\nCreate a versioned package extension such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\n.ftprivate\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe package must contain an authenticated encrypted payload.\r\n\r\nA recommended outer structure is:\r\n\r\n```text\r\npackage.ftprivate\r\n  envelope.json\r\n  payload.enc\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe decrypted payload may contain:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nmanifest.json\r\ncontent/\r\n  tale.json\r\n  drafts/\r\n  published-versions/\r\n  blocks/\r\n  artifacts/\r\n  locations/\r\nassets/\r\n  index.json\r\n  objects/\r\n    <sha256>.<extension>\r\nprovenance/\r\n  licenses.json\r\n  sources.json\r\nchecksums.json\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe exact payload structure must be documented and versioned.\r\n\r\n## 10.1 Envelope rules\r\n\r\nThe unencrypted envelope must reveal only operational information required to decrypt and validate the package.\r\n\r\nDo not include:\r\n\r\n* Tale title;\r\n* Player name;\r\n* recipient name;\r\n* story summary;\r\n* filenames revealing surprises;\r\n* artifact names;\r\n* private creator notes;\r\n* chapter titles;\r\n* riddle text.\r\n\r\nUse generic envelope metadata.\r\n\r\n## 10.2 Payload rules\r\n\r\nThe encrypted payload must contain:\r\n\r\n* complete manifest;\r\n* content data;\r\n* referenced private assets;\r\n* asset metadata;\r\n* checksums;\r\n* package compatibility metadata;\r\n* provenance and license information where available.\r\n\r\nUse relative paths only.\r\n\r\nNo absolute paths.\r\n\r\nNo parent traversal.\r\n\r\nNo symlinks.\r\n\r\nNo hard links.\r\n\r\nNo executable scripts.\r\n\r\nNo package content may be executed during import.\r\n\r\n## 10.3 Content addressing\r\n\r\nPrivate asset objects should be content-addressed by SHA-256 or equivalent strong digest.\r\n\r\nBenefits must include:\r\n\r\n* deduplication;\r\n* tamper detection;\r\n* deterministic import;\r\n* backup verification;\r\n* safe object-storage migration;\r\n* stable identity independent of original filename.\r\n\r\nOriginal filenames may be stored as metadata, but must not become trusted storage paths.\r\n\r\n## 10.4 Package digest\r\n\r\nAfter package creation, report:\r\n\r\n* package SHA-256;\r\n* encrypted payload SHA-256;\r\n* package ID;\r\n* package revision;\r\n* format version;\r\n* asset count;\r\n* total bytes.\r\n\r\nDo not report private titles or filenames in ordinary logs.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 11. Encryption Requirements\r\n\r\nDo not invent a custom cipher.\r\n\r\nUse maintained, reviewed primitives.\r\n\r\nA suitable baseline for local Phase 1 packages is:\r\n\r\n* AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption;\r\n* unique random nonce per package;\r\n* unique random salt per package;\r\n* scrypt or Argon2id key derivation;\r\n* cryptographically secure random generation;\r\n* authenticated encryption tag verification before payload use.\r\n\r\nIf adding a dependency, justify it and prefer a maintained package.\r\n\r\nIf using Nodeâ€™s built-in crypto:\r\n\r\n* use an approved AEAD construction;\r\n* use a 256-bit derived key;\r\n* store KDF parameters in the envelope;\r\n* use safe minimum KDF costs;\r\n* use a 96-bit GCM nonce;\r\n* reject reused or malformed nonce values;\r\n* use a full authentication tag;\r\n* verify authentication before parsing plaintext;\r\n* never continue after authentication failure.\r\n\r\n## 11.1 Key handling\r\n\r\nNever:\r\n\r\n* store the passphrase in the database;\r\n* store the passphrase in the package;\r\n* log the passphrase;\r\n* include the passphrase in process arguments when avoidable;\r\n* place the passphrase in a browser URL;\r\n* return the passphrase from an API;\r\n* write it into audit events;\r\n* commit it to `.env`;\r\n* add a real…24 tokens truncated…e playthrough remains pinned;\r\n* later imported revision does not mutate active playthrough;\r\n* unpublished answers are absent from Player APIs;\r\n* Creator notes are absent from Player APIs;\r\n* private package metadata is absent from Player APIs;\r\n* asset IDs appear only when permitted.\r\n\r\n## 29.9 Secret-scan tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* `.ftprivate` tracked file detected;\r\n* key file detected;\r\n* decrypted directory detected;\r\n* synthetic private sentinel in public source detected;\r\n* sentinel in authorized encrypted fixture allowed;\r\n* matched secret value is redacted;\r\n* build output scan finds deliberate leak fixture;\r\n* clean build passes;\r\n* source map leak detected;\r\n* screenshot/trace leak detected where test framework permits inspection.\r\n\r\n## 29.10 Studio tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* unauthorized role cannot open private-content administration;\r\n* authorized Creator can inspect;\r\n* wrong passphrase receives safe error;\r\n* dry-run preview shows conflicts;\r\n* import requires explicit confirmation;\r\n* import wizard does not auto-publish;\r\n* keyboard-only completion;\r\n* focus restoration;\r\n* reduced-motion state;\r\n* screen-reader status;\r\n* abort cancels and cleans staging;\r\n* refresh preserves safe operation state where supported;\r\n* passphrase is not persisted in browser storage.\r\n\r\n## 29.11 Backup tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* encrypted backup creation;\r\n* backup verification;\r\n* tampered backup failure;\r\n* isolated restore;\r\n* restored checksums;\r\n* restored authorization;\r\n* missing asset detection;\r\n* database/asset mismatch detection;\r\n* temporary restore cleanup;\r\n* sanitized recovery report.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 30. Browser and Database Isolation\r\n\r\nNo mutating browser test may use the canonical development or production database.\r\n\r\nBefore mutation:\r\n\r\n1. Create a uniquely named isolated database.\r\n2. Create a uniquely named private asset root.\r\n3. Create a uniquely named staging root.\r\n4. Use an alternate owned server port.\r\n5. Record:\r\n\r\n   * process ID;\r\n   * port;\r\n   * database path or redacted database identity;\r\n   * private asset root;\r\n   * staging root;\r\n   * test nonce.\r\n6. Verify the running application resolves the isolated resources.\r\n7. Hash or record the canonical database.\r\n8. Hash or record the canonical private asset root where present.\r\n9. Run mutating tests.\r\n10. Verify canonical resources remain unchanged.\r\n11. Delete isolated plaintext staging.\r\n\r\nDo not assume a different port implies database isolation.\r\n\r\nUse synthetic content only.\r\n\r\nOnly one validation owner may control:\r\n\r\n* server process;\r\n* test database;\r\n* test asset root;\r\n* test staging root;\r\n* Playwright mutation suite;\r\n* final validation.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 31. Performance and Resource Limits\r\n\r\nPhase 1 is not a performance-optimization project, but operations must be bounded.\r\n\r\nDefine and test budgets such as:\r\n\r\n* package-envelope validation under 100 ms for normal packages;\r\n* manifest validation under 500 ms for normal fixtures;\r\n* no unbounded memory growth during large-file streaming;\r\n* no whole-package buffering above a documented threshold;\r\n* cancellation cleanup under 1 second where OS file handling permits;\r\n* no orphaned staging directory after normal failure;\r\n* no duplicate private objects;\r\n* bounded archive entry count;\r\n* bounded decompression ratio;\r\n* bounded JSON depth;\r\n* bounded import retry count.\r\n\r\nDo not weaken integrity checks to meet timing.\r\n\r\nFor expensive media inspection, return a durable status or documented limitation rather than pretending synchronous validation is free.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 32. Concurrency Plan\r\n\r\nAfter the design record and central interfaces are frozen, work may proceed in controlled lanes.\r\n\r\n## Lane A: Package and cryptography\r\n\r\nOwns:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nsrc/private-content/package/\r\npackage-format unit tests\r\nencryption tests\r\narchive-safety tests\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Lane B: Storage and authorized delivery\r\n\r\nOwns:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nsrc/private-content/storage/\r\nprivate asset routes\r\nasset authorization tests\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Lane C: Import/export domain\r\n\r\nOwns:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nsrc/private-content/import/\r\nsrc/private-content/export/\r\nCLI scripts\r\nimport/export tests\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Lane D: Studio interface\r\n\r\nOwns:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nprivate-content Studio screens\r\nwizard tests\r\nlanguage integration\r\naccessibility tests\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Lane E: Security and operations\r\n\r\nOwns:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nrepository scan\r\nbuild-output scan\r\nbackup tooling\r\noperations documentation\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Serialized ownership\r\n\r\nOne architecture owner controls:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nsrc/private-content/core/\r\nshared package types\r\nmanifest schema\r\ncompatibility registry\r\nredaction policy\r\n```\r\n\r\nOne database owner controls:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nprisma/schema.prisma\r\nmigrations\r\ndatabase generation\r\ndatabase-isolated tests\r\n```\r\n\r\nOne authorization owner controls shared:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nauthentication helpers\r\nCreator authorization\r\nPlayer asset authorization\r\nvisibility projection\r\n```\r\n\r\nOne validation owner controls:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ntest server\r\nports\r\ndatabase\r\nasset roots\r\nPlaywright\r\nfull validation\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not allow multiple lanes to edit the same high-conflict file concurrently.\r\n\r\nSubagents must return:\r\n\r\n* findings;\r\n* proposed patch;\r\n* test evidence;\r\n* files touched;\r\n* unresolved risks.\r\n\r\nThey must not independently commit shared architecture.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 33. Required Implementation Sequence\r\n\r\nFollow this order.\r\n\r\n## Step 1: Repository and privacy audit\r\n\r\n* record current state;\r\n* identify concurrent work;\r\n* inspect current asset and Tale architecture;\r\n* scan for possible private material;\r\n* document findings without quoting content.\r\n\r\n## Step 2: Design freeze\r\n\r\nCreate and complete:\r\n\r\n* design record;\r\n* threat model;\r\n* package format;\r\n* operations runbook draft;\r\n* test plan.\r\n\r\nFreeze:\r\n\r\n* envelope;\r\n* manifest;\r\n* error codes;\r\n* import states;\r\n* storage interface;\r\n* redaction policy.\r\n\r\n## Step 3: Core schemas and package codec\r\n\r\nImplement:\r\n\r\n* envelope schema;\r\n* manifest schema;\r\n* limits;\r\n* compatibility registry;\r\n* encryption;\r\n* decryption;\r\n* integrity verification;\r\n* archive safety.\r\n\r\nRun focused tests before continuing.\r\n\r\n## Step 4: Local private asset store\r\n\r\nImplement:\r\n\r\n* secure root resolution;\r\n* staging;\r\n* content-addressed objects;\r\n* atomic finalization;\r\n* object verification;\r\n* cleanup;\r\n* path policy.\r\n\r\n## Step 5: Import dry-run\r\n\r\nImplement:\r\n\r\n* package inspection;\r\n* content validation;\r\n* dependency closure;\r\n* conflict detection;\r\n* import plan;\r\n* sanitized preview.\r\n\r\nProve it creates no Tale, asset or publication state.\r\n\r\n## Step 6: Durable import execution\r\n\r\nAdd only necessary database records and migrations.\r\n\r\nImplement:\r\n\r\n* import state;\r\n* idempotency;\r\n* staging;\r\n* transaction;\r\n* finalization;\r\n* rollback;\r\n* retry;\r\n* audit.\r\n\r\n## Step 7: Export and round-trip verification\r\n\r\nImplement:\r\n\r\n* content collection;\r\n* referential closure;\r\n* package generation;\r\n* encryption;\r\n* round-trip verification;\r\n* sanitized receipt;\r\n* safe output path policy.\r\n\r\n## Step 8: Authorized asset delivery\r\n\r\nImplement:\r\n\r\n* server-controlled asset access;\r\n* role and membership checks;\r\n* reveal checks;\r\n* safe headers;\r\n* range behavior if required;\r\n* no static public mapping.\r\n\r\n## Step 9: Studio integration\r\n\r\nImplement:\r\n\r\n* private-content area;\r\n* import wizard;\r\n* conflict review;\r\n* explicit confirmation;\r\n* import history;\r\n* export flow if safely supported;\r\n* accessibility;\r\n* universal language.\r\n\r\n## Step 10: Repository and build guards\r\n\r\nImplement:\r\n\r\n* ignore patterns;\r\n* repository scanner;\r\n* staged-diff scanner;\r\n* secret scanner integration;\r\n* build-output sentinel test;\r\n* CI workflow or local gate.\r\n\r\n## Step 11: Backup and recovery verification\r\n\r\nImplement:\r\n\r\n* encrypted backup;\r\n* verify;\r\n* isolated restore test;\r\n* operations documentation.\r\n\r\n## Step 12: Integrated validation\r\n\r\nRun:\r\n\r\n* format;\r\n* lint;\r\n* typecheck;\r\n* unit tests;\r\n* database tests;\r\n* authorization tests;\r\n* focused E2E;\r\n* build;\r\n* build-output scan;\r\n* repository scan;\r\n* full validation where supported.\r\n\r\n## Step 13: Documentation and synchronization\r\n\r\nUpdate:\r\n\r\n* SECURITY.md;\r\n* `.env.example`;\r\n* README setup;\r\n* deployment instructions;\r\n* package format;\r\n* threat model;\r\n* operations runbook;\r\n* test plan;\r\n* implementation report;\r\n* project status metadata if already present;\r\n* required chat/archive synchronization.\r\n\r\n## Step 14: Commit and push\r\n\r\nAfter all gates:\r\n\r\n* stage only Phase 1 files;\r\n* create an implementation manifest;\r\n* hash the manifest;\r\n* commit to the dedicated branch;\r\n* push the branch;\r\n* do not merge automatically;\r\n* stop for review.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 34. Environment Variables\r\n\r\nAdd safe documented variables as required, using names consistent with the repository.\r\n\r\nPotential examples:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPRIVATE_CONTENT_ROOT=\r\nPRIVATE_CONTENT_STAGING_ROOT=\r\nPRIVATE_CONTENT_MAX_PACKAGE_BYTES=\r\nPRIVATE_CONTENT_MAX_PLAINTEXT_BYTES=\r\nPRIVATE_CONTENT_MAX_ASSET_BYTES=\r\nPRIVATE_CONTENT_MAX_FILE_COUNT=\r\nPRIVATE_CONTENT_ALLOWED_MODEL_TYPES=\r\nPRIVATE_CONTENT_IMPORT_ENABLED=\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not add:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nNEXT_PUBLIC_PRIVATE_CONTENT_KEY\r\nNEXT_PUBLIC_PRIVATE_CONTENT_ROOT\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not put a real key in `.env.example`.\r\n\r\nUse placeholders and explain secure configuration.\r\n\r\nFail startup or disable private operations safely when required roots are invalid.\r\n\r\nDo not silently fall back to a public directory.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 35. Exact Acceptance Criteria\r\n\r\nPhase 1 is complete only when every requirement below is satisfied.\r\n\r\n## 35.1 Repository boundary\r\n\r\n* The public repository contains no real Chronicle content.\r\n* Private exports are ignored and actively detected.\r\n* Private assets cannot be configured under `public/`.\r\n* Decrypted staging cannot be configured inside the repository.\r\n* Repository scan completes without exposing matched values.\r\n* Synthetic fixtures are clearly nonpersonal.\r\n\r\n## 35.2 Package security\r\n\r\n* Every package payload is authenticated and encrypted.\r\n* Wrong key fails closed.\r\n* Tampering fails closed.\r\n* Unsupported versions fail closed.\r\n* Checksums cover all payload files.\r\n* Package verification occurs before import.\r\n* Package export is round-trip verified.\r\n* Keys are never logged or persisted.\r\n* Temporary plaintext is cleaned.\r\n\r\n## 35.3 Archive safety\r\n\r\n* Path traversal is rejected.\r\n* Absolute paths are rejected.\r\n* links are rejected.\r\n* duplicate normalized paths are rejected.\r\n* decompression limits are enforced.\r\n* executable content is rejected.\r\n* GLB or other future model assets are constrained to approved formats.\r\n* untrusted filenames never become storage paths.\r\n\r\n## 35.4 Import safety\r\n\r\n* Dry-run creates no domain state.\r\n* Import requires explicit confirmation.\r\n* Import does not publish.\r\n* Import does not launch a session.\r\n* Import does not invite Players.\r\n* Existing published versions are never overwritten.\r\n* Re-import is idempotent.\r\n* Asset hash reuse is deterministic.\r\n* Conflict behavior is explicit.\r\n* Database failure cleans staging.\r\n* Finalization failure does not expose broken content.\r\n* Retry does not duplicate data.\r\n\r\n## 35.5 Asset protection\r\n\r\n* Private assets are outside public static storage.\r\n* Anonymous access fails.\r\n* unauthorized Player access fails.\r\n* unrevealed artifact access fails.\r\n* Creator draft access is authorized.\r\n* Player-visible published assets work only when allowed.\r\n* thumbnails inherit source protection.\r\n* storage paths are not exposed.\r\n* revoked access stops working.\r\n* guessed IDs do not disclose existence.\r\n\r\n## 35.6 Publishing isolation\r\n\r\n* Imported drafts remain private.\r\n* Published versions remain immutable.\r\n* active playthroughs remain version-pinned.\r\n* later imports do not mutate live sessions.\r\n* Player APIs exclude unpublished content.\r\n* Player APIs exclude answers and Creator notes.\r\n* package metadata is not projected to Players.\r\n\r\n## 35.7 Operations\r\n\r\n* Secure backup can be created.\r\n* Backup can be verified.\r\n* Isolated restore succeeds.\r\n* Tampered backup fails.\r\n* database and asset mismatch is detected.\r\n* recovery procedure is documented.\r\n* package and backup hashes are reported.\r\n\r\n## 35.8 UI and accessibility\r\n\r\n* Only authorized Creator/Admin users see private-content administration.\r\n* Import requires explicit confirmation.\r\n* Wrong-key errors are safe.\r\n* keyboard-only operation works.\r\n* focus is restored.\r\n* progress is announced.\r\n* reduced motion works.\r\n* secret values are not persisted in browser storage.\r\n* secret values are not announced.\r\n\r\n## 35.9 Build and CI\r\n\r\n* Production build succeeds.\r\n* secret scan succeeds.\r\n* private-path scan succeeds.\r\n* synthetic sentinel is absent from public bundles.\r\n* synthetic sentinel is absent from unauthorized responses.\r\n* no private fixture enters public static output.\r\n* full validation passes or every blocker is documented accurately.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 36. Required Validation Commands\r\n\r\nUse repository-supported commands.\r\n\r\nAt minimum run the current equivalents of:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nnpm run format:check\r\nnpm run lint\r\nnpm run typecheck\r\nnpm test\r\nnpm run build\r\nnpm run private-content:scan\r\nnpm run private-content:verify-fixtures\r\nnpm run private-content:build-scan\r\n```\r\n\r\nRun focused tests while developing.\r\n\r\nRun database-mutating and browser tests only after isolation proof.\r\n\r\nOnly one validation owner may run the final full gate.\r\n\r\nIf the repository already uses:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nnpm run validate\r\n```\r\n\r\nrun it once after focused work is green.\r\n\r\nDo not rerun massive suites repeatedly without diagnosing failure.\r\n\r\nClassify every result as:\r\n\r\n* passed;\r\n* task regression;\r\n* known baseline failure;\r\n* environment failure;\r\n* skipped;\r\n* blocked.\r\n\r\nA skipped test is not a pass.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 37. Implementation Manifest\r\n\r\nCreate:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Sealed_Hold_Phase_1_Implementation_Manifest.txt\r\n```\r\n\r\nInclude:\r\n\r\n* repository;\r\n* branch;\r\n* starting commit;\r\n* ending commit before final report update;\r\n* package-format version;\r\n* migration identifiers;\r\n* files added;\r\n* files modified;\r\n* files deleted;\r\n* tests added;\r\n* documentation added;\r\n* validation commands;\r\n* known limitations.\r\n\r\nGenerate and report the SHA-256 of the completed manifest.\r\n\r\nDo not include:\r\n\r\n* passphrases;\r\n* private asset paths;\r\n* private prose;\r\n* secrets;\r\n* decrypted filenames.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 38. Mandatory Final Report\r\n\r\nCreate:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Sealed_Hold_Phase_1_Completion_Report.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe final Codex response must also summarize the following.\r\n\r\n## Repository state\r\n\r\n* repository;\r\n* branch;\r\n* starting commit;\r\n* ending commit;\r\n* upstream;\r\n* ahead/behind;\r\n* worktree;\r\n* concurrent work preserved;\r\n* exact files changed.\r\n\r\n## Architecture\r\n\r\n* final package extension;\r\n* envelope version;\r\n* payload version;\r\n* cipher;\r\n* KDF;\r\n* package manifest type;\r\n* storage interface;\r\n* import status model;\r\n* import receipt;\r\n* export receipt;\r\n* compatibility registry;\r\n* redaction policy;\r\n* asset-delivery design.\r\n\r\n## Implementation totals\r\n\r\n* routes added;\r\n* services added;\r\n* package schemas;\r\n* Prisma models added or reused;\r\n* migrations;\r\n* CLI commands;\r\n* Studio screens;\r\n* tests;\r\n* synthetic fixtures;\r\n* documentation files.\r\n\r\n## Security proof\r\n\r\nProvide safe evidence for:\r\n\r\n* wrong-key failure;\r\n* tamper failure;\r\n* traversal rejection;\r\n* decompression-limit rejection;\r\n* repository export refusal;\r\n* dry-run no-mutation;\r\n* import idempotency;\r\n* rollback cleanup;\r\n* no automatic publishing;\r\n* no automatic invitation;\r\n* unauthorized asset denial;\r\n* unrevealed artifact denial;\r\n* synthetic sentinel absent from public build;\r\n* passphrase absent from logs;\r\n* temporary plaintext cleanup.\r\n\r\n## Backup proof\r\n\r\nProvide:\r\n\r\n* backup package SHA-256;\r\n* verification result;\r\n* isolated restore result;\r\n* restored asset counts;\r\n* mismatch-detection result;\r\n* cleanup result.\r\n\r\nUse only synthetic test data.\r\n\r\n## Validation\r\n\r\nFor every command report:\r\n\r\n* exact command;\r\n* exit status;\r\n* passed count;\r\n* failed count;\r\n* skipped count;\r\n* duration where available;\r\n* database identity;\r\n* test asset-root identity;\r\n* environment blockers;\r\n* baseline failures;\r\n* task regressions.\r\n\r\n## Remaining limitations\r\n\r\nExplicitly list what Phase 1 does not solve, including:\r\n\r\n* full 3D artifact renderer;\r\n* cinematic artifact reveal system;\r\n* artifact assembly;\r\n* external KMS;\r\n* distributed object storage if not implemented;\r\n* antivirus service;\r\n* media transcoding;\r\n* complete production backup automation;\r\n* public marketplace;\r\n* cross-installation Tale sharing;\r\n* history remediation for any preexisting leak;\r\n* full multi-tenant isolation.\r\n\r\n## Synchronization\r\n\r\nRun repository-required:\r\n\r\n* Codex chat archive synchronization;\r\n* Development_Docs synchronization;\r\n* implementation-manifest verification;\r\n* project-status reconciliation if that system already exists.\r\n\r\nReport exact synchronized files.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 39. Commit and Push Requirements\r\n\r\nAfter successful validation:\r\n\r\n1. Review `git diff`.\r\n2. Confirm no private content is present.\r\n3. Run the private-content scanner against the exact staged diff.\r\n4. Stage only intended files.\r\n5. Create the implementation manifest.\r\n6. Hash the manifest.\r\n7. Commit with a clear message such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nfeat(private-content): establish sealed Chronicle package architecture\r\n```\r\n\r\n8. Push the dedicated branch.\r\n9. Report branch and commit hash.\r\n10. Do not merge automatically.\r\n11. Do not delete the worktree.\r\n12. Stop for review.\r\n\r\nIf validation has unresolved task regressions:\r\n\r\n* do not claim completion;\r\n* do not merge;\r\n* clearly report partial implementation;\r\n* preserve all work;\r\n* push only if repository policy permits a reviewable incomplete branch and label it accurately.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 40. Phase Completion Gate\r\n\r\nDo not declare **Lock the Chronicle** complete unless:\r\n\r\n1. Private Chronicle content can live entirely outside Git.\r\n2. Encrypted packages are authenticated and versioned.\r\n3. Wrong keys and tampering fail closed.\r\n4. Package archives are protected against traversal and resource abuse.\r\n5. Private assets are stored outside public static paths.\r\n6. Private assets require authorization.\r\n7. Dry-run import produces no Tale or asset mutation.\r\n8. Import requires explicit confirmation.\r\n9. Import does not publish, launch or invite.\r\n10. Re-import is idempotent.\r\n11. Partial failure cannot expose or duplicate content.\r\n12. Export performs complete referential closure.\r\n13. Export is encrypted and round-trip verified.\r\n14. Keys and plaintext are absent from logs.\r\n15. Temporary plaintext is cleaned.\r\n16. Repository and build scans prevent accidental leaks.\r\n17. Synthetic private sentinel is absent from public output.\r\n18. Backup verification and isolated restore are demonstrated.\r\n19. Studio operations are Creator-authorized and accessible.\r\n20. Current generic content and existing Player behavior remain intact.\r\n21. Future GLB assets can be carried safely without falsely claiming a completed 3D system.\r\n22. Documentation matches implementation.\r\n23. The implementation manifest is hashed.\r\n24. The dedicated branch is committed and pushed.\r\n25. Concurrent work is preserved.\r\n26. No real private content was introduced during implementation.\r\n\r\nDo not weaken encryption to simplify testing.\r\n\r\nDo not rely solely on `.gitignore`.\r\n\r\nDo not store private assets under `public/`.\r\n\r\nDo not place a passphrase in a command-line example.\r\n\r\nDo not treat an encrypted package as valid until authentication, schema validation and checksum validation all succeed.\r\n\r\nDo not treat successful database insertion as a completed import until private assets are finalized and verified.\r\n\r\nDo not allow an imported Tale to become visible merely because its records exist.\r\n\r\nDo not begin the full 2D/3D artifact implementation automatically.\r\n\r\nComplete Phase 1, validate it, document it, commit it to the dedicated branch, push it and stop for review.\r\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]
