Encryption Requirements\r\n\r\nDo not invent a custom cipher.\r\n\r\nUse maintained, reviewed primitives.\r\n\r\nA suitable baseline for local Phase 1 packages is:\r\n\r\n* AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption;\r\n* unique random nonce per package;\r\n* unique random salt per package;\r\n* scrypt or Argon2id key derivation;\r\n* cryptographically secure random generation;\r\n* authenticated encryption tag verification before payload use.\r\n\r\nIf adding a dependency, justify it and prefer a maintained package.\r\n\r\nIf using Nodeâ€™s built-in crypto:\r\n\r\n* use an approved AEAD construction;\r\n* use a 256-bit derived key;\r\n* store KDF parameters in the envelope;\r\n* use safe minimum KDF costs;\r\n* use a 96-bit GCM nonce;\r\n* reject reused or malformed nonce values;\r\n* use a full authentication tag;\r\n* verify authentication before parsing plaintext;\r\n* never continue after authentication failure.\r\n\r\n## 11.1 Key handling\r\n\r\nNever:\r\n\r\n* store the passphrase in the database;\r\n* store the passphrase in the package;\r\n* log the passphrase;\r\n* include the passphrase in process arguments when avoidable;\r\n* place the passphrase in a browser URL;\r\n* return the passphrase from an API;\r\n* write it into audit events;\r\n* commit it to `.env`;\r\n* add a real key to `.env.example`.\r\n\r\nCLI tools should prefer:\r\n\r\n* interactive hidden input;\r\n* standard input;\r\n* environment-provided secret only when explicitly selected;\r\n* file descriptor or secret-file input with strict permissions.\r\n\r\nIf environment input exists, use a private variable name without a `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix.\r\n\r\nBrowser-based export may use a one-time passphrase supplied by an authorized Creator, but the server must not persist it.\r\n\r\n## 11.2 Temporary plaintext\r\n\r\nDecrypted payloads must use:\r\n\r\n* an OS temporary directory or configured secure staging root;\r\n* randomized directory names;\r\n* restrictive permissions;\r\n* no repository-relative paths;\r\n* cleanup on success;\r\n* cleanup on expected failure;\r\n* cleanup on abort;\r\n* best-effort cleanup on process termination.\r\n\r\nDo not assume file deletion is cryptographic erasure.\r\n\r\nMinimize plaintext lifetime.\r\n\r\nFor small data, prefer in-memory parsing when practical.\r\n\r\nFor large assets, use bounded streaming or protected staging.\r\n\r\n## 11.3 Wrong keys and tampering\r\n\r\nWrong passphrase, corrupted package and tampered authentication data must produce the same general safe user message:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nThe private package could not be authenticated or opened.\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not reveal whether:\r\n\r\n* the passphrase was almost correct;\r\n* the package header was recognized;\r\n* a specific private file existed.\r\n\r\nDetailed failure classification may be written to protected server diagnostics using codes only.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 12. Package Limits and Archive Safety\r\n\r\nDefine centralized limits.\r\n\r\nAt minimum include:\r\n\r\n* maximum package bytes;\r\n* maximum encrypted payload bytes;\r\n* maximum decrypted payload bytes;\r\n* maximum file count;\r\n* maximum asset count;\r\n* maximum individual asset bytes;\r\n* maximum compression ratio;\r\n* maximum path length;\r\n* allowed MIME types;\r\n* allowed model formats;\r\n* allowed image formats;\r\n* allowed audio formats;\r\n* allowed video formats;\r\n* allowed document formats;\r\n* maximum nested-object depth in JSON;\r\n* maximum manifest length.\r\n\r\nUse conservative defaults with environment overrides.\r\n\r\nReject:\r\n\r\n* path traversal;\r\n* absolute paths;\r\n* Windows drive paths;\r\n* network paths;\r\n* symlinks;\r\n* hard links;\r\n* duplicate normalized paths;\r\n* case-insensitive collisions;\r\n* null bytes;\r\n* reserved filenames;\r\n* executable file types;\r\n* shell scripts;\r\n* server-side source files;\r\n* nested encrypted packages;\r\n* archives inside the payload unless explicitly permitted later.\r\n\r\nMIME type must not be trusted solely from:\r\n\r\n* filename;\r\n* client-provided content type;\r\n* manifest declaration.\r\n\r\nUse magic-byte inspection where feasible.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 may permit future 3D assets only in an explicitly approved format, preferably:\r\n\r\n```text\r\n.glb\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not permit arbitrary executable 3D formats.\r\n\r\nCarrying a GLB safely does not mean rendering it during this phase.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 13. Package Compatibility and Schema Evolution\r\n\r\nEvery package must declare:\r\n\r\n* envelope version;\r\n* payload format version;\r\n* source application version;\r\n* minimum compatible application version;\r\n* optional maximum compatible application version;\r\n* schema versions for imported content;\r\n* block-schema versions where applicable;\r\n* artifact-schema version;\r\n* asset-index version.\r\n\r\nImport must fail closed when:\r\n\r\n* the envelope version is unsupported;\r\n* the payload format is unsupported;\r\n* the application is below the minimum version;\r\n* a required block schema is unreadable;\r\n* a required artifact schema is unreadable;\r\n* a package claims an impossible version relationship.\r\n\r\nDo not silently reinterpret unknown fields.\r\n\r\nUnknown optional fields may be preserved only if the format explicitly permits forward-compatible extension fields.\r\n\r\nCreate a compatibility registry rather than scattering version checks across routes.\r\n\r\nPrepare for future transformations such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPrivate Package Payload v1 -> v2\r\nArtifact metadata v1 -> v2\r\nStory block snapshot v1 -> v2\r\nLocation metadata v1 -> v2\r\n```\r\n\r\nPhase 1 must implement the registry and v1 identity path.\r\n\r\nIt does not need hypothetical future migrations.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 14. Private Asset Storage\r\n\r\nCreate or strengthen an abstraction such as:\r\n\r\n```ts\r\ninterface PrivateAssetStore {\r\n  stageObject(input: StagePrivateObjectInput): Promise<StagedPrivateObject>;\r\n  finalizeObject(input: FinalizePrivateObjectInput): Promise<StoredPrivateObject>;\r\n  objectExists(sha256: string): Promise<boolean>;\r\n  readAuthorizedObject(input: ReadPrivateObjectInput): Promise<PrivateObjectStream>;\r\n  deleteStagedObject(stagingId: string): Promise<void>;\r\n  deleteUnreferencedObject(sha256: string): Promise<void>;\r\n  verifyObject(sha256: string): Promise<PrivateObjectVerification>;\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nImplement a safe local filesystem adapter for current development and single-server use.\r\n\r\nDesign it so a future S3-compatible adapter can be added without changing import semantics.\r\n\r\n## 14.1 Root location\r\n\r\nPrivate assets must live outside:\r\n\r\n* the Git repository;\r\n* `public/`;\r\n* `.next/`;\r\n* source directories;\r\n* static export directories;\r\n* default test-artifact folders.\r\n\r\nUse a configured absolute root such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPRIVATE_CONTENT_ROOT=/absolute/path/to/private-content\r\n```\r\n\r\nFor Linux deployment, document a recommended location such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\n/opt/forever-treasure/private-content\r\n```\r\n\r\nor:\r\n\r\n```text\r\n/var/lib/forever-treasure/private-content\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not hardcode a production path.\r\n\r\n## 14.2 Permissions\r\n\r\nDocument and enforce where practical:\r\n\r\n* service-account ownership;\r\n* directory mode;\r\n* file mode;\r\n* no world-readable access;\r\n* no web-server direct static mapping;\r\n* no directory listing.\r\n\r\n## 14.3 Storage paths\r\n\r\nStorage paths must derive from trusted hashes or generated IDs.\r\n\r\nNever directly join an untrusted filename into the private asset root.\r\n\r\nExample:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nobjects/ab/cd/<full-sha256>.glb\r\n```\r\n\r\nUse atomic moves when finalizing.\r\n\r\nDo not overwrite an existing object unless its checksum is identical.\r\n\r\n## 14.4 Staging\r\n\r\nAssets must first enter a staging namespace.\r\n\r\nStaged assets are not serveable.\r\n\r\nThey become serveable only after:\r\n\r\n* package authentication;\r\n* full validation;\r\n* import-plan approval;\r\n* database commit;\r\n* finalization;\r\n* authorization metadata exists.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 15. Private Asset Delivery\r\n\r\nPrivate assets must not be served from a public static path.\r\n\r\nImplement authenticated delivery through:\r\n\r\n* an authorized application route; or\r\n* short-lived signed URLs generated only after authorization.\r\n\r\nCurrent local Phase 1 may use an application-controlled streaming route if that matches the existing architecture.\r\n\r\nEvery request must validate:\r\n\r\n1. authenticated identity where required;\r\n2. current session validity;\r\n3. Tale or playthrough membership;\r\n4. content visibility;\r\n5. asset relationship to authorized content;\r\n6. published/draft status;\r\n7. revoked access;\r\n8. expiration where signed URLs are used.\r\n\r\n## 15.1 Authorization classes\r\n\r\nAt minimum distinguish:\r\n\r\n* Creator draft asset;\r\n* Captain preview asset;\r\n* published Player-visible asset;\r\n* Player-specific asset;\r\n* crew-visible asset;\r\n* locked artifact asset;\r\n* reveal-poster asset;\r\n* public generic asset.\r\n\r\nA Player must not gain access merely because the Player knows an asset ID.\r\n\r\n## 15.2 Derived assets\r\n\r\nThe following inherit the protection level of their source:\r\n\r\n* thumbnails;\r\n* posters;\r\n* waveform images;\r\n* transcoded video;\r\n* compressed audio;\r\n* future model previews;\r\n* future 3D thumbnails.\r\n\r\nA protected original with a public thumbnail is still a leak.\r\n\r\n## 15.3 Response behavior\r\n\r\nUse safe headers.\r\n\r\nConsider:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCache-Control: private, no-store\r\nX-Content-Type-Options: nosniff\r\nContent-Security-Policy appropriate to the media route\r\nContent-Disposition with sanitized filenames\r\n```\r\n\r\nSupport byte ranges when required for authorized audio or video.\r\n\r\nDo not reveal storage paths.\r\n\r\nDo not reveal whether an unauthorized asset exists.\r\n\r\nUnauthorized and nonexistent private asset responses should avoid useful enumeration differences.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 16. Import Workflow\r\n\r\nImport must have two modes:\r\n\r\n1. Dry-run inspection\r\n2. Explicit commit\r\n\r\nNo upload should automatically create or publish a Tale.\r\n\r\n## 16.1 Dry-run sequence\r\n\r\nThe dry-run must:\r\n\r\n1. Receive the encrypted package.\r\n2. Enforce encrypted package-size limit.\r\n3. Store it in protected temporary staging.\r\n4. Validate envelope shape.\r\n5. Derive the key.\r\n6. Authenticate and decrypt.\r\n7. Enforce plaintext-size limits.\r\n8. Validate archive paths.\r\n9. Validate entry counts.\r\n10. Validate manifest schema.\r\n11. Validate package compatibility.\r\n12. Validate checksums.\r\n13. Validate asset metadata.\r\n14. Validate media formats.\r\n15. Validate Tale content.\r\n16. Validate story blocks.\r\n17. Validate artifact references.\r\n18. Validate location references.\r\n19. Validate dependency closure.\r\n20. Detect ID and slug conflicts.\r\n21. Detect content-addressed asset reuse.\r\n22. Build an import plan.\r\n23. Return a sanitized preview.\r\n\r\nThe preview may show private content only to the authorized Creator who supplied the package.\r\n\r\nNormal server logs must remain redacted.\r\n\r\n## 16.2 Import plan\r\n\r\nThe import plan must include:\r\n\r\n* package identity;\r\n* package revision;\r\n* target Tale action;\r\n* draft action;\r\n* published-version action;\r\n* ID mappings;\r\n* slug conflicts;\r\n* asset objects to reuse;\r\n* asset objects to create;\r\n* missing dependencies;\r\n* warnings;\r\n* blocking errors;\r\n* estimated storage;\r\n* whether a database migration is needed;\r\n* whether content would replace anything.\r\n\r\nDefault policy:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nAbort on destructive conflict.\r\n```\r\n\r\nNever silently overwrite:\r\n\r\n* an existing published version;\r\n* an existing Tale owned by another Creator;\r\n* an existing asset with a different checksum;\r\n* a newer imported package revision;\r\n* a current live playthroughâ€™s pinned snapshot.\r\n\r\n## 16.3 Conflict resolution\r\n\r\nSupported Phase 1 conflict behavior should be explicit.\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n### Same package and same revision\r\n\r\nReturn:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPACKAGE_ALREADY_IMPORTED\r\n```\r\n\r\nTreat as idempotent.\r\n\r\nDo not duplicate the Tale or assets.\r\n\r\n### Same package ID with newer revision\r\n\r\nPermit import only after dry-run.\r\n\r\nImport as a new draft revision or new package record.\r\n\r\nDo not modify an immutable published version.\r\n\r\n### Tale slug conflict\r\n\r\nRequire an explicit new slug or import namespace.\r\n\r\nDo not silently add random suffixes without showing them in the plan.\r\n\r\n### Asset hash reuse\r\n\r\nReuse the existing private object if:\r\n\r\n* checksum matches;\r\n* byte length matches;\r\n* media validation matches;\r\n* authorization metadata can safely reference it.\r\n\r\n### Asset logical-ID conflict with different checksum\r\n\r\nAbort by default.\r\n\r\nRequire explicit remapping.\r\n\r\n### Published-version conflict\r\n\r\nNever overwrite an immutable published version.\r\n\r\nImport as:\r\n\r\n* a new draft;\r\n* a new Tale;\r\n* or a separately versioned candidate.\r\n\r\n## 16.4 Import execution\r\n\r\nImport execution must be idempotent and crash-aware.\r\n\r\nA recommended sequence is:\r\n\r\n1. Create import record with status `CREATED`.\r\n2. Acquire package/import lock.\r\n3. Confirm dry-run plan still matches current state.\r\n4. Set status `VALIDATING`.\r\n5. Revalidate package and hashes.\r\n6. Set status `STAGING_ASSETS`.\r\n7. Stage new private objects.\r\n8. Verify staged-object hashes.\r\n9. Set status `COMMITTING_DATABASE`.\r\n10. Open database transaction.\r\n11. Create or update allowed Tale records.\r\n12. Create draft records.\r\n13. Create version data only where permitted.\r\n14. Create asset metadata.\r\n15. Create ID mappings.\r\n16. Create audit event.\r\n17. Commit database transaction.\r\n18. Set status `FINALIZING_ASSETS`.\r\n19. Atomically finalize staged objects.\r\n20. Verify final objects.\r\n21. Mark imported records available.\r\n22. Set status `COMPLETED`.\r\n23. Remove temporary plaintext and encrypted staging.\r\n\r\nIf object finalization fails after database commit:\r\n\r\n* do not mark import completed;\r\n* mark content unavailable;\r\n* retain enough safe state to retry finalization;\r\n* do not expose broken asset references;\r\n* do not duplicate the database import on retry.\r\n\r\nIf the database transaction fails:\r\n\r\n* roll back;\r\n* delete staged objects;\r\n* mark import failed;\r\n* clean plaintext.\r\n\r\n## 16.5 Concurrency\r\n\r\nPrevent:\r\n\r\n* two imports of the same package;\r\n* concurrent revision import races;\r\n* duplicate asset finalization;\r\n* duplicate Tale creation;\r\n* duplicate audit receipts.\r\n\r\nUse database uniqueness constraints or transactional locking where appropriate.\r\n\r\nDo not rely only on an in-memory mutex.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 17. Database Modeling\r\n\r\nInspect existing models before changing Prisma.\r\n\r\nAvoid a broad schema redesign.\r\n\r\nAdd only the minimum durable records required for:\r\n\r\n* package identity;\r\n* import status;\r\n* import idempotency;\r\n* package revision;\r\n* package digest;\r\n* safe audit summary;\r\n* imported Tale mappings;\r\n* asset-storage identity;\r\n* rollback/retry state.\r\n\r\nPotential models may include equivalents of:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPrivateContentImport\r\nPrivateContentImportMapping\r\nPrivateAssetObject\r\nPrivateAssetReference\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not use these exact names if current architecture already has suitable models.\r\n\r\n## 17.1 Required constraints\r\n\r\nAt minimum consider uniqueness for:\r\n\r\n* package ID plus package revision;\r\n* package SHA-256;\r\n* private asset SHA-256;\r\n* logical mapping within one import;\r\n* finalized storage object path.\r\n\r\n## 17.2 Sensitive database content\r\n\r\nThe database may necessarily contain private story content after import.\r\n\r\nTherefore:\r\n\r\n* do not log full database rows;\r\n* do not serialize draft records into generic audit metadata;\r\n* do not return unpublished content through broad list APIs;\r\n* do not include private data in error details;\r\n* do not include private data in analytics.\r\n\r\nDocument that database backups are sensitive.\r\n\r\n## 17.3 Migration safety\r\n\r\nIf Prisma changes are necessary:\r\n\r\n1. Use the current migration system.\r\n2. Create a forward migration.\r\n3. Test against an isolated database copy.\r\n4. Do not reset the canonical development database.\r\n5. Verify existing generic seed content remains valid.\r\n6. Verify rollback/recovery procedure.\r\n7. Update deployment migration instructions.\r\n8. Ensure concurrent branches touching Prisma are reconciled before final integration.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 18. Export Workflow\r\n\r\nAuthorized Creators must be able to export private Tale content into an encrypted package.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 should provide a canonical CLI.\r\n\r\nStudio export may be implemented if it fits the current architecture without weakening key handling.\r\n\r\nRecommended CLI commands:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nnpm run private-content:inspect\r\nnpm run private-content:import\r\nnpm run private-content:export\r\nnpm run private-content:verify\r\nnpm run private-content:scan\r\nnpm run private-content:backup\r\nnpm run private-content:restore-verify\r\n```\r\n\r\nExact names may follow repository conventions.\r\n\r\n## 18.1 Export collection\r\n\r\nExport must collect:\r\n\r\n* selected Tale metadata;\r\n* selected draft or published version;\r\n* story blocks;\r\n* locations;\r\n* artifacts;\r\n* relevant settings;\r\n* referenced private assets;\r\n* references required by dependencies;\r\n* provenance;\r\n* license metadata;\r\n* accessibility metadata;\r\n* package compatibility metadata.\r\n\r\nDo not include:\r\n\r\n* unrelated Tales;\r\n* unrelated private assets;\r\n* passwords;\r\n* account credentials;\r\n* invitation secrets;\r\n* active session tokens;\r\n* login sessions;\r\n* server configuration;\r\n* database credentials;\r\n* unrelated audit history;\r\n* logs.\r\n\r\n## 18.2 Referential closure\r\n\r\nExport must prove that:\r\n\r\n* every referenced asset is included or declared as a dependency;\r\n* every artifact reference resolves;\r\n* every block target resolves;\r\n* every location reference resolves;\r\n* every published version is internally consistent;\r\n* no path points outside package scope.\r\n\r\nIf closure fails, export fails.\r\n\r\n## 18.3 Export destination\r\n\r\nDefault output must be outside the repository.\r\n\r\nIf the user explicitly selects a path inside the repository:\r\n\r\n* refuse by default;\r\n* explain that private packages must remain outside Git roots;\r\n* allow a deliberate override only for synthetic test packages;\r\n* never allow a real unencrypted export.\r\n\r\nAdd repository ignore patterns for defense in depth, such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\n*.ftprivate\r\n*.ftkey\r\n.private-content/\r\nprivate-content/\r\nprivate-exports/\r\ndecrypted-private-content/\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not treat these patterns as the primary security control.\r\n\r\n## 18.4 Export verification\r\n\r\nBefore reporting success:\r\n\r\n1. Finish package encryption.\r\n2. Compute package hash.\r\n3. Reopen the package.\r\n4. Authenticate it.\r\n5. Decrypt it.\r\n6. Validate its manifest.\r\n7. Validate all checksums.\r\n8. Verify referential closure.\r\n9. Delete verification plaintext.\r\n10. Report sanitized receipt.\r\n\r\nA package that was written but not successfully round-trip verified is not a successful export.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 19. Studio Integration\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

Studio Integration\r\n\r\nIntegrate private-package operations into Creator Studio using the current universal language system.\r\n\r\nDo not hardcode obsolete wording.\r\n\r\nRecommended Creator-only area:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nStudio Settings\r\n  Private Chronicle\r\n```\r\n\r\nor the current equivalent consistent with the updated navigation language.\r\n\r\n## 19.1 Required screens\r\n\r\n### Private-content overview\r\n\r\nShow:\r\n\r\n* configured storage health;\r\n* number of imported private packages;\r\n* last successful import;\r\n* failed imports requiring attention;\r\n* package-format support;\r\n* storage usage;\r\n* backup verification status.\r\n\r\nDo not reveal private titles to unauthorized roles.\r\n\r\n### Import wizard\r\n\r\nStages:\r\n\r\n1. Select encrypted package.\r\n2. Enter or provide decryption secret.\r\n3. Authenticate and inspect.\r\n4. Review package summary.\r\n5. Review conflicts.\r\n6. Select allowed conflict resolutions.\r\n7. Confirm import.\r\n8. Show progress.\r\n9. Show completion receipt.\r\n\r\n### Export flow\r\n\r\nStages:\r\n\r\n1. Select Tale.\r\n2. Select draft or published version.\r\n3. Review included assets.\r\n4. Choose secure destination method.\r\n5. Supply encryption secret.\r\n6. Export.\r\n7. Verify.\r\n8. Present package hash and safe recovery instructions.\r\n\r\n### Import history\r\n\r\nShow:\r\n\r\n* import ID;\r\n* package ID;\r\n* revision;\r\n* date;\r\n* status;\r\n* safe counts;\r\n* warnings;\r\n* failure code;\r\n* retry eligibility.\r\n\r\nDo not show:\r\n\r\n* passphrase;\r\n* decrypted temp path;\r\n* private payload in logs;\r\n* raw archive errors;\r\n* storage root.\r\n\r\n## 19.2 Authorization\r\n\r\nOnly authorized Creator/Admin roles may:\r\n\r\n* inspect a private package;\r\n* import;\r\n* export;\r\n* retry finalization;\r\n* view private import history;\r\n* view draft assets;\r\n* delete a failed staged import.\r\n\r\nCaptain access should be limited to Tale/session behavior and preview permissions already granted.\r\n\r\nPlayer access must not include package administration.\r\n\r\n## 19.3 User experience safety\r\n\r\nThe import wizard must state clearly:\r\n\r\n* import does not publish;\r\n* import does not invite Players;\r\n* import does not launch a Tale;\r\n* imported drafts remain private;\r\n* losing the package key may make the package unrecoverable;\r\n* package hashes should be stored with backup records.\r\n\r\n## 19.4 Accessibility\r\n\r\nThe flow must support:\r\n\r\n* complete keyboard operation;\r\n* visible focus;\r\n* semantic progress states;\r\n* screen-reader status announcements;\r\n* non-color-only warning communication;\r\n* error summaries;\r\n* reduced-motion behavior;\r\n* focus restoration after dialogs;\r\n* readable long-running status.\r\n\r\nDo not announce secret values through ARIA live regions.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 20. Publishing and Visibility Safety\r\n\r\nImporting private content must not bypass existing publishing rules.\r\n\r\nThe following states must remain distinct:\r\n\r\n1. Package imported\r\n2. Tale draft created\r\n3. Draft validated\r\n4. Tale published\r\n5. Published version selected for playthrough\r\n6. Player invited\r\n7. Player accepted\r\n8. Session started\r\n9. Content revealed\r\n\r\nA package import must stop at an unpublished private state by default.\r\n\r\n## 20.1 Published snapshots\r\n\r\nWhen private content is published:\r\n\r\n* preserve immutable version behavior;\r\n* include only content required by that published version;\r\n* preserve asset authorization relationships;\r\n* maintain version-pinned sessions;\r\n* prevent later draft edits from changing active sessions.\r\n\r\n## 20.2 Player projection\r\n\r\nVerify that Player APIs receive only:\r\n\r\n* authorized published content;\r\n* currently revealed content;\r\n* Player-safe event data;\r\n* permitted asset IDs;\r\n* permitted artifact metadata.\r\n\r\nThey must not receive:\r\n\r\n* unpublished block configuration;\r\n* future riddle answers;\r\n* Captain-only notes;\r\n* Creator notes;\r\n* unrevealed finale data;\r\n* unrelated package metadata;\r\n* storage paths;\r\n* original package manifest;\r\n* provenance data not intended for Players.\r\n\r\n## 20.3 Artifact assets\r\n\r\nFuture 2D and 3D artifact assets may be imported and stored.\r\n\r\nDuring this phase:\r\n\r\n* model assets remain protected;\r\n* access is tied to artifact visibility;\r\n* unrevealed models are inaccessible;\r\n* a poster or thumbnail remains protected;\r\n* storage metadata may identify `model-3d`;\r\n* the package validator may approve GLB;\r\n* no full 3D viewer is required.\r\n\r\nDo not expose a model simply because the Tale references it.\r\n\r\nThe reveal state remains authoritative.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 21. Secret Scanning and Repository Guards\r\n\r\nImplement defense in depth.\r\n\r\n## 21.1 Repository scan\r\n\r\nCreate a command that detects:\r\n\r\n* `.ftprivate` packages;\r\n* key files;\r\n* decrypted private directories;\r\n* configured private asset roots inside the repository;\r\n* suspicious private-content directories;\r\n* private export manifests;\r\n* known secret patterns;\r\n* real credentials;\r\n* forbidden file extensions in tracked private locations;\r\n* private test sentinel strings where not expected.\r\n\r\nUse an existing maintained secret scanner if one is already configured.\r\n\r\nIf introducing a scanner such as Gitleaks, do so deliberately and document configuration.\r\n\r\nDo not print matched secret values.\r\n\r\nPrint:\r\n\r\n* file;\r\n* line number where safe;\r\n* rule ID;\r\n* classification;\r\n* remediation guidance.\r\n\r\n## 21.2 Staged-diff guard\r\n\r\nProvide a guard suitable for:\r\n\r\n* pre-commit usage;\r\n* CI usage;\r\n* manual execution.\r\n\r\nIt must inspect staged or proposed changes without exposing secrets.\r\n\r\nDo not require every developer to install a global binary if a repository-local method is practical.\r\n\r\n## 21.3 Build-output scan\r\n\r\nAfter production build, scan:\r\n\r\n```text\r\n.next/\r\nbuild output\r\nstatic output\r\nsource maps\r\ntest artifacts\r\n```\r\n\r\nfor known synthetic private sentinel values.\r\n\r\nCreate a synthetic test Tale containing a sentinel such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nSEALED-HOLD-SYNTHETIC-PRIVATE-SENTINEL-73A9C1\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe sentinel may appear only in:\r\n\r\n* authorized server-side test storage;\r\n* encrypted fixture payload;\r\n* expected test source fixture.\r\n\r\nIt must not appear in:\r\n\r\n* public JavaScript bundles;\r\n* static HTML;\r\n* source maps;\r\n* public asset output;\r\n* unauthorized API responses;\r\n* Playwright screenshots or traces intended as general artifacts.\r\n\r\n## 21.4 CI gate\r\n\r\nAdd or prepare a CI gate that runs:\r\n\r\n* secret scanning;\r\n* forbidden-path scanning;\r\n* private-package detection;\r\n* build-output sentinel scan;\r\n* package-format tests;\r\n* authorization tests.\r\n\r\nIf complete hosted CI is not yet available, add the repository commands and workflow definition without pretending deployment infrastructure exists.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 22. Logging and Audit\r\n\r\nCreate one redaction policy used by:\r\n\r\n* package services;\r\n* API routes;\r\n* CLI tools;\r\n* import history;\r\n* audit events;\r\n* error handling.\r\n\r\n## 22.1 Allowed log fields\r\n\r\nLogs may include:\r\n\r\n* correlation ID;\r\n* import ID;\r\n* package ID;\r\n* package revision;\r\n* package digest;\r\n* format version;\r\n* safe status;\r\n* safe failure code;\r\n* file count;\r\n* asset count;\r\n* total bytes;\r\n* Tale count;\r\n* reused asset count;\r\n* created asset count;\r\n* elapsed time;\r\n* authenticated user ID;\r\n* authorization decision.\r\n\r\n## 22.2 Forbidden log fields\r\n\r\nDo not log:\r\n\r\n* package passphrase;\r\n* derived key;\r\n* raw nonce plus key material;\r\n* decrypted payload;\r\n* Tale prose;\r\n* riddle text;\r\n* accepted answers;\r\n* dedication;\r\n* private titles where unnecessary;\r\n* private filenames;\r\n* private asset paths;\r\n* recipient names;\r\n* invitation tokens;\r\n* session tokens;\r\n* raw database rows;\r\n* full stack traces containing private configuration.\r\n\r\n## 22.3 Audit events\r\n\r\nAudit at least:\r\n\r\n* inspection started;\r\n* inspection failed;\r\n* dry-run completed;\r\n* import confirmed;\r\n* import completed;\r\n* import failed;\r\n* import rolled back;\r\n* finalization retried;\r\n* private package exported;\r\n* package verification completed;\r\n* private asset accessed administratively;\r\n* private content deleted if deletion is supported.\r\n\r\nAudit metadata must remain sanitized.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 23. Backup and Recovery\r\n\r\nPrivate content must be recoverable without relying on Git.\r\n\r\nImplement or document a safe backup model.\r\n\r\n## 23.1 Minimum Phase 1 backup\r\n\r\nProvide a command that can create an encrypted private backup containing:\r\n\r\n* selected private Tale packages;\r\n* package/import registry metadata;\r\n* referenced private assets;\r\n* checksums;\r\n* compatibility metadata;\r\n* restore instructions.\r\n\r\nThe backup may reuse the private package encryption layer.\r\n\r\nDo not bundle:\r\n\r\n* application credentials;\r\n* active user sessions;\r\n* unrelated logs;\r\n* plaintext keys.\r\n\r\n## 23.2 Database considerations\r\n\r\nDocument that imported private prose may exist in the database.\r\n\r\nThe operations runbook must explain:\r\n\r\n* development SQLite backup sensitivity where applicable;\r\n* production MySQL backup sensitivity;\r\n* encryption requirements;\r\n* storage location;\r\n* retention;\r\n* restoration order;\r\n* consistency between database and asset storage.\r\n\r\nDo not build a fragile cross-database backup abstraction merely to claim completion.\r\n\r\nIf the current system supports SQLite development and MySQL production, provide:\r\n\r\n* verified development procedure;\r\n* documented production procedure;\r\n* interfaces or hooks for later automation.\r\n\r\n## 23.3 Restore verification\r\n\r\nProvide a restore-verification operation that:\r\n\r\n1. Opens backup.\r\n2. Authenticates and validates it.\r\n3. Restores into an isolated temporary environment.\r\n4. Verifies package and asset hashes.\r\n5. Verifies Tale referential closure.\r\n6. Verifies private assets remain unauthorized to anonymous users.\r\n7. Produces a sanitized report.\r\n8. Removes temporary restored plaintext.\r\n\r\nDo not restore over the canonical database during testing.\r\n\r\n## 23.4 Recovery documentation\r\n\r\nDocument:\r\n\r\n* what is recoverable;\r\n* what requires the package key;\r\n* what happens if the key is lost;\r\n* what happens if package and database revisions disagree;\r\n* how to verify a backup before deleting older copies;\r\n* recommended redundant storage;\r\n* recommended offline copy;\r\n* checksum recording;\r\n* restore rehearsal cadence.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 24. Synthetic Fixtures Only\r\n\r\nAll committed private-content fixtures must be synthetic.\r\n\r\nDo not use:\r\n\r\n* Seraâ€™s real name inside story fixtures;\r\n* real photos;\r\n* real dedication text;\r\n* real riddles;\r\n* real accepted answers;\r\n* real invitation wording;\r\n* real personal dates;\r\n* real finale content;\r\n* real voice recordings;\r\n* real artifact designs intended as surprises.\r\n\r\nCreate clearly fictional fixtures such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nThe Clockmakerâ€™s Lantern\r\nPlayer: Test Mariner\r\nArtifact: Brass Test Token\r\nRiddle answer: synthetic-answer-42\r\n```\r\n\r\nInclude the synthetic sentinel for leak testing.\r\n\r\nFixtures must be visually and semantically sufficient to test:\r\n\r\n* private prose;\r\n* hidden answers;\r\n* images;\r\n* audio metadata;\r\n* future GLB metadata;\r\n* artifacts;\r\n* locations;\r\n* publishing;\r\n* Player reveal;\r\n* unauthorized access.\r\n\r\nDo not commit large binary fixtures when tiny deterministic test assets are sufficient.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 25. Required CLI Behavior\r\n\r\nCLI tools must:\r\n\r\n* provide `--help`;\r\n* return nonzero exit codes on failure;\r\n* support machine-readable output where useful;\r\n* never echo passphrases;\r\n* never include private content in default output;\r\n* support quiet mode;\r\n* support sanitized JSON receipts;\r\n* validate output paths;\r\n* refuse repository-root private export by default;\r\n* clean temporary files;\r\n* handle Ctrl+C safely;\r\n* distinguish user error from package corruption;\r\n* distinguish incompatible package from wrong key only in protected diagnostics, not unsafe public text.\r\n\r\nRecommended examples:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nnpm run private-content:inspect -- /path/to/package.ftprivate\r\nnpm run private-content:verify -- /path/to/package.ftprivate\r\nnpm run private-content:import -- /path/to/package.ftprivate --dry-run\r\nnpm run private-content:import -- /path/to/package.ftprivate --commit\r\nnpm run private-content:export -- --tale <id> --out /safe/path/\r\nnpm run private-content:scan\r\nnpm run private-content:backup -- --out /safe/path/\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not put the passphrase directly in the documented command line.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 26. API Routes\r\n\r\nUse current route conventions.\r\n\r\nPotential routes may include equivalents of:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPOST /api/studio/private-content/inspect\r\nPOST /api/studio/private-content/import\r\nGET  /api/studio/private-content/imports\r\nGET  /api/studio/private-content/imports/:id\r\nPOST /api/studio/private-content/imports/:id/retry\r\nPOST /api/studio/private-content/export\r\nGET  /api/private-assets/:assetId\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not add routes merely to match this list if current architecture suggests a better design.\r\n\r\n## 26.1 Route requirements\r\n\r\nEvery private-content route must include:\r\n\r\n* authentication;\r\n* role authorization;\r\n* CSRF protection where appropriate;\r\n* rate limiting;\r\n* package-size limits;\r\n* request cancellation;\r\n* safe error mapping;\r\n* correlation ID;\r\n* redacted logging;\r\n* no caching of private administrative responses;\r\n* no secret in query parameters.\r\n\r\n## 26.2 Upload behavior\r\n\r\nUse streaming where practical.\r\n\r\nDo not load an arbitrarily large package into memory.\r\n\r\nEnforce limits before and during upload.\r\n\r\nCancel and clean staging when:\r\n\r\n* client disconnects;\r\n* authorization expires;\r\n* size exceeds limit;\r\n* decryption fails;\r\n* validation fails;\r\n* request is aborted.\r\n\r\n## 26.3 Long-running operations\r\n\r\nIf current request architecture cannot safely complete import synchronously:\r\n\r\n* create a durable import record;\r\n* return a safe operation ID;\r\n* expose status polling;\r\n* keep operation idempotent.\r\n\r\nDo not invent a fake background worker.\r\n\r\nIf no durable worker exists, keep Phase 1 imports bounded and document the limitation.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 27. Universal Language Integration\r\n\r\nAll new UI language must use the current universal language architecture.\r\n\r\nDo not reintroduce old words that the language overhaul replaced.\r\n\r\nThe new interface should feel consistent with the experience but remain operationally clear.\r\n\r\nAvoid excessively theatrical wording for destructive or security-sensitive actions.\r\n\r\nFor example, a button may be titled:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nImport Private Chronicle\r\n```\r\n\r\nrather than an ambiguous phrase such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nUnseal the Ancient Vault of Hidden Tides\r\n```\r\n\r\nHumans need to know what the button does before it consumes a 2 GB encrypted package and their remaining optimism.\r\n\r\nSecurity errors must be calm and precise.\r\n\r\nDo not expose internal cryptographic jargon to normal users unless placed in an advanced details section.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 28. Animation and Motion Integration\r\n\r\nThis is not an animation phase.\r\n\r\nNew Studio flows must use the current accepted animation architecture and resolved motion policy.\r\n\r\nDo not:\r\n\r\n* introduce an independent animation runtime;\r\n* bypass Lanternwake ownership rules;\r\n* create route transitions outside current hosts;\r\n* add dramatic unbounded import animations;\r\n* delay critical error display for ceremony.\r\n\r\nUse subtle motion for:\r\n\r\n* wizard progression;\r\n* validation state;\r\n* import completion;\r\n* expandable details.\r\n\r\nReduced motion must preserve:\r\n\r\n* all information;\r\n* progress;\r\n* focus;\r\n* status;\r\n* error recovery.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 29. Required Tests\r\n\r\n## 29.1 Encryption tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* successful encryption/decryption;\r\n* wrong passphrase;\r\n* tampered ciphertext;\r\n* tampered authentication tag;\r\n* tampered envelope;\r\n* malformed nonce;\r\n* malformed salt;\r\n* unsupported cipher;\r\n* unsupported KDF;\r\n* truncated payload;\r\n* duplicate nonce defense where applicable;\r\n* empty payload rejection;\r\n* cleanup after failure;\r\n* no passphrase in thrown error;\r\n* no passphrase in logs.\r\n\r\n## 29.2 Package-format tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* valid v1 package;\r\n* unsupported envelope version;\r\n* unsupported payload version;\r\n* missing manifest;\r\n* malformed manifest;\r\n* unsupported application version;\r\n* missing checksum file;\r\n* checksum mismatch;\r\n* missing referenced asset;\r\n* extra unreferenced asset warning;\r\n* duplicate asset path;\r\n* duplicate logical ID;\r\n* invalid media type;\r\n* invalid GLB signature;\r\n* executable file rejection;\r\n* invalid JSON depth;\r\n* excessive file count;\r\n* excessive byte count.\r\n\r\n## 29.3 Archive-safety tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* `../` traversal;\r\n* absolute Unix path;\r\n* Windows drive path;\r\n* UNC path;\r\n* symbolic link;\r\n* hard link;\r\n* null byte;\r\n* duplicate normalized path;\r\n* case-fold collision;\r\n* reserved Windows filename;\r\n* decompression bomb;\r\n* extreme compression ratio;\r\n* nested archive;\r\n* overlong filename;\r\n* malformed Unicode path.\r\n\r\n## 29.4 Import-planning tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* clean new Tale;\r\n* same package and revision;\r\n* newer package revision;\r\n* Tale ID conflict;\r\n* Tale slug conflict;\r\n* published-version conflict;\r\n* asset hash reuse;\r\n* logical-ID conflict;\r\n* missing dependency;\r\n* incompatible schema;\r\n* explicit slug remap;\r\n* ID mapping stability;\r\n* dry-run creates no domain records;\r\n* dry-run creates no finalized assets.\r\n\r\n## 29.5 Import-execution tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* successful import;\r\n* imported Tale remains unpublished;\r\n* no session is created;\r\n* no invitation is created;\r\n* no Player access is granted;\r\n* database failure cleans staged assets;\r\n* asset staging failure changes no database state;\r\n* finalization failure leaves content unavailable;\r\n* retry finalization succeeds;\r\n* process retry does not duplicate Tale;\r\n* same package import is idempotent;\r\n* concurrent same-package imports produce one completed import;\r\n* audit receipt is sanitized;\r\n* temporary plaintext is removed.\r\n\r\n## 29.6 Export tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* draft export;\r\n* published-version export;\r\n* referenced asset closure;\r\n* missing asset failure;\r\n* unrelated asset excluded;\r\n* credentials excluded;\r\n* invitation secrets excluded;\r\n* round-trip verification;\r\n* package hash generation;\r\n* output inside repository refused;\r\n* interrupted export cleans temporary files;\r\n* wrong verification key fails safely.\r\n\r\n## 29.7 Asset authorization tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* anonymous request denied;\r\n* unrelated Player denied;\r\n* invited but unaccepted Player denied where policy requires acceptance;\r\n* authorized Creator can access draft asset;\r\n* authorized Captain can access permitted preview asset;\r\n* authorized Player can access revealed published asset;\r\n* Player cannot access unrevealed artifact asset;\r\n* Player cannot access future chapter media;\r\n* revoked membership loses access;\r\n* expired signed URL fails;\r\n* guessed asset ID does not reveal existence;\r\n* thumbnail follows source authorization;\r\n* range request respects authorization;\r\n* original storage path is never exposed.\r\n\r\n## 29.8 Publishing tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* import does not publish;\r\n* explicit publish creates immutable version;\r\n* active playthrough remains pinned;\r\n* later imported revision does not mutate active playthrough;\r\n* unpublished answers are absent from Player APIs;\r\n* Creator notes are absent from Player APIs;\r\n* private package metadata is absent from Player APIs;\r\n* asset IDs appear only when permitted.\r\n\r\n## 29.9 Secret-scan tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* `.ftprivate` tracked file detected;\r\n* key file detected;\r\n* decrypted directory detected;\r\n* synthetic private sentinel in public source detected;\r\n* sentinel in authorized encrypted fixture allowed;\r\n* matched secret value is redacted;\r\n* build output scan finds deliberate leak fixture;\r\n* clean build passes;\r\n* source map leak detected;\r\n* screenshot/trace leak detected where test framework permits inspection.\r\n\r\n## 29.10 Studio tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* unauthorized role cannot open private-content administration;\r\n* authorized Creator can inspect;\r\n* wrong passphrase receives safe error;\r\n* dry-run preview shows conflicts;\r\n* import requires explicit confirmation;\r\n* import wizard does not auto-publish;\r\n* keyboard-only completion;\r\n* focus restoration;\r\n* reduced-motion state;\r\n* screen-reader status;\r\n* abort cancels and cleans staging;\r\n* refresh preserves safe operation state where supported;\r\n* passphrase is not persisted in browser storage.\r\n\r\n## 29.11 Backup tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* encrypted backup creation;\r\n* backup verification;\r\n* tampered backup failure;\r\n* isolated restore;\r\n* restored checksums;\r\n* restored authorization;\r\n* missing asset detection;\r\n* database/asset mismatch detection;\r\n* temporary restore cleanup;\r\n* sanitized recovery report.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 30. Browser and Database Isolation\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

Mission\r\n\r\nImplement **Phase 1 of Project Sealed Hold**, the private-content architecture for the Forever Treasure / Tall Tale platform.\r\n\r\nThis phase is called:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nLock the Chronicle\r\n```\r\n\r\nIts formal engineering scope is:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPrivate Content Boundaries, Encrypted Chronicle Packages,\r\nSecure Asset Storage, Import/Export Safety, and Deployment Isolation\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe repository is intended to remain a reusable, generic application engine.\r\n\r\nThe real Chronicle may eventually contain:\r\n\r\n* private story text;\r\n* personal dedications;\r\n* unpublished riddles and accepted answers;\r\n* photographs;\r\n* artwork;\r\n* audio;\r\n* video;\r\n* 2D artifact imagery;\r\n* future 3D artifact models;\r\n* creator-only notes;\r\n* surprise reveals;\r\n* private invitation wording;\r\n* finale material;\r\n* content intended for only one Player or one crew.\r\n\r\nThat material must not be treated like ordinary source code.\r\n\r\nThe goal of this phase is to create a secure, versioned architecture in which:\r\n\r\n1. The public engine repository remains generic and safe to share.\r\n2. Private Chronicle content exists outside Git history.\r\n3. Private content can be packaged, encrypted, validated, imported, backed up, restored and deployed.\r\n4. Importing content does not automatically publish it.\r\n5. Publishing content does not automatically expose every asset.\r\n6. Player access remains controlled by existing Tale, session, invitation and visibility rules.\r\n7. Private assets are never stored under a public static directory.\r\n8. Package decryption credentials are never committed, logged or persisted insecurely.\r\n9. Tampered, malformed or incompatible packages fail closed.\r\n10. Failed imports do not leave partially created Tales or orphaned private assets.\r\n11. Re-importing the same package is deterministic and idempotent.\r\n12. Existing generic seed content and current public behavior remain intact.\r\n13. Future 2D and 3D artifact assets can be carried safely without implementing the full artifact renderer during this phase.\r\n\r\nThe core rule is:\r\n\r\n> The repository contains the engine. The sealed package contains the Chronicle.\r\n\r\nAnother governing rule is:\r\n\r\n> Gitignore is a convenience, not a security boundary.\r\n\r\nA file being ignored does not make it safe. The implementation must include active validation, scanning, access control, storage isolation and operational documentation.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 2. Current Repository Context\r\n\r\nImplementation must target the **current checked-out canonical repository state**.\r\n\r\nDo not assume that the repository still matches an earlier audit commit.\r\n\r\nBefore editing:\r\n\r\n1. Determine the repository root.\r\n2. Record:\r\n\r\n   * current branch;\r\n   * current commit;\r\n   * default branch;\r\n   * upstream;\r\n   * ahead/behind state;\r\n   * staged changes;\r\n   * unstaged changes;\r\n   * untracked files;\r\n   * linked worktrees.\r\n3. Determine whether:\r\n\r\n   * Lanternwake Phase 5 has been merged;\r\n   * the universal-language overhaul has been merged;\r\n   * any private-content or artifact work already exists;\r\n   * another worktree is modifying Prisma, Studio, assets, security or publishing.\r\n4. Revalidate current models, routes, authorization helpers, asset storage and publishing logic.\r\n5. Preserve all existing work.\r\n6. Do not overwrite newer changes with older documentation or remembered architecture.\r\n7. Do not merge unrelated branches.\r\n8. Do not automatically merge this work into `main`.\r\n\r\nIf concurrent integration work is still occurring, create or use a dedicated worktree and branch such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ncodex/project-sealed-hold-phase-1\r\n```\r\n\r\nBranch from the current approved canonical base.\r\n\r\nDo not decide independently that an unmerged feature branch is authoritative.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 3. Mandatory Governing Review\r\n\r\nBefore writing code, inspect the current versions of all relevant files and systems, including equivalents if paths have changed:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nREADME.md\r\nSECURITY.md\r\n.env.example\r\npackage.json\r\nnext.config.ts\r\nprisma/schema.prisma\r\nsrc/domain/\r\nsrc/lib/\r\nsrc/app/api/\r\nsrc/components/studio/\r\nsrc/components/player/\r\nsrc/components/captain/\r\nsrc/animation/\r\nscripts/\r\ntests/\r\nDevelopment_Docs/\r\n```\r\n\r\nSpecifically identify:\r\n\r\n* Tall Tale models;\r\n* Tale drafts;\r\n* immutable published versions;\r\n* published snapshots;\r\n* assets and asset variants;\r\n* artifacts and artifact references;\r\n* story blocks;\r\n* location references;\r\n* invitation authorization;\r\n* Player memberships;\r\n* Captain/Creator authorization;\r\n* asset upload routes;\r\n* asset-serving routes;\r\n* visibility projection;\r\n* audit-event infrastructure;\r\n* database transaction patterns;\r\n* current local asset root;\r\n* any S3/object-storage abstraction;\r\n* current logging and redaction;\r\n* current background-job capabilities;\r\n* current backup scripts;\r\n* current secret-scanning or CI systems.\r\n\r\nSearch the repository for possible private material.\r\n\r\nThe scan must be read-only.\r\n\r\nDo not print potentially private prose into logs or the final report.\r\n\r\nReport only:\r\n\r\n* number of suspicious files;\r\n* paths;\r\n* classification;\r\n* whether content appears synthetic, generic or potentially private.\r\n\r\nIf actual private content appears to have already entered Git history:\r\n\r\n1. Do not delete it automatically.\r\n2. Do not rewrite history.\r\n3. Do not quote it in reports.\r\n4. Mark it as a security finding.\r\n5. Stop any action that would replicate it.\r\n6. Recommend a separately authorized history-remediation operation.\r\n\r\nHistory rewriting is explicitly outside this implementation phase unless separately approved.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 4. Repository Safety\r\n\r\nNever use destructive repository commands.\r\n\r\nDo not use:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ngit reset --hard\r\ngit clean\r\ngit checkout -- .\r\ngit restore .\r\ngit restore --staged .\r\ngit stash --all\r\nforce push\r\nbroad git add .\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not:\r\n\r\n* modify unrelated user files;\r\n* delete unknown untracked files;\r\n* replace current files with baseline copies;\r\n* stage the entire repository;\r\n* commit private package fixtures containing real content;\r\n* commit decrypted package output;\r\n* commit real keys;\r\n* commit real passwords;\r\n* commit real photos, recordings, riddles or finale material.\r\n\r\nUse targeted staging only.\r\n\r\nPreserve concurrent edits.\r\n\r\nBefore modifying a high-conflict file such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nprisma/schema.prisma\r\npackage.json\r\nSECURITY.md\r\n.env.example\r\nnext.config.ts\r\nStudio navigation files\r\npublished snapshot schemas\r\nasset service modules\r\nauthorization helpers\r\n```\r\n\r\ninspect the current file and recent changes carefully.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 5. Threat Model\r\n\r\nThe threat model must explicitly cover at least the following.\r\n\r\n## 6.1 Accidental Git exposure\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* private files copied into the repository;\r\n* exports written inside the repository;\r\n* decrypted temporary files left under the project root;\r\n* a developer using `git add .`;\r\n* private assets placed under `public/`;\r\n* generated thumbnails copied into tracked directories;\r\n* package keys placed in `.env.example`;\r\n* real content included in test fixtures;\r\n* client-side bundles containing unpublished story text.\r\n\r\n## 6.2 Unauthorized HTTP access\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* guessing an asset URL;\r\n* accessing a private asset without membership;\r\n* using an expired invitation;\r\n* accessing Creator-only content as a Player;\r\n* requesting an unpublished draft through a public route;\r\n* accessing thumbnails when the original asset is protected;\r\n* using stale signed URLs after access revocation;\r\n* cross-Tale asset-reference confusion.\r\n\r\n## 6.3 Package tampering\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* changed encrypted payload;\r\n* changed manifest;\r\n* corrupted authentication tag;\r\n* checksum mismatch;\r\n* missing asset;\r\n* replaced asset;\r\n* unsupported package version;\r\n* invalid schema;\r\n* conflicting IDs;\r\n* altered published version;\r\n* package claiming a different source Tale.\r\n\r\n## 6.4 Malicious archives\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* path traversal;\r\n* absolute paths;\r\n* drive-letter paths;\r\n* symbolic links;\r\n* hard links;\r\n* duplicate paths;\r\n* decompression bombs;\r\n* excessive entry count;\r\n* excessive total extracted size;\r\n* unexpected executable files;\r\n* MIME spoofing;\r\n* nested archives;\r\n* malformed Unicode filenames;\r\n* case-folding collisions;\r\n* reserved Windows filenames.\r\n\r\n## 6.5 Secret leakage through operations\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* logs;\r\n* stack traces;\r\n* audit events;\r\n* analytics;\r\n* error-monitoring payloads;\r\n* shell history;\r\n* process arguments;\r\n* temporary files;\r\n* CI artifacts;\r\n* test screenshots;\r\n* Playwright traces;\r\n* browser local storage;\r\n* browser source maps;\r\n* database dumps;\r\n* backup manifests.\r\n\r\n## 6.6 Partial import corruption\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* database transaction succeeds but asset move fails;\r\n* asset staging succeeds but database transaction fails;\r\n* process crashes during commit;\r\n* duplicate package import;\r\n* concurrent import of the same package;\r\n* two packages contain conflicting Tales;\r\n* imported asset becomes visible before authorization state exists.\r\n\r\n## 6.7 Loss and recovery\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* private package lost;\r\n* key lost;\r\n* database restored without assets;\r\n* assets restored without matching database;\r\n* stale backup;\r\n* package version no longer readable;\r\n* backup tampering;\r\n* incomplete restore.\r\n\r\nFor each threat, document:\r\n\r\n* asset at risk;\r\n* attacker or failure mode;\r\n* current control;\r\n* new Phase 1 control;\r\n* remaining limitation;\r\n* test or operational verification.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 7. Explicit Non-Goals\r\n\r\nDo not implement the following during Phase 1 unless an exact dependency requires a narrow interface:\r\n\r\n* the full 2D/3D artifact inspection experience;\r\n* Three.js or React Three Fiber rendering;\r\n* final GLB presentation;\r\n* artifact assembly animation;\r\n* public Tale marketplace;\r\n* public content sharing;\r\n* multi-tenant billing;\r\n* DRM;\r\n* external KMS integration;\r\n* hardware security modules;\r\n* complete object-storage migration;\r\n* antivirus vendor integration;\r\n* production media transcoding;\r\n* facial recognition;\r\n* private-content history rewriting;\r\n* broad account-system redesign;\r\n* unrelated Lanternwake animation work;\r\n* broad language rewrites;\r\n* a replacement Tale publishing system;\r\n* automatic publishing after import;\r\n* automatic session creation after import;\r\n* automatic invitation delivery after import;\r\n* automatic deployment after import.\r\n\r\nPhase 1 may define interfaces and metadata needed by future 2D/3D assets, but it must not implement Project #3â€™s renderer or reveal system.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 8. Required Architecture\r\n\r\nCreate a cohesive private-content subsystem rather than distributing cryptographic and archive behavior across API routes.\r\n\r\nRecommended organization:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nsrc/private-content/\r\n  core/\r\n    types.ts\r\n    errors.ts\r\n    constants.ts\r\n    redaction.ts\r\n\r\n  package/\r\n    envelope.ts\r\n    manifest.ts\r\n    manifest.schema.ts\r\n    compatibility.ts\r\n    encrypt.ts\r\n    decrypt.ts\r\n    archive-reader.ts\r\n    archive-writer.ts\r\n    integrity.ts\r\n    limits.ts\r\n\r\n  storage/\r\n    PrivateAssetStore.ts\r\n    LocalPrivateAssetStore.ts\r\n    private-path-policy.ts\r\n    asset-access.ts\r\n\r\n  import/\r\n    import-types.ts\r\n    inspect-package.ts\r\n    build-import-plan.ts\r\n    conflict-policy.ts\r\n    stage-assets.ts\r\n    execute-import.ts\r\n    rollback-import.ts\r\n    import-audit.ts\r\n\r\n  export/\r\n    collect-export-content.ts\r\n    build-export-manifest.ts\r\n    stage-export.ts\r\n    write-package.ts\r\n    verify-round-trip.ts\r\n\r\n  backup/\r\n    backup-types.ts\r\n    create-private-backup.ts\r\n    verify-private-backup.ts\r\n    restore-private-backup.ts\r\n\r\n  security/\r\n    secret-policy.ts\r\n    repository-scan.ts\r\n    build-output-scan.ts\r\n    temp-file-policy.ts\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe exact structure may change if current repository conventions suggest a better shape.\r\n\r\nDo not place cryptographic primitives directly in React components or route handlers.\r\n\r\nAPI routes must call domain services.\r\n\r\nCLI scripts must call the same domain services.\r\n\r\nStudio must call typed API routes backed by the same validation and authorization.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 9. Core Types\r\n\r\nDefine typed interfaces or discriminated unions for at least:\r\n\r\n```ts\r\ntype PrivatePackageFormatVersion = 1;\r\n\r\ntype PrivatePackageEnvelope = {\r\n  format: \"forever-treasure-private-package\";\r\n  envelopeVersion: 1;\r\n  payloadFormatVersion: number;\r\n  cipher: string;\r\n  keyDerivation: string;\r\n  salt: string;\r\n  nonce: string;\r\n  authenticationTag: string;\r\n  encryptedPayloadBytes: number;\r\n  encryptedPayloadSha256: string;\r\n  createdAt: string;\r\n};\r\n\r\ntype PrivatePackageManifest = {\r\n  packageId: string;\r\n  packageRevision: number;\r\n  formatVersion: number;\r\n  createdAt: string;\r\n  sourceApplicationVersion: string;\r\n  minimumApplicationVersion: string;\r\n  maximumApplicationVersion?: string;\r\n  classification: \"private\";\r\n  contentType: \"tale-draft\" | \"published-tale\" | \"tale-archive\";\r\n  tales: PrivatePackageTaleEntry[];\r\n  assets: PrivatePackageAssetEntry[];\r\n  dependencies: PrivatePackageDependency[];\r\n  totals: {\r\n    files: number;\r\n    assets: number;\r\n    plaintextBytes: number;\r\n  };\r\n};\r\n\r\ntype PrivatePackageAssetEntry = {\r\n  logicalId: string;\r\n  sha256: string;\r\n  relativePath: string;\r\n  mediaType: string;\r\n  byteLength: number;\r\n  originalFilename?: string;\r\n  representation:\r\n    | \"image\"\r\n    | \"audio\"\r\n    | \"video\"\r\n    | \"document\"\r\n    | \"model-3d\"\r\n    | \"binary\";\r\n  role?: string;\r\n  source?: string;\r\n  license?: string;\r\n  accessibilityDescription?: string;\r\n};\r\n\r\ntype PrivatePackageInspection =\r\n  | {\r\n      status: \"valid\";\r\n      envelope: SanitizedEnvelopeSummary;\r\n      manifest: SanitizedManifestSummary;\r\n      importPlan: PrivateImportPlan;\r\n    }\r\n  | {\r\n      status: \"invalid\";\r\n      code: PrivatePackageFailureCode;\r\n      safeMessage: string;\r\n    };\r\n\r\ntype PrivateImportStatus =\r\n  | \"CREATED\"\r\n  | \"VALIDATING\"\r\n  | \"PLANNED\"\r\n  | \"STAGING_ASSETS\"\r\n  | \"COMMITTING_DATABASE\"\r\n  | \"FINALIZING_ASSETS\"\r\n  | \"COMPLETED\"\r\n  | \"FAILED\"\r\n  | \"ROLLED_BACK\";\r\n\r\ntype PrivateImportConflict =\r\n  | \"PACKAGE_ALREADY_IMPORTED\"\r\n  | \"TALE_ID_CONFLICT\"\r\n  | \"TALE_SLUG_CONFLICT\"\r\n  | \"PUBLISHED_VERSION_CONFLICT\"\r\n  | \"ASSET_LOGICAL_ID_CONFLICT\"\r\n  | \"ASSET_HASH_REUSE\"\r\n  | \"UNSUPPORTED_SCHEMA_VERSION\"\r\n  | \"MISSING_DEPENDENCY\";\r\n\r\ntype PrivateImportReceipt = {\r\n  importId: string;\r\n  packageId: string;\r\n  packageRevision: number;\r\n  packageSha256: string;\r\n  status: PrivateImportStatus;\r\n  importedTaleIds: string[];\r\n  importedAssetIds: string[];\r\n  reusedAssetIds: string[];\r\n  idMappings: Record<string, string>;\r\n  warnings: SafePrivateImportWarning[];\r\n  startedAt: string;\r\n  completedAt?: string;\r\n};\r\n```\r\n\r\nExact names may differ, but do not replace typed outcomes with generic thrown strings.\r\n\r\nAll externally visible errors must have:\r\n\r\n* stable machine-readable code;\r\n* safe human-readable message;\r\n* server-side correlation ID;\r\n* no private content;\r\n* no decrypted filenames unless authorized and necessary.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 10. Private Chronicle Package Format\r\n\r\nCreate a versioned package extension such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\n.ftprivate\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe package must contain an authenticated encrypted payload.\r\n\r\nA recommended outer structure is:\r\n\r\n```text\r\npackage.ftprivate\r\n  envelope.json\r\n  payload.enc\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe decrypted payload may contain:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nmanifest.json\r\ncontent/\r\n  tale.json\r\n  drafts/\r\n  published-versions/\r\n  blocks/\r\n  artifacts/\r\n  locations/\r\nassets/\r\n  index.json\r\n  objects/\r\n    <sha256>.<extension>\r\nprovenance/\r\n  licenses.json\r\n  sources.json\r\nchecksums.json\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe exact payload structure must be documented and versioned.\r\n\r\n## 10.1 Envelope rules\r\n\r\nThe unencrypted envelope must reveal only operational information required to decrypt and validate the package.\r\n\r\nDo not include:\r\n\r\n* Tale title;\r\n* Player name;\r\n* recipient name;\r\n* story summary;\r\n* filenames revealing surprises;\r\n* artifact names;\r\n* private creator notes;\r\n* chapter titles;\r\n* riddle text.\r\n\r\nUse generic envelope metadata.\r\n\r\n## 10.2 Payload rules\r\n\r\nThe encrypted payload must contain:\r\n\r\n* complete manifest;\r\n* content data;\r\n* referenced private assets;\r\n* asset metadata;\r\n* checksums;\r\n* package compatibility metadata;\r\n* provenance and license information where available.\r\n\r\nUse relative paths only.\r\n\r\nNo absolute paths.\r\n\r\nNo parent traversal.\r\n\r\nNo symlinks.\r\n\r\nNo hard links.\r\n\r\nNo executable scripts.\r\n\r\nNo package content may be executed during import.\r\n\r\n## 10.3 Content addressing\r\n\r\nPrivate asset objects should be content-addressed by SHA-256 or equivalent strong digest.\r\n\r\nBenefits must include:\r\n\r\n* deduplication;\r\n* tamper detection;\r\n* deterministic import;\r\n* backup verification;\r\n* safe object-storage migration;\r\n* stable identity independent of original filename.\r\n\r\nOriginal filenames may be stored as metadata, but must not become trusted storage paths.\r\n\r\n## 10.4 Package digest\r\n\r\nAfter package creation, report:\r\n\r\n* package SHA-256;\r\n* encrypted payload SHA-256;\r\n* package ID;\r\n* package revision;\r\n* format version;\r\n* asset count;\r\n* total bytes.\r\n\r\nDo not report private titles or filenames in ordinary logs.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 11. Encryption Requirements\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

Browser and Database Isolation\r\n\r\nNo mutating browser test may use the canonical development or production database.\r\n\r\nBefore mutation:\r\n\r\n1. Create a uniquely named isolated database.\r\n2. Create a uniquely named private asset root.\r\n3. Create a uniquely named staging root.\r\n4. Use an alternate owned server port.\r\n5. Record:\r\n\r\n   * process ID;\r\n   * port;\r\n   * database path or redacted database identity;\r\n   * private asset root;\r\n   * staging root;\r\n   * test nonce.\r\n6. Verify the running application resolves the isolated resources.\r\n7. Hash or record the canonical database.\r\n8. Hash or record the canonical private asset root where present.\r\n9. Run mutating tests.\r\n10. Verify canonical resources remain unchanged.\r\n11. Delete isolated plaintext staging.\r\n\r\nDo not assume a different port implies database isolation.\r\n\r\nUse synthetic content only.\r\n\r\nOnly one validation owner may control:\r\n\r\n* server process;\r\n* test database;\r\n* test asset root;\r\n* test staging root;\r\n* Playwright mutation suite;\r\n* final validation.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 31. Performance and Resource Limits\r\n\r\nPhase 1 is not a performance-optimization project, but operations must be bounded.\r\n\r\nDefine and test budgets such as:\r\n\r\n* package-envelope validation under 100 ms for normal packages;\r\n* manifest validation under 500 ms for normal fixtures;\r\n* no unbounded memory growth during large-file streaming;\r\n* no whole-package buffering above a documented threshold;\r\n* cancellation cleanup under 1 second where OS file handling permits;\r\n* no orphaned staging directory after normal failure;\r\n* no duplicate private objects;\r\n* bounded archive entry count;\r\n* bounded decompression ratio;\r\n* bounded JSON depth;\r\n* bounded import retry count.\r\n\r\nDo not weaken integrity checks to meet timing.\r\n\r\nFor expensive media inspection, return a durable status or documented limitation rather than pretending synchronous validation is free.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 32. Concurrency Plan\r\n\r\nAfter the design record and central interfaces are frozen, work may proceed in controlled lanes.\r\n\r\n## Lane A: Package and cryptography\r\n\r\nOwns:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nsrc/private-content/package/\r\npackage-format unit tests\r\nencryption tests\r\narchive-safety tests\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Lane B: Storage and authorized delivery\r\n\r\nOwns:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nsrc/private-content/storage/\r\nprivate asset routes\r\nasset authorization tests\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Lane C: Import/export domain\r\n\r\nOwns:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nsrc/private-content/import/\r\nsrc/private-content/export/\r\nCLI scripts\r\nimport/export tests\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Lane D: Studio interface\r\n\r\nOwns:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nprivate-content Studio screens\r\nwizard tests\r\nlanguage integration\r\naccessibility tests\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Lane E: Security and operations\r\n\r\nOwns:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nrepository scan\r\nbuild-output scan\r\nbackup tooling\r\noperations documentation\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Serialized ownership\r\n\r\nOne architecture owner controls:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nsrc/private-content/core/\r\nshared package types\r\nmanifest schema\r\ncompatibility registry\r\nredaction policy\r\n```\r\n\r\nOne database owner controls:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nprisma/schema.prisma\r\nmigrations\r\ndatabase generation\r\ndatabase-isolated tests\r\n```\r\n\r\nOne authorization owner controls shared:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nauthentication helpers\r\nCreator authorization\r\nPlayer asset authorization\r\nvisibility projection\r\n```\r\n\r\nOne validation owner controls:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ntest server\r\nports\r\ndatabase\r\nasset roots\r\nPlaywright\r\nfull validation\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not allow multiple lanes to edit the same high-conflict file concurrently.\r\n\r\nSubagents must return:\r\n\r\n* findings;\r\n* proposed patch;\r\n* test evidence;\r\n* files touched;\r\n* unresolved risks.\r\n\r\nThey must not independently commit shared architecture.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 33. Required Implementation Sequence\r\n\r\nFollow this order.\r\n\r\n## Step 1: Repository and privacy audit\r\n\r\n* record current state;\r\n* identify concurrent work;\r\n* inspect current asset and Tale architecture;\r\n* scan for possible private material;\r\n* document findings without quoting content.\r\n\r\n## Step 2: Design freeze\r\n\r\nCreate and complete:\r\n\r\n* design record;\r\n* Environment Variables\r\n\r\nAdd safe documented variables as required, using names consistent with the repository.\r\n\r\nPotential examples:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPRIVATE_CONTENT_ROOT=\r\nPRIVATE_CONTENT_STAGING_ROOT=\r\nPRIVATE_CONTENT_MAX_PACKAGE_BYTES=\r\nPRIVATE_CONTENT_MAX_PLAINTEXT_BYTES=\r\nPRIVATE_CONTENT_MAX_ASSET_BYTES=\r\nPRIVATE_CONTENT_MAX_FILE_COUNT=\r\nPRIVATE_CONTENT_ALLOWED_MODEL_TYPES=\r\nPRIVATE_CONTENT_IMPORT_ENABLED=\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not add:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nNEXT_PUBLIC_PRIVATE_CONTENT_KEY\r\nNEXT_PUBLIC_PRIVATE_CONTENT_ROOT\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not put a real key in `.env.example`.\r\n\r\nUse placeholders and explain secure configuration.\r\n\r\nFail startup or disable private operations safely when required roots are invalid.\r\n\r\nDo not silently fall back to a public directory.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 35. Exact Acceptance Criteria\r\n\r\nPhase 1 is complete only when every requirement below is satisfied.\r\n\r\n## 35.1 Repository boundary\r\n\r\n* The public repository contains no real Chronicle content.\r\n* Private exports are ignored and actively detected.\r\n* Private assets cannot be configured under `public/`.\r\n* Decrypted staging cannot be configured inside the repository.\r\n* Repository scan completes without exposing matched values.\r\n* Synthetic fixtures are clearly nonpersonal.\r\n\r\n## 35.2 Package security\r\n\r\n* Every package payload is authenticated and encrypted.\r\n* Wrong key fails closed.\r\n* Tampering fails closed.\r\n* Unsupported versions fail closed.\r\n* Checksums cover all payload files.\r\n* Package verification occurs before import.\r\n* Package export is round-trip verified.\r\n* Keys are never logged or persisted.\r\n* Temporary plaintext is cleaned.\r\n\r\n## 35.3 Archive safety\r\n\r\n* Path traversal is rejected.\r\n* Absolute paths are rejected.\r\n* links are rejected.\r\n* duplicate normalized paths are rejected.\r\n* decompression limits are enforced.\r\n* executable content is rejected.\r\n* GLB or other future model assets are constrained to approved formats.\r\n* untrusted filenames never become storage paths.\r\n\r\n## 35.4 Import safety\r\n\r\n* Dry-run creates no domain state.\r\n* Import requires explicit confirmation.\r\n* Import does not publish.\r\n* Import does not launch a session.\r\n* Import does not invite Players.\r\n* Existing published versions are never overwritten.\r\n* Re-import is idempotent.\r\n* Asset hash reuse is deterministic.\r\n* Conflict behavior is explicit.\r\n* Database failure cleans staging.\r\n* Finalization failure does not expose broken content.\r\n* Retry does not duplicate data.\r\n\r\n## 35.5 Asset protection\r\n\r\n* Private assets are outside public static storage.\r\n* Anonymous access fails.\r\n* unauthorized Player access fails.\r\n* unrevealed artifact access fails.\r\n* Creator draft access is authorized.\r\n* Player-visible published assets work only when allowed.\r\n* thumbnails inherit source protection.\r\n* storage paths are not exposed.\r\n* revoked access stops working.\r\n* guessed IDs do not disclose existence.\r\n\r\n## 35.6 Publishing isolation\r\n\r\n* Imported drafts remain private.\r\n* Published versions remain immutable.\r\n* active playthroughs remain version-pinned.\r\n* later imports do not mutate live sessions.\r\n* Player APIs exclude unpublished content.\r\n* Player APIs exclude answers and Creator notes.\r\n* package metadata is not projected to Players.\r\n\r\n## 35.7 Operations\r\n\r\n* Secure backup can be created.\r\n* Backup can be verified.\r\n* Isolated restore succeeds.\r\n* Tampered backup fails.\r\n* database and asset mismatch is detected.\r\n* recovery procedure is documented.\r\n* package and backup hashes are reported.\r\n\r\n## 35.8 UI and accessibility\r\n\r\n* Only authorized Creator/Admin users see private-content administration.\r\n* Import requires explicit confirmation.\r\n* Wrong-key errors are safe.\r\n* keyboard-only operation works.\r\n* focus is restored.\r\n* progress is announced.\r\n* reduced motion works.\r\n* secret values are not persisted in browser storage.\r\n* secret values are not announced.\r\n\r\n## 35.9 Build and CI\r\n\r\n* Production build succeeds.\r\n* secret scan succeeds.\r\n* private-path scan succeeds.\r\n* synthetic sentinel is absent from public bundles.\r\n* synthetic sentinel is absent from unauthorized responses.\r\n* no private fixture enters public static output.\r\n* full validation passes or every blocker is documented accurately.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 36. Required Validation Commands\r\n\r\nUse repository-supported commands.\r\n\r\nAt minimum run the current equivalents of:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nnpm run format:check\r\nnpm run lint\r\nnpm run typecheck\r\nnpm test\r\nnpm run build\r\nnpm run private-content:scan\r\nnpm run private-content:verify-fixtures\r\nnpm run private-content:build-scan\r\n```\r\n\r\nRun focused tests while developing.\r\n\r\nRun database-mutating and browser tests only after isolation proof.\r\n\r\nOnly one validation owner may run the final full gate.\r\n\r\nIf the repository already uses:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nnpm run validate\r\n```\r\n\r\nrun it once after focused work is green.\r\n\r\nDo not rerun massive suites repeatedly without diagnosing failure.\r\n\r\nClassify every result as:\r\n\r\n* passed;\r\n* task regression;\r\n* known baseline failure;\r\n* environment failure;\r\n* skipped;\r\n* blocked.\r\n\r\nA skipped test is not a pass.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 37. Implementation Manifest\r\n\r\nCreate:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Sealed_Hold_Phase_1_Implementation_Manifest.txt\r\n```\r\n\r\nInclude:\r\n\r\n* repository;\r\n* branch;\r\n* starting commit;\r\n* ending commit before final report update;\r\n* package-format version;\r\n* migration identifiers;\r\n* files added;\r\n* files modified;\r\n* files deleted;\r\n* tests added;\r\n* documentation added;\r\n* validation commands;\r\n* known limitations.\r\n\r\nGenerate and report the SHA-256 of the completed manifest.\r\n\r\nDo not include:\r\n\r\n* passphrases;\r\n* private asset paths;\r\n* private prose;\r\n* secrets;\r\n* decrypted filenames.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 38. Mandatory Final Report\r\n\r\nCreate:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Sealed_Hold_Phase_1_Completion_Report.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe final Codex response must also summarize the following.\r\n\r\n## Repository state\r\n\r\n* repository;\r\n* branch;\r\n* starting commit;\r\n* ending commit;\r\n* upstream;\r\n* ahead/behind;\r\n* worktree;\r\n* concurrent work preserved;\r\n* exact files changed.\r\n\r\n## Architecture\r\n\r\n* final package extension;\r\n* envelope version;\r\n* payload version;\r\n* cipher;\r\n* KDF;\r\n* package manifest type;\r\n* storage interface;\r\n* import status model;\r\n* import receipt;\r\n* export receipt;\r\n* compatibility registry;\r\n* redaction policy;\r\n* asset-delivery design.\r\n\r\n## Implementation totals\r\n\r\n* routes added;\r\n* services added;\r\n* package schemas;\r\n* Prisma models added or reused;\r\n* migrations;\r\n* CLI commands;\r\n* Studio screens;\r\n* tests;\r\n* synthetic fixtures;\r\n* documentation files.\r\n\r\n## Security proof\r\n\r\nProvide safe evidence for:\r\n\r\n* wrong-key failure;\r\n* tamper failure;\r\n* traversal rejection;\r\n* decompression-limit rejection;\r\n* repository export refusal;\r\n* dry-run no-mutation;\r\n* import idempotency;\r\n* rollback cleanup;\r\n* no automatic publishing;\r\n* no automatic invitation;\r\n* unauthorized asset denial;\r\n* unrevealed artifact denial;\r\n* synthetic sentinel absent from public build;\r\n* passphrase absent from logs;\r\n* temporary plaintext cleanup.\r\n\r\n## Backup proof\r\n\r\nProvide:\r\n\r\n* backup package SHA-256;\r\n* verification result;\r\n* isolated restore result;\r\n* restored asset counts;\r\n* mismatch-detection result;\r\n* cleanup result.\r\n\r\nUse only synthetic test data.\r\n\r\n## Validation\r\n\r\nFor every command report:\r\n\r\n* exact command;\r\n* exit status;\r\n* passed count;\r\n* failed count;\r\n* skipped count;\r\n* duration where available;\r\n* database identity;\r\n* test asset-root identity;\r\n* environment blockers;\r\n* baseline failures;\r\n* task regressions.\r\n\r\n## Remaining limitations\r\n\r\nExplicitly list what Phase 1 does not solve, including:\r\n\r\n* full 3D artifact renderer;\r\n* cinematic artifact reveal system;\r\n* artifact assembly;\r\n* external KMS;\r\n* distributed object storage if not implemented;\r\n* antivirus service;\r\n* media transcoding;\r\n* complete production backup automation;\r\n* public marketplace;\r\n* cross-installation Tale sharing;\r\n* history remediation for any preexisting leak;\r\n* full multi-tenant isolation.\r\n\r\n## Synchronization\r\n\r\nRun repository-required:\r\n\r\n* Codex chat archive synchronization;\r\n* Development_Docs synchronization;\r\n* implementation-manifest verification;\r\n* project-status reconciliation if that system already exists.\r\n\r\nReport exact synchronized files.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 39. Commit and Push Requirements\r\n\r\nAfter successful validation:\r\n\r\n1. Review `git diff`.\r\n2. Confirm no private content is present.\r\n3. Run the private-content scanner against the exact staged diff.\r\n4. Stage only intended files.\r\n5. Create the implementation manifest.\r\n6. Hash the manifest.\r\n7. Commit with a clear message such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nfeat(private-content): establish sealed Chronicle package architecture\r\n```\r\n\r\n8. Push the dedicated branch.\r\n9. Report branch and commit hash.\r\n10. Do not merge automatically.\r\n11. Do not delete the worktree.\r\n12. Stop for review.\r\n\r\nIf validation has unresolved task regressions:\r\n\r\n* do not claim completion;\r\n* do not merge;\r\n* clearly report partial implementation;\r\n* preserve all work;\r\n* push only if repository policy permits a reviewable incomplete branch and label it accurately.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 40. Phase Completion Gate\r\n\r\nDo not declare **Lock the Chronicle** complete unless:\r\n\r\n1. Private Chronicle content can live entirely outside Git.\r\n2. Encrypted packages are authenticated and versioned.\r\n3. Wrong keys and tampering fail closed.\r\n4. Package archives are protected against traversal and resource abuse.\r\n5. Private assets are stored outside public static paths.\r\n6. Private assets require authorization.\r\n7. Dry-run import produces no Tale or asset mutation.\r\n8. Import requires explicit confirmation.\r\n9. Import does not publish, launch or invite.\r\n10. Re-import is idempotent.\r\n11. Partial failure cannot expose or duplicate content.\r\n12. Export performs complete referential closure.\r\n13. Export is encrypted and round-trip verified.\r\n14. Keys and plaintext are absent from logs.\r\n15. Temporary plaintext is cleaned.\r\n16. Repository and build scans prevent accidental leaks.\r\n17. Synthetic private sentinel is absent from public output.\r\n18. Backup verification and isolated restore are demonstrated.\r\n19. Studio operations are Creator-authorized and accessible.\r\n20. Current generic content and existing Player behavior remain intact.\r\n21. Future GLB assets can be carried safely without falsely claiming a completed 3D system.\r\n22. Documentation matches implementation.\r\n23. The implementation manifest is hashed.\r\n24. The dedicated branch is committed and pushed.\r\n25. Concurrent work is preserved.\r\n26. No real private content was introduced during implementation.\r\n\r\nDo not weaken encryption to simplify testing.\r\n\r\nDo not rely solely on `.gitignore`.\r\n\r\nDo not store private assets under `public/`.\r\n\r\nDo not place a passphrase in a command-line example.\r\n\r\nDo not treat an encrypted package as valid until authentication, schema validation and checksum validation all succeed.\r\n\r\nDo not treat successful database insertion as a completed import until private assets are finalized and verified.\r\n\r\nDo not allow an imported Tale to become visible merely because its records exist.\r\n\r\nDo not begin the full 2D/3D artifact implementation automatically.\r\n\r\nComplete Phase 1, validate it, document it, commit it to the dedicated branch, push it and stop for review.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

TALL_TALE_ASSET_ROOT=\"D:\\ForeverTreasureAssets\"\r\nREADME.md:7:The application is now a unified Tall Tale platform. The cinematic gateway opens distinct Player, Captain, and Creator workspaces without treating role choice as authorization. Players have durable identities, invitation acceptance, a multi-voyage library, live waiting/runtime state, and exact-version archives. Captains can create version-pinned crew voyages, deliver individually hashed link/QR/code/PIN invitations, manage invitation lifecycle, preview the Player-safe surface, and launch accepted crews. Creator Studio retains immutable publishing and now adds structured version comparison, restore-to-draft, and fork provenance.\r\nREADME.md:42:- Captain: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/captain`\r\nREADME.md:43:- GM development[REDACTED: LOGIN CREDENTIAL]\r\nREADME.md:83:Full setup, validation stages, output locations, clean-clone instructions, and troubleshooting are in [local development](docs/local-development.md) and [testing](docs/testing.md). Unified identity, invitations, libraries, authorization, history, and migration are in [Tall Tale Platform](docs/tall-tale-platform.md); Studio architecture and operations are in [Tall Tale Studio](docs/tall-tale-studio.md). The future recognition seam is in [vision helper boundary](docs/future-vision-helper.md).
