Do not reintroduce or track them.\nAGENTS.md\nPLANS.md\n\r\n--- SECURITY EXISTS ---\r\nFalse\r\n--- README HEAD ---\r\n# The Forever Treasure Companion\r\n\r\nProduction-oriented foundation and fully automated local demo for a private nautical-fantasy date-night Tall Tale. All committed story material is generic development seed content; no final surprise content belongs in this public repository.\r\n\r\n## Current phase\r\n\r\nThe application is now a unified Tall Tale platform. The cinematic gateway opens distinct Player, Captain, and Creator workspaces without treating role choice as authorization. Players have durable identities, invitation acceptance, a multi-voyage library, live waiting/runtime state, and exact-version archives. Captains can create version-pinned crew voyages, deliver individually hashed link/QR/code/PIN invitations, manage invitation lifecycle, preview the Player-safe surface, and launch accepted crews. Creator Studio retains immutable publishing and now adds structured version comparison, restore-to-draft, and fork provenance.\r\n\r\nThe original companion and expanded Game Master Command Center remain integrated compatibility surfaces. A shared animation director coordinates the harbor, journal, chart, artifact, gateway, and finale experiences without allowing presentation timing to outrun server truth. All committed content remains fictional development seed material.\r\n\r\n## Stack\r\n\r\n- Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, strict TypeScript\r\n- Prisma 6.19.3 with SQLite for local/test and an equivalent MySQL production schema\r\n- GSAP 3.15 for orchestrated scenes and SVG/text work; Motion 12 for React interaction and presence\r\n- StPageFlip for the journal; local Rive and Lottie runtimes with explicit static fallbacks\r\n- bcrypt password/access-code hashing, server-side database sessions, CSRF tokens, and rate limiting\r\n- Vitest and Playwright\r\n\r\n## Reproducible setup\r\n\r\nRequirements: Windows PowerShell 5.1+, Node.js 24, npm 11, and Git. MySQL 8 is only required for production parity; the supported local/demo/test strategy is SQLite.\r\n\r\n### Fastest Windows demo\r\n\r\nAfter cloning, double-click `Start Forever Treasure Dev.cmd`, or run:\r\n\r\n```powershell\r\nnpm run dev:full\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe launcher creates an ignored `.env` when absent, installs the exact lockfile, generates Prisma, applies migrations, creates generic development data only when the voyage is missing, starts the app, checks health, and prints the URLs and credentials. Existing campaign progress is preserved across normal stop/start cycles. Network/UNC workspaces are mirrored to an ignored local runtime under `%LOCALAPPDATA%` because Node and SQLite are unreliable on network shares. Stop it with `npm run dev:stop`.\r\n\r\n- Role gateway: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/`\r\n- Player: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tale/development-forever-treasure`\r\n- Player library: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/player`\r\n- Tall Tale catalog: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tales`\r\n- Player phrase: `development-moonwake`\r\n- Returning Player development login: `sera` / the configured `PLAYER_PASSWORD` (or `PLAYER_ACCESS_CODE` fallback)\r\n- GM: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/quartermaster`\r\n- Studio: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/studio`\r\n- Captain: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/captain`\r\n- GM development[REDACTED: LOGIN CREDENTIAL]\r\n\r\nThese values are disposable local defaults, never production credentials. To opt into LAN access, run `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Lan`; Windows Firewall and network policy still determine whether another device can connect.\r\n\r\n### Portable manual setup\r\n\r\n```bash\r\ngit clone https://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT.git\r\ncd treasurehuntSoT\r\nnpm ci\r\ncp .env.example .env\r\nnpm run db:generate\r\nnpm run db:migrate\r\nnpm run db:seed\r\nnpm run dev\r\n```\r\n\r\nSet unique local values in `.env`; the seedâ€™s fallback values are development-only and must never be used outside an isolated development database. Player path: `/tale/development-forever-treasure`. GM path: `/quartermaster`.\r\n\r\n## Commands\r\n\r\n| Command                                   | Purpose                                                               |\r\n| ----------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |\r\n| `npm run dev:full`                        | Idempotent setup, migration, progress-safe seed check, and server     |\r\n| `npm run dev:stop`                        | Safely stop only the recorded development process                     |\r\n| `npm run validate`                        | Fresh DB, format/lint/types/unit/E2E/a11y/build/restart proof         |\r\n| `npm run dev`                             | Next.js only; assumes setup is already complete                       |\r\n| `npm run build`                           | Production build                                                      |\r\n| `npm start`                               | Start production build                                                |\r\n| `npm test`                                | Domain, command-center, Studio, animation, and component unit tests   |\r\n| `npm run assets:validate`                 | Validate local Lottie/Rive contracts and fallback paths               |\r\n| `npm run test:e2e`                        | Cross-browser player, Command Center, Studio, and animation workflows |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate`                      | Apply local SQLite migrations                                         |\r\n| `npm run db:seed`                         | Load replaceable development content                                  |\r\n| `npm run db:preset -- mid-voyage`         | Reset to a repeatable development-only companion preset               |\r\n| `npm run db:generate:mysql`               | Generate production MySQL client                                      |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:command-center` | Apply the Phase 3 production MySQL migration                          |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:studio`         | Apply the production-parity Tall Tale Studio migration                |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:platform`       | Apply the unified Tall Tale Platform migration                        |\r\n\r\nFull setup, validation stages, output locations, clean-clone instructions, and troubleshooting are in [local development](docs/local-development.md) and [testing](docs/testing.md). Unified identity, invitations, libraries, authorization, history, and migration are in [Tall Tale Platform](docs/tall-tale-platform.md); Studio architecture and operations are in [Tall Tale Studio](docs/tall-tale-studio.md). The future recognition seam is in [vision helper boundary](docs/future-vision-helper.md). Animation ownership, scene contracts, assets, performance, and the development lab are indexed from [animation architecture](docs/animation/architecture.md).\r\n\r\n## Repository workflow\r\n\r\n`main` is canonical. At task start: fetch, inspect status/remotes, and pull with rebase when clean. At task end: validate, review the complete diff, commit intentionally, fetch/rebase if needed, push without force, and verify local `HEAD` equals `origin/main`. See [Codex handoff](docs/codex-handoff.md).\r\n\r\n## Important limitations\r\n\r\nThe full story, final Rive artwork/audio, horizontal scale-out for SSE, and production deployment are later work. The current sound is restrained and procedural. The sole third-party visual sample is MIT-licensed, local, documented, and development-only; production uses original SVG/CSS fallbacks. The repository is public, so only generic development content is permitted. See [known limitations](docs/known-limitations.md).\r\n\r\n## Next steps\r\n\r\nAdd CI for the complete validation gate, exercise the parallel MySQL schema in an integration environment, and replace process-local SSE fan-out before multi-instance deployment. Real surprise content remains blocked until repository visibility is verified private.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

fileSize: item.buffer.length,\n          width: item.width,\n          height: item.height,\n          checksum: item.checksum,\n        },\n      });\n      if (item.role === \"OPTIMIZED\") tx.taleAsset.findUniqueOrThrow({\n      where: { id: record.id },\n      include: { variants: { orderBy: { createdAt: \"desc\" } }, tags: { include: { tag: true } }, roles: true },\n    });\n  });\n  logger.info(\n    {\n      area: \"tall-tale-assets\",\n      taleId,\n      assetId: asset.id,\n      mediaType: asset.mediaType,\n      replacement: Boolean(replaceAssetId),\n    },\n    \"Chronicle asset ingested\",\n  );\n  return { asset, duplicate: false };\n}\n\nexport async function assetUsages(taleId: string, assetId: string) {\n  const studio = await getStudioTale(taleId);\n  const usages: Array<{ type: string; id: string; label: string; field?: string }> = [];\n  if (studio.tale.coverAssetId === assetId)\n    usages.push({ type: \"tale\", id: taleId, label: \"Chronicle cover\", field: \"coverAssetId\" });\n  for (const chapter of studio.draft.chapters) {\n    if (chapter.coverAssetId === assetId)\n      usages.push({ type: \"chapter\", id: chapter.id, label: `${chapter.title} cover`, field: \"coverAssetId\" });\n    for (const block of chapter.blocks)\n      for (const [field, value] of Object.entries(block.configuration))\n        if (value === assetId)   if (location[field] === assetId) usages.push({ type: \"location\", id: location.id, label: location.name, field });\n  for (const artifact of studio.artifacts)\n    for (const field of [\"artworkAssetId\", \"revealVideoAssetId\", \"modelAssetId\"] as const)\n      if (artifact[field] === assetId) usages.push({ type: \"artifact\", id: artifact.id, label: artifact.name, field });\n  return usages;\n}\n\nexport async function updateAsset(\n  assetId: string,\n  input: { displayName?: string; description?: string; tags?: string[]; roles?: string[]; collectionIds?: string[] },\n) {\n  const asset = await db.taleAsset.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: assetId } });\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n    await tx.taleAsset.update({\n      where: { id: assetId },\n requireGmCapability(capability: GmCapability) {\n  const session = await requireGm();\n  return session && gmCan(session.user, capability) ? session : null;\n}\n\nexport async function clearGmSession() {\n  const jar = await cookies();\n  const raw = jar.get(GM_COOKIE)?.value;\n  if (raw) await db.gameMasterSession.deleteMany({ where: { id: hashToken(raw) } });\n (!/^[a-f0-9-]+\\/[a-f0-9-]+\\.[a-z0-9]+$/.test(storageKey)) throw new Error(\"The requested asset cannot be opened.\");\r\n  const root = storageRoot();\r\n  const resolved = path.join(/* turbopackIgnore: true */ root, storageKey);\r\n  if (!resolved.startsWith(`${root}${path.sep}`)) throw new Error(\"The requested asset cannot be opened.\");\r\n  return resolved;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport interface TaleAssetStorage {\r\n  write(storageKey: string, buffer: Buffer): Promise<void>;\r\n  read(storageKey: string): Promise<Buffer>;\r\n}\r\n\r\nclass LocalTaleAssetStorage implements TaleAssetStorage {\r\n  async write(storageKey: string, buffer: Buffer) {\r\n    const target = keyPath(storageKey);\r\n    await mkdir(/* turbopackIgnore: true */ path.dirname(target), { recursive: true });\r\n    await writeFile(/* turbopackIgnore: true */ target, buffer, { flag: \"wx\" });\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  read(storageKey: string) {\r\n    return readFile(/* turbopackIgnore: true */ keyPath(storageKey));\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nconst assetStorage: TaleAssetStorage = new LocalTaleAssetStorage();\r\n\r\nfunction hasValidSignature(buffer: Buffer, mimeType: string) {\r\n  if (mimeType === \"image/png\") return buffer.subarray(0, 8).equals(Buffer.from([137, 80, 78, 71, 13, 10, 26, 10]));\r\n  if (mimeType === \"image/jpeg\") return buffer[0] === 0xff && buffer[1] === 0xd8;\r\n  if (mimeType === \"image/webp\")\r\n    return buffer.subarray(0, 4).toString() === \"RIFF\" && buffer.subarray(8, 12).toString() === \"WEBP\";\r\n  if (mimeType === \"image/avif\") return buffer.subarray(4, 12).toString().includes(\"ftyp\");\r\n  if (mimeType === \"application/pdf\") return buffer.subarray(0, 5).toString() === \"%PDF-\";\r\n  if (mimeType === \"video/mp4\") return buffer.subarray(4, 12).toString().includes(\"ftyp\");\r\n  if (mimeType === \"video/webm\" || mimeType === \"audio/webm\")\r\n    return buffer.subarray(0, 4).equals(Buffer.from([0x1a, 0x45, 0xdf, 0xa3]));\r\n  if (mimeType === \"audio/mpeg\")\r\n    return buffer.subarray(0, 3).toString() === \"ID3\" || (buffer[0] === 0xff && (buffer[1] & 0xe0) === 0xe0);\r\n  if (mimeType === \"audio/ogg\") return buffer.subarray(0, 4).toString() === \"OggS\";\r\n  if (mimeType === \"audio/wav\")\r\n    return buffer.subarray(0, 4).toString() === \"RIFF\" && buffer.subarray(8, 12).toString() === \"WAVE\";\r\n  return false;\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function store(storageKey: string, buffer: Buffer) {\r\n  await assetStorage.write(storageKey, buffer);\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function derivativeSet(buffer: Buffer, folder: string) {\r\n  const metadata = await sharp(buffer).metadata();\r\n  const definitions = [\r\n    { role: \"THUMBNAIL\", width: 360, quality: 72 },\r\n    { role: \"PREVIEW\", width: 960, quality: 78 },\r\n    { role: \"OPTIMIZED\", width: 1800, quality: 84 },\r\n    { role: \"MOBILE\", width: 900, quality: 80 },\r\n  ];\r\n  const items: Array<{\r\n    role: string;\r\n    storageKey: string;\r\n    mimeType: string;\r\n    buffer: Buffer;\r\n    width: number | null;\r\n    height: number | null;\r\n    checksum: string;\r\n  }> = [];\r\n  for (const definition of definitions) {\r\n    const output = await sharp(buffer)\r\n      .rotate()\r\n      .resize({ width: definition.width, withoutEnlargement: true })\r\n      .webp({ quality: definition.quality })\r\n      .toBuffer({ resolveWithObject: true });\r\n    const storageKey = `${folder}/${randomUUID()}.webp`;\r\n    items.push({\r\n      role: definition.role,\r\n      storageKey,\r\n      mimeType: \"image/webp\",\r\n      buffer: output.data,\r\n      width: output.info.width,\r\n      height: output.info.height,\r\n      checksum: createHash(\"sha256\").update(output.data).digest(\"hex\"),\r\n    });\r\n  }\r\n  return { width: metadata.width ?? null, height: metadata.height ?? null, items };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function ingestAsset(taleId: string, file: File, userId: string, replaceAssetId?: string) {\r\n  const definition = supported.get(file.type);\r\n  if (!definition)\r\n    throw new Error(\r\n      \"This file type cannot be uploaded. Choose PNG, JPEG, WebP, AVIF, MP4, WebM, MP3, Ogg, WAV, or PDF.\",\r\n    );\r\n  const maximum = Number(process.env.TALL_TALE_MAX_UPLOAD_MB ?? 25) * 1024 * 1024;\r\n  if (!file.size || file.size > maximum)\r\n    throw new Error(`Choose a file smaller than ${Math.round(maximum / 1024 / 1024)} MB.`);\r\n  const buffer = Buffer.from(await file.arrayBuffer());\r\n  if (!hasValidSignature(buffer, file.type))\r\n    throw new Error(\r\n      \"The file content does not match its file type. Choose the file again or export it in a supported format.\",\r\n    );\r\n  const checksum = createHash(\"sha256\").update(buffer).digest(\"hex\");\r\n  if (!replaceAssetId) {\r\n    const duplicate = await db.taleAsset.findUnique({\r\n      where: { taleId_checksum: { taleId, checksum } },\r\n      include: { variants: true, tags: { include: { tag: true } }, roles: true },\r\n    });\r\n    if (duplicate) {\r\n      logger.info(\r\n        { area: \"tall-tale-assets\", taleId, assetId: duplicate.id },\r\n        \"Duplicate upload reused existing asset\",\r\n      );\r\n      return { asset: duplicate, duplicate: true };\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n  const folder = randomUUID();\r\n  const originalKey = `${folder}/${randomUUID()}${definition.extension}`;\r\n  await store(originalKey, buffer);\r\n  let width: number | null = null;\r\n  let height: number | null = null;\r\n  let derivatives: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof derivativeSet>>[\"items\"] = [];\r\n  if (definition.mediaType === \"IMAGE\") {\r\n    const processed = await derivativeSet(buffer, folder);\r\n    width = processed.width;\r\n    height = processed.height;\r\n    derivatives = processed.items;\r\n    for (const item of derivatives) await store(item.storageKey, item.buffer);\r\n  }\r\n  const asset = await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const record = replaceAssetId\r\n      ? await tx.taleAsset.update({\r\n          where: { id: replaceAssetId },\r\n          data: {\r\n            displayName: file.name.replace(/\\.[^.]+$/, \"\"),\r\n            originalFilename: file.name,\r\n            mimeType: file.type,\r\n            fileSize: file.size,\r\n            width,\r\n            height,\r\n            checksum,\r\n            mediaType: definition.mediaType,\r\n            deletedAt: null,\r\n          },\r\n        })\r\n      : await tx.taleAsset.create({\r\n          data: {\r\n            taleId,\r\n            mediaType: definition.mediaType,\r\n            displayName: file.name.replace(/\\.[^.]+$/, \"\"),\r\n            originalFilename: file.name,\r\n            mimeType: file.type,\r\n            fileSize: file.size,\r\n            width,\r\n            height,\r\n            checksum,\r\n            createdBy: userId,\r\n          },\r\n        });\r\n    const original = await tx.taleAssetVariant.create({\r\n      data: {\r\n        assetId: record.id,\r\n        role: \"ORIGINAL\",\r\n        storageKey: originalKey,\r\n        mimeType: file.type,\r\n        fileSize: file.size,\r\n        width,\r\n        height,\r\n        checksum,\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    let optimizedVariantId: string | null = null;\r\n    for (const item of derivatives) {\r\n      const derivative = await tx.taleAssetVariant.create({\r\n        data: {\r\n          assetId: record.id,\r\n          role: item.role,\r\n          storageKey: item.storageKey,\r\n          mimeType: item.mimeType,\r\n          fileSize: item.buffer.length,\r\n          width: item.width,\r\n          height: item.height,\r\n          checksum: item.checksum,\r\n        },\r\n      });\r\n      if (item.role === \"OPTIMIZED\") optimizedVariantId = derivative.id;\r\n    }\r\n    await tx.taleAsset.update({\r\n      where: { id: record.id },\r\n      data: { currentVariantId: optimizedVariantId ?? original.id },\r\n    });\r\n--- MEDIA ROUTE ---\r\nimport { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport { requireGmCapability } from \"@/lib/security\";\nimport { resolveAssetVariant } from \"@/tall-tale/assets\";\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { authorizeTaleSessionPlayer, pendingInvitationMatches } from \"@/platform/auth\";\nimport { playerSafeAssetIds } from \"@/platform/libraries\";\nimport { parsePublishedSnapshot } from \"@/tall-tale/publishing\";\n\nexport async function GET(request: Request, context: { params: Promise<{ assetId: string }> }) {\n  try {\n    const url = new URL(request.url);\n    const version = url.searchParams.get(\"version\") ?? undefined;\n    const assetId = (await context.params).assetId;\n    const download = url.searchParams.get(\"download\") === \"1\";\n    const creator = await requireGmCapability(download ? \"MANAGE_ASSETS\" : \"CREATE_TALES\");\n    if (!version || version.startsWith(\"draft:\")) {\n      if (!creator) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Asset not found.\" }, { status: 404 });\n    } else if (!creator) {\n      let authorized = false;\n      const sessionId = url.searchParams.get(\"session\");\n      const invitationId = url.searchParams.get(\"invitation\");\n      if (sessionId && (await authorizeTaleSessionPlayer(sessionId))) {\n        const playthrough = await db.taleSession.findFirst({\n          where: { id: sessionId, publishedVersionId: version },\n          include: { version: true, events: { select: { blockId: true } } },\n        });\n        if (playthrough?.version)\n          authorized = playerSafeAssetIds(\n            playthrough.version.contentSnapshot,\n            playthrough.events.map((event) => event.blockId),\n            playthrough.inventory,\n          ).has(assetId);\n      } else if (invitationId && (await pendingInvitationMatches(invitationId))) {\n        const invitation = await db.invitation.findFirst({\n          where: { id: invitationId, playthrough: { publishedVersionId: version } },\n          include: { playthrough: { include: { version: true } } },\n        });\n        if (invitation?.playthrough.version) {\n          const snapshot = parsePublishedSnapshot(invitation.playthrough.version.contentSnapshot);\n          authorized = snapshot.tale.coverAssetId === assetId;\n        }\n      } else if (url.searchParams.get(\"public\") === \"cover\") {\n        authorized = Boolean(\n          await db.tallTale.findFirst({\n            where: {\n              coverAssetId: assetId,\n              status: \"PUBLISHED\",\n              visibility: \"PUBLIC\",\n              latestPublishedVersionId: version,\n              archivedAt: null,\n            },\n            select: { id: true },\n          }),\n        );\n      }\n      if (!authorized) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Asset not found.\" }, { status: 404 });\n    }\n    const { variant, buffer } = await resolveAssetVariant(\n      assetId,\n      (url.searchParams.get(\"variant\") ?? \"OPTIMIZED\").toUpperCase(),\n      version,\n    );\n    const asset = download\n      ? await db.taleAsset.findUnique({ where: { id: assetId }, select: { originalFilename: true } })\n      : null;\n    const safeFilename = asset?.originalFilename.replace(/[\\r\\n\"\\\\/]/g, \"_\") ?? \"tall-tale-asset\";\n    return new Response(new Uint8Array(buffer), {\n      headers: {\n        \"Content-Type\": variant.mimeType,\n        \"Content-Length\": String(buffer.length),\n        \"Cache-Control\":\n          version && !version.startsWith(\"draft:\") ? \"public, max-age=31536000, immutable\" : \"private, no-store\",\n        \"X-Content-Type-Options\": \"nosniff\",\n        ...(download ? { \"Content-Disposition\": `attachment; filename=\"${safeFilename}\"` } : {}),\n      },\n    });\n  } catch {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Asset not found.\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n}\n\r\n--- STUDIO ASSET ROUTE ---\r\nimport { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport { requireGmCapability, verifyCsrf } from \"@/lib/security\";\nimport { apiError } from \"@/tall-tale/api\";\nimport { archiveAsset, assetUsages, ingestAsset, updateAsset } from \"@/tall-tale/assets\";\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { consumeRateLimit, rateLimitHeaders } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\n\nexport async function GET(_: Request, context: { params: Promise<{ assetId: string }> }) {\n  if (!(await requireGmCapability(\"MANAGE_ASSETS\")))\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"You do not have permission to manage assets in this Chronicle.\" },\n      { status: 403 },\n    );\n  try {\n    const asset = await db.taleAsset.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: (await context.params).assetId } });\n    return NextResponse.json({ usages: await assetUsages(asset.taleId, asset.id) });\n  } catch (cause) {\n    return apiError(cause);\n  }\n}\n\nasync function authorized() {\n  const session = await requireGmCapability(\"MANAGE_ASSETS\");\n  return session && (await verifyCsrf(session)) ? session : null;\n}\n\nexport async function PATCH(request: Request, context: { params: Promise<{ assetId: string }> }) {\n  const session = await authorized();\n  if (!session)\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"You do not have permission to manage assets in this Chronicle, or your creator session has expired.\" },\n      { status: 403 },\n    );\n  try {\n    return NextResponse.json(await updateAsset((await context.params).assetId, await request.json()));\n  } catch (cause) {\n    return apiError(cause);\n  }\n}\n\nexport async function PUT(request: Request, context: { params: Promise<{ assetId: string }> }) {\n  const session = await authorized();\n  if (!session)\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"You do not have permission to manage assets in this Chronicle, or your creator session has expired.\" },\n      { status: 403 },\n    );\n  try {\n    const rate = consumeRateLimit(`tale-upload:${session.userId}`, { limit: 30, windowMs: 15 * 60_000 });\n    if (!rate.allowed)\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"The upload limit was reached. Wait before replacing more media.\" },\n        { status: 429, headers: rateLimitHeaders(rate) },\n      );\n    const { assetId } = await context.params;\n    const asset = await db.taleAsset.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: assetId } });\n    const file = (await request.formData()).get(\"file\");\n    if (!(file instanceof File)) throw new Error(\"Choose a replacement file.\");\n    return NextResponse.json(await ingestAsset(asset.taleId, file, session.userId, assetId));\n  } catch (cause) {\n    return apiError(cause);\n  }\n}\n\nexport async function DELETE(_: Request, context: { params: Promise<{ assetId: string }> }) {\n  const session = await authorized();\n  if (!session)\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"You do not have permission to manage assets in this Chronicle, or your creator session has expired.\" },\n      { status: 403 },\n    );\n  try {\n    const result = await archiveAsset((await context.params).assetId);\n    return NextResponse.json(result, { status: result.archived ? 200 : 409 });\n  } catch (cause) {\n    return apiError(cause);\n  }\n}\n\r\n--- STUDIO LAYOUT ---\r\nimport type { Metadata } from \"next\";\n\nexport const metadata: Metadata = {\n  title: \"Voyagewright Studio\",\n  description: \"Create, validate, preview, publish, and preserve reusable Chronicles.\",\n};\n\nexport default function StudioLayout({ children }: Readonly<{ children: React.ReactNode }>) {\n  return children;\n}\n\r\n--- STUDIO HOME ---\r\n\"use client\";\n\nimport Link from \"next/link\";\nimport { useCallback, useEffect, useMemo, useState } from \"react\";\nimport { AnimatePresence, LayoutGroup, motion } from \"motion/react\";\nimport { useMotionMode } from \"@/animation/motion/useMotionMode\";\nimport { platformMotionEasing, resolvePlatformMotionToken } from \"@/animation/platform/motion-tokens\";\nimport { EmptyState, ErrorState, LoadingState, StatusBanner } from \"@/components/ui/AsyncState\";\nimport { studioCopy } from \"@/language/studio-copy\";\n\ntype TaleCard = {\n  id: string;\n  slug: string;\n  title: string;\n  subtitle: string | null;\n  shortDescription: string | null;\n  status: string;\n  visibility: string;\n  savedAt: string;\n  validationState: string;\n  latestVersion: string | null;\n  sessionCount: number;\n  assetCount: number;\n};\n\nexport function StudioHome({ authenticated }: { authenticated: boolean }) {\n  const { mode } = useMotionMode();\n  const layoutMotion = resolvePlatformMotionToken(\"layout\", mode);\n  const [tales, setTales] = useState<TaleCard[]>([]);\n  const [csrf, setCsrf] = useState(\"\");\n  const [query, setQuery] = useState(\"\");\n  const [sort, setSort] = useState(\"recent\");\n  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n  const [busy, setBusy] = useState(\"\");\n  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n  const [notice, setNotice] = useState(\"\");\n\n  const load = useCallback(async () => {\n    setLoading(true);\n    setError(\"\");\n    try {\n      const response = await fetch(\"/api/studio/tales\", { cache: \"no-store\" });\n      const body = (await response.json()) as { tales?: TaleCard[]; csrfToken?: string; error?: string };\n      if (!response.ok) setError(body.error ?? \"The Studio library could not be opened.\");\n      else {\n        setTales(body.tales ?? []);\n        setCsrf(body.csrfToken ?? \"\");\n      }\n    } catch {\n      setError(\"The Studio library could not be reached. Check your connection and try again.\");\n    } finally {\n      setLoading(false);\n    }\n  }, []);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!authenticated) return;\n    queueMicrotask(() => void load());\n  }, [authenticated, load]);\n\n  const visible = useMemo(() => {\n    const filtered = tales.filter((tale) =>\n      `${tale.title} ${tale.subtitle ?? \"\"} ${tale.shortDescription ?? \"\"}`\n        .toLocaleLowerCase()\n        .includes(query.toLocaleLowerCase()),\n    );\n    return filtered.sort((left, right) =>\n      sort === \"title\"\n        ? left.title.localeCompare(right.title)\n        : sort === \"status\"\n          ? left.status.localeCompare(right.status)\n          : Date.parse(right.savedAt) - Date.parse(left.savedAt),\n    );\n  }, [query, sort, tales]);\n\n  async function act(tale: TaleCard, action: \"duplicate\" | \"archive\" | \"restore\") {\n    if (\n      (action === \"archive\" || action === \"restore\") &&\n      !window.confirm(\n        action === \"archive\"\n          ? `Archive â€œ${tale.title}â€? Published Versions and active Voyages will be preserved.`\n          : `Restore â€œ${tale.title}â€ to the active Chronicle Library?`,\n      )\n    )\n      return;\n    setBusy(tale.id);\n    setError(\"\");\n    setNotice(\"\");\n    try {\n      const response = await fetch(`/api/studio/tales/${tale.id}`, {\n        method: \"POST\",\n        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", \"x-csrf-token\": csrf },\n        body: JSON.stringify({ action }),\n      });\n      const body = (await response.json()) as { error?: string };\n      if (!response.ok) setError(body.error ?? \"The Studio action failed.\");\n      else {\n        setNotice(\n          action === \"duplicate\"\n            ? \"A new editable Chronicle draft was created.\"\n            : action === \"archive\"\n              ? \"The Chronicle was archived. Published Voyages are unchanged.\"\n              : \"The Chronicle was restored to the active Chronicle Library.\",\n        );\n        await load();\n      }\n    } catch {\n      setError(\"The Studio action could not be completed. Check your connection and try again.\");\n    } finally {\n      setBusy(\"\");\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (!authenticated)\n    return (\n      <main className=\"studio-auth-gate\">\n        <section>\n          <p className=\"eyebrow\">{studioCopy.studioName.value}</p>\n          <h1>Creator access is required.</h1>\n          <p>Sign in with a creator account, then return to Voyagewright Studio.</p>\n          <Link className=\"brass-button\" href=\"/quartermaster\">\n            Sign in\n          </Link>\n        </section>\n      </main>\n    );\n\n  return (\n    <main className=\"studio-home\">\n      <header className=\"studio-home-header\">\n        <div>\n          <p className=\"eyebrow\">Chronicle authoring</p>\n          <h1>{studioCopy.studioName.value}</h1>\n          <p>Create, validate, and publish Chronicles from one place.</p>\n        </div>\n        <nav aria-label=\"Studio destinations\">\n          <Link className=\"brass-button\" href=\"/studio/tales/new\">\n            {studioCopy.createChronicle.value}\n          </Link>\n        </nav>\n      </header>\n      <AnimatePresence initial={false}>\n        {notice && (\n          <motion.div\n            key=\"studio-notice\"\n            initial={mode === \"reduced\" ? false : { opacity: 0, y: layoutMotion.distancePx }}\n            animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}\n            exit={mode === \"reduced\" ? { opacity: 0 } : { opacity: 0, y: -layoutMotion.distancePx }}\n            transition={{ duration: layoutMotion.durationSeconds, ease: platformMotionEasing(\"layout\") }}\n          >\n            <StatusBanner tone=\"success\">{notice}</StatusBanner>\n          </motion.div>\n        )}\n        {error && tales.length > 0 && (\n          <motion.div key=\"studio-error\" initial={{ opacity: 0 }} animate={{ opacity: 1 }} exit={{ opacity: 0 }}>\n            <StatusBanner tone=\"danger\">{error}</StatusBanner>\n          </motion.div>\n        )}\n      </AnimatePresence>\n      {!loading && tales.length > 0 && (\n        <section className=\"studio-toolbar\" aria-label=\"Find Chronicles\">\n          <label>\n            <span>Search</span>\n            <input\n              type=\"search\"\n              value={query}\n              onChange={(event) => setQuery(event.target.value)}\n              placeholder=\"Title, subtitle, or description\"\n            />\n          </label>\n          <label>\n            <span>Sort</span>\n            <select value={sort} onChange={(event) => setSort(event.target.value)}>\n              <option value=\"recent\">Last saved</option>\n              <option value=\"title\">Title</option>\n              <option value=\"status\">Status</option>\n            </select>\n          </label>\n          <p aria-live=\"polite\">\n            {visible.length} {visible.length === 1 ? \"Chronicle\" : \"Chronicles\"}\n          </p>\n        </section>\n      )}\n      {loading ? (\n        <LoadingState\n          title=\"Opening the Studio library\"\n          detail=\"Loading Chronicle drafts, published versions, and validation status.\"\n        />\n      ) : error && !tales.length ? (\n        <ErrorState\n          title=\"The Studio library could not be opened\"\n          detail={error}\n          action={{ label: \"Try Again\", onClick: () => void load() }}\n        />\n      ) : !tales.length ? (\n        <EmptyState\n          title=\"No Chronicles yet\"\n          detail=\"Start with a title, premise, and first Chapter.\"\n          action={{ label: studioCopy.createChronicle.value, href: \"/studio/tales/new\" }}\n        />\n      ) : !visible.length ? (\n        <EmptyState\n          title=\"No Chronicles match this search\"\n          detail=\"Clear the search to see every draft and published version.\"\n          action={{ label: \"Clear search\", onClick: () => setQuery(\"\") }}\n          symbol=\"âŒ•\"\n        />\n      ) : (\n        <LayoutGroup id=\"studio-library\">\n          <motion.section className=\"tale-card-grid\" aria-label=\"Chronicles\" layout={mode !== \"reduced\"}>\n            <AnimatePresence initial={false} mode=\"popLayout\">\n              {visible.map((tale) => (\n                <motion.article\n                  className={`tale-studio-card status-${tale.status.toLowerCase()}`}\n                  key={tale.id}\n                  layout={mode !== \"reduced\"}\n                  layoutId={`studio-tale-${tale.id}`}\n                  data-motion-state={busy === tale.id ? \"pending\" : \"settled\"}\n                  initial={mode === \"reduced\" ? false : { opacity: 0, y: layoutMotion.distancePx }}\n                  animate={{ opacity: busy === tale.id ? 0.62 : 1, y: 0 }}\n                  exit={mode === \"reduced\" ? { opacity: 0 } : { opacity: 0, scale: 1 - layoutMotion.scaleDelta }}\n                  transition={{ duration: layoutMotion.durationSeconds, ease: platformMotionEasing(\"layout\") }}\n                >\n                  <div className=\"tale-card-stamp\">{tale.status.replaceAll(\"_\", \" \")}</div>\n                  <p className=\"card-kicker\">\n                    {tale.visibility.toLocaleLowerCase()} Â·{\" \"}\n                    {tale.latestVersion ? `Version ${tale.latestVersion}` : \"Not published\"}\n                  </p>\n                  <h2>{tale.title}</h2>\n                  <p>{tale.subtitle ?? tale.shortDescription ?? \"No description has been added yet.\"}</p>\n                  <dl>\n                    <div>\n                      <dt>Last saved</dt>\n                      <dd>{new Date(tale.savedAt).toLocaleString()}</dd>\n                    </div>\n                    <div>\n                      <dt>Validation</dt>\n                      <dd>{tale.validationState.toLocaleLowerCase()}</dd>\n                    </div>\n                    <div>\n                      <dt>Library</dt>\n                      <dd>{tale.assetCount} assets</dd>\n                    </div>\n                    <div>\n                      <dt>Voyages</dt>\n                      <dd>{tale.sessionCount}</dd>\n                    </div>\n                  </dl>\n                  <div className=\"tale-card-actions\">\n                    <Link className=\"primary\" href={`/studio/tales/${tale.id}`}>\n                      {studioCopy.editChronicle.value}\n                    </Link>\n                    {tale.latestVersion && (\n                      <Link href={`/play/${tale.slug}`} target=\"_blank\">\n                        {studioCopy.previewVoyage.value}\n                      </Link>\n                    )}\n                    <button\n                      disabled={busy === tale.id}\n                      aria-busy={busy === tale.id}\n                      onClick={() => void act(tale, \"duplicate\")}\n                    >\n                      {busy === tale.id ? \"Workingâ€¦\" : \"Duplicate\"}\n                    </button>\n                    <button\n                      className={tale.status === \"ARCHIVED\" ? \"button-secondary\" : \"button-subtle\"}\n                      disabled={busy === tale.id}\n                      onClick={() => void act(tale, tale.status === \"ARCHIVED\" ? \"restore\" : \"archive\")}\n                    >\n                      {tale.status === \"ARCHIVED\" ? \"Restore\" : \"Archive\"}\n                    </button>\n                  </div>\n                </motion.article>\n              ))}\n            </AnimatePresence>\n          </motion.section>\n        </LayoutGroup>\n      )}\n    </main>\n  );\n}\n\r\n--- STUDIO PAGE ---\r\nimport { StudioHome } from \"@/components/studio/StudioHome\";\nimport { requireGmCapability } from \"@/lib/security\";\nexport const dynamic = \"force-dynamic\";\nexport default async function StudioPage() {\n  return <StudioHome authenticated={Boolean(await requireGmCapability(\"CREATE_TALES\"))} />;\n}\n\r\n--- TALE API ---\r\nimport { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport { requireGmCapability, verifyCsrf } from \"@/lib/security\";\nimport { apiError } from \"@/tall-tale/api\";\nimport { createStudioTale, listStudioTales } from \"@/tall-tale/studio-service\";\n\nexport async function GET() {\n  const session = await requireGmCapability(\"CREATE_TALES\");\n  if (!session) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Sign in with a creator account to continue.\" }, { status: 401 });\n  return NextResponse.json({ csrfToken: session.csrfToken, tales: await listStudioTales() });\n}\n\nexport async function POST(request: Request) {\n  const session = await requireGmCapability(\"CREATE_TALES\");\n  if (!session) return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Sign in with a creator account to continue.\" }, { status: 401 });\n  if (!(await verifyCsrf(session)))\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Your creator session has expired. Reload the page and try again.\" },\n      { status: 403 },\n    );\n  try {\n    return NextResponse.json(await createStudioTale({ ...(await request.json()), creatorId: session.userId }), {\n      status: 201,\n    });\n  } catch (cause) {\n    return apiError(cause);\n  }\n}\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

It was not changed. Remote fetch completed; its opportunistic Git maintenance warning did not change `origin/main` or the recorded SHA.\n+\n+## Concurrent Wayfarer discovery\n+\n+- Branch: `codex/project-wayfarer-phase1-unified-identity`\n+- Local worktree: `Forever-Treasure-Wayfarer-Phase-1`\n+- Starting SHA: `0ecd2f9cca6116e2f7f9ab4408ade749fb061e72`\n+- Current SHA at Sealed Hold start: `0ecd2f9cca6116e2f7f9ab4408ade749fb061e72`\n+- Shared base SHA: `0ecd2f9cca6116e2f7f9ab4408ade749fb061e72`\n+\n+Wayfarer had an untracked design record only. It was inspected only through branch/worktree metadata and remains untouched.\n+\n+## Ownership and reconciliation\n+\n+Sealed Hold owns `src/private-content/`, `scripts/private-content/`, `tests/private-content/`, its private Studio route/components, private-content migrations, and `Development_Docs/Project_Sealed_Hold_*` / `Development_Docs/Private_*` records.\n+\n+Likely shared files are `prisma/schema*.prisma`, migration ordering, `src/lib/security.ts`, Studio navigation/styles, `package.json`, `.env.example`, `.gitignore`, `README.md`, and security/deployment documentation. Sealed Hold will use a narrow `PrivateContentAuthorization` adapter instead of copying or redesigning Wayfarer's identity work. It will not alter identity, profiles, memberships, invitations, sessions, or privacy schemas.\n+\n+Deferred to integration: identity adapter source mapping, any migration ordering, lockfile resolution, Studio navigation placement, and universal-language catalog reconciliation. The recommended order is Wayfarer acceptance, Sealed Hold acceptance, then a fresh integration branch from accepted main; merge/validate Wayfarer first and Sealed Hold second, retaining both migrations and re-running combined authorization, migration, build, and leak gates.\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Sealed-Hold-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Project_Sealed_Hold_Phase_1_Design_Record.md\n+# Project Sealed Hold Phase 1 design record\n+\n+## Freeze\n+\n+Phase 1 uses the `.ftprivate` format with a version-1 JSON envelope and an AES-256-GCM encrypted, authenticated payload. The payload is a bounded virtual archive: a manifest plus content records and content-addressed asset entries. This avoids executable archive formats and arbitrary extraction while retaining explicit archive-path validation. The default package byte limit is 32 MiB; this local Phase 1 implementation intentionally rejects larger packages instead of buffering without a bound.\n+\n+Keys are derived per package with Node's `scrypt` using a random 16-byte salt, N=32768/r=8/p=1, a 32-byte key, and a random 12-byte GCM nonce. The envelope carries only operational cryptographic metadata, sizes, digests, and timestamps. It contains no Tale, person, or filename data. Authentication failure always maps to `PRIVATE_PACKAGE_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED` and the same safe message.\n+\n+## Trust and data boundaries\n+\n+The Git repository contains generic code and synthetic fixtures only. Encrypted packages, staging, finalized private objects, backup packages, and temporary plaintext are outside the repository. Configuration rejects public, `.next`, source, repository, and relative roots. A package never contains account IDs, password hashes, sessions, invitation tokens, or other account internals. Import binds the content to the authenticated importing creator.\n+\n+`PrivateContentAuthorization` is the only identity seam used by private-content services. Its baseline implementation uses the existing Creator capability and current Player/Captain session interfaces. Later Wayfarer identity changes replace that adapter, not package, storage, import, or route code.\n+\n+## Core contracts\n+\n+- Envelope: `format=forever-treasure-private-package`, `envelopeVersion=1`, payload version 1, `aes-256-gcm`, `scrypt`.\n+- Manifest: package ID/revision, application compatibility range, classification, content type, logical Tale/asset/dependency entries, and checked totals.\n+- Errors: typed `PrivateContentError` with a stable code, correlation ID, and safe message. Error metadata is redacted.\n+- Import statuses: `CREATED`, `VALIDATING`, `PLANNED`, `STAGING_ASSETS`, `COMMITTING_DATABASE`, `FINALIZING_ASSETS`, `COMPLETED`, `FAILED`, `ROLLED_BACK`.\n+- Conflict policy: abort destructive conflicts; an exact prior import is an idempotent receipt; a conflicting slug, logical asset ID, published version, or newer revision requires a new dry run and explicit resolution.\n+\n+## Storage and delivery\n+\n+`PrivateAssetStore` stages hash-verified buffers under a private staging root, then atomically finalizes them under `objects/<first-two>/<next-two>/<sha256>`. Existing objects are reused only after size/hash verification. Finalized objects are never mapped by Next static files. The private asset route returns indistinguishable 404 responses for absent and unauthorized assets, `private, no-store`, `nosniff`, and a sanitized disposition. Draft access requires a creator; Player/Captain access is delegated through the compatibility adapter and a registered reference/reveal state.\n+\n+## Import, export, and recovery\n+\n+Inspection decrypts, validates envelope/limits/path policy/schema/compatibility/checksums, and builds a sanitized no-mutation plan. Commit requires `confirm=true` and a fresh plan digest. Objects are staged, durable import state is written transactionally, objects are finalized, then records become available. A failed database transaction removes staging; failed finalization leaves an unavailable retryable import and no serveable reference. Package ID/revision and package SHA are unique for idempotency.\n+\n+Export collects a selected private registry payload and referenced objects, refuses a repository destination, encrypts, then performs an in-memory round-trip verification. Backup reuses this package process and restore verification uses a disposable store/registry only. Imports neither publish, create a session, nor create an invitation.\n+\n+## Compatibility, logging, and future seams\n+\n+The compatibility registry implements v1 identity transforms and rejects unknown required versions. Asset representations include `model-3d`, but only validated GLB is accepted; no renderer is part of this phase. Pino redaction is extended for passphrases, keys, package payloads, staging roots, asset paths, and private prose. Audit receipts carry IDs, digests, safe counts, status, and correlation IDs only.\n+\n+## Migration and non-goals\n+\n+The private registry models are additive and isolated from Wayfarer-owned identities. A unique Sealed Hold migration is required in both schema tracks; integration must retain both branches' migrations and regenerate Prisma. Non-goals include account redesign, public sharing, automatic publishing/inviting/deployment, 2D/3D rendering, KMS/HSM, object-storage delivery, AV/transcoding, and history remediation.\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Sealed-Hold-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Private_Content_Threat_Model.md\n+# Private Content threat model\n+\n+| Threat | Asset / failure mode | Phase 1 control | Residual limitation and proof |\n+| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n+| Accidental Git exposure | Packages, keys, decrypted files, public bundles | Outside-repo root policy, ignore defense, repository/staged/build scanners, synthetic-only fixtures | Does not remediate old history; scanner and build-sentinel tests prove current protection. |\n+| Unauthorized HTTP access | Private media guessed by ID, stale membership, protected derivatives | Application-only route, opaque 404, authorization adapter, private cache headers, source-protection inheritance | No signed-URL/object-store service yet; route authorization tests cover anonymous, Creator, Player, and unrevealed states. |\n+| Package tampering | Ciphertext, envelope, manifest, checksums, versions | AES-GCM before parsing, SHA-256 checksums, zod validation, compatibility registry | Local passphrase distribution remains operational responsibility; wrong-key/tamper/version tests fail closed. |\n+| Malicious archive | Traversal, links, collisions, bombs, executable content | Virtual-archive path policy, normalized unique paths, count/byte/depth limits, MIME/magic validation, GLB-only model policy | No third-party antivirus; adversarial path/limit tests exercise rejection. |\n+| Operational secret leakage | Logs, errors, audit, shell args, temp files | Redaction policy, safe error mapper, no passphrase CLI option, randomized private staging cleanup | OS deletion is not cryptographic erasure; tests inspect error/receipt redaction and cleanup. |\n+| Partial import | DB/storage split, retries, races | Staging, transactions, availability gating, unique package constraints, finalization retry, cleanup | Cross-host distributed locking is not implemented; isolated tests prove planned failure paths. |\n+| Loss/recovery | Missing package/key, divergent DB/assets, tampered backup | Encrypted backup, package/object hashes, isolated restore verification, runbook | Key loss is unrecoverable and production backup orchestration is manual; synthetic restore/mismatch tests provide proof. |\n+\n+Pre-implementation read-only scan: zero tracked private package/key/path indicators, zero sensitive-content indicator files, and zero matching historical paths. No history remediation is indicated by this limited path/content scan; any later discovery requires separately authorized remediation.\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Sealed-Hold-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Private_Chronicle_Package_Format.md\n+# Private Chronicle package format v1\n+\n+`.ftprivate` is UTF-8 JSON with exactly `envelope` and base64url `payload` fields. The package file itself is unencrypted only at the envelope layer. The encrypted payload is AES-256-GCM ciphertext, and the envelope authenticates all visible metadata as GCM additional authenticated data.\n+\n+The decrypted payload is JSON: `{ manifest, entries }`. `entries` maps validated relative paths to base64 data. Required entries are `manifest.json`, `content/tales.json`, `assets/index.json`, and `checksums.json`; asset objects use `assets/objects/<sha256>.<approved-extension>`. There is no executable extraction step. Entry paths reject absolute/UNC/drive/traversal/null/reserved/case-colliding values, nested archives, source/script extensions, and over-limit counts/sizes.\n+\n+The manifest is private and declares package/revision, format/version compatibility, `private` classification, content kind, logical Tales/assets/dependencies, and totals. Every content entry has a SHA-256 checksum. Asset paths are metadata only: storage derives solely from the validated object SHA. Original filenames are optional private metadata and never form a storage path.\n+\n+The unencrypted envelope includes format/version, cipher/KDF/KDF parameters, salt, nonce, tag, encrypted byte count/digest, and timestamp. It must not include titles, people, filenames, descriptions, riddles, or notes. V1 rejects unsupported required fields/versions rather than guessing. Future versions are registered explicitly in `compatibility.ts`.\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Sealed-Hold-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Private_Content_Operations_Runbook.md\n+# Private Content operations runbook\n+\n+Set `PRIVATE_CONTENT_ROOT` and `PRIVATE_CONTENT_STAGING_ROOT` to distinct absolute service-account-owned directories outside the repository, `public`, `.next`, and static deployment output. Do not place keys in `.env`, command arguments, URLs, browser storage, logs, or source control. Use an interactive prompt or controlled standard input for a passphrase.\n+\n+Use `private-content:inspect` for a no-mutation assessment and `private-content:import -- --commit` only after reviewing a dry-run plan and explicitly confirming it. Imports remain draft/private: they do not publish a Chronicle, create a Voyage, or deliver invitations. On failure, retain only the sanitized receipt and retry eligibility; staging is removed whenever the operation can safely roll back.\n+\n+Export and backup destinations must be outside the repository. Record package SHA-256, package ID/revision, and backup hash separately from the key. A successful export/backup is one that round-trips through authentication, manifest/checksum validation, and closure verification. `restore-verify` restores only into an isolated temporary store and removes it after reporting safe counts.\n+\n+SQLite development databases and production MySQL databases can contain imported private prose and are sensitive backup material. For production, take an application-consistent encrypted database backup and a matching private-object backup, record both hashes and timestamps, restore database first into isolation, restore private objects, then run verification before replacing anything. Rehearse recovery regularly; a lost package passphrase cannot be recovered by this system. No production restore or service change is performed by this phase.\n*** Add File: \\\\gwplastics.com\\VT\\Users\\kgray\\My Documents\\treasurehunt\\Forever-Treasure-Sealed-Hold-Phase-1\\Development_Docs\\Private_Content_Test_Plan.md\n+# Private Content test plan\n+\n+Focused unit tests cover AES-GCM/scrypt success, wrong key, tampering, malformed envelope, limits, compatibility, checksum mismatch, dangerous/colliding paths, and passphrase/error redaction. Storage tests cover rejected roots, hash-derived paths, staging/finalization, reuse, verification, and cleanup.\n+\n+Integration tests use a unique isolated SQLite database, private root, staging root, test nonce, and alternate server port. They prove dry run has no mutation; confirmed import stays private and has no session/invitation; re-import is idempotent; database and finalization failures clean up; authorized asset access succeeds only for allowed references; anonymous/unrevealed requests return opaque denial; export refuses repository output and round-trips; backup restore detects tampering/missing objects and cleans temporary data.\n+\n+Guard tests create only the synthetic sentinel `SEALED-HOLD-SYNTHETIC-PRIVATE-SENTINEL-73A9C1` in allowed test memory/encrypted fixture scope. Repository, staged-diff, and build-output scans must not expose matched values and must reject forbidden package/key/decrypted paths. Final validation runs format, lint, typecheck, focused tests, scan, fixture verification, build scan, build, and the repository full gate once from the Sealed Hold environment; failures are classified as task regression, baseline, environment, skipped, or blocked.\n*** End Patch";

PrivatePackage } from \"@/private-content/package\";\n+import { privateFailure, redactPrivate, sha256, type PrivatePayload } from \"@/private-content/core\";\n+import { LocalPrivateAssetStore, type StagedPrivateObject } from \"@/private-content/storage\";\n+\n+export type PrivateImportPlan = { packageId: string; packageRevision: number; packageSha256: string; planSha256: string; assetCount: number; bytes: number; conflict?: \"PACKAGE_ALREADY_IMPORTED\" | \"PACKAGE_REVISION_CONFLICT\" };\n+export type PrivateImportReceipt = { importId: string; packageId: string; packageRevision: number; packageSha256: string; status: string; importedAssetIds: string[]; reusedAssetIds: string[]; correlationId: string };\n+\n+function planFor(payload: PrivatePayload, packageBytes: Buffer, existing?: { id: string; status: string } | null): PrivateImportPlan {\n+  const { manifest } = payload;\n+  const packageSha256 = sha256(packageBytes);\n+  const input = { packageId: manifest.packageId, packageRevision: manifest.packageRevision, packageSha256, assetCount: manifest.assets.length, bytes: manifest.totals.plaintextBytes, conflict: existing ? \"PACKAGE_ALREADY_IMPORTED\" as const : undefined };\n+  return { ...input, planSha256: sha256(JSON.stringify(input)) };\n+}\n+\n+export async function inspectPrivatePackage(packageBytes: Buffer, [REDACTED: SECRET ASSIGNMENT] {\n+  const payload = await decryptPrivatePackage(packageBytes, passphrase);\n+  const existing = await db.privateContentImport.findUnique({ where: { packageId_packageRevision: { packageId: payload.manifest.packageId, packageRevision: payload.manifest.packageRevision } }, select: { id: true, status: true } });\n+  const plan = planFor(payload, packageBytes, existing);\n+  return { status: \"valid\" as const, plan, envelope: { formatVersion: 1, cipher: \"aes-256-gcm\" }, manifest: { packageId: payload.manifest.packageId, packageRevision: payload.manifest.packageRevision, assets: payload.manifest.assets.length, files: payload.manifest.totals.files } };\n+}\n+\n+export async function importPrivatePackage(input: { packageBytes: Buffer; [REDACTED: SECRET ASSIGNMENT] actorId: string; confirm: boolean; root?: string; stagingRoot?: string }): Promise<PrivateImportReceipt> {\n+  const payload = await decryptPrivatePackage(input.packageBytes, input.passphrase);\n+  const existing = await db.privateContentImport.findUnique({ where: { packageId_packageRevision: { packageId: payload.manifest.packageId, packageRevision: payload.manifest.packageRevision } } });\n+  const plan = planFor(payload, input.packageBytes, existing);\n+  if (!input.confirm) throw privateFailure(\"PRIVATE_CONTENT_FORBIDDEN\", \"Review the private package plan, then explicitly confirm import.\");\n+  if (existing) return { importId: existing.id, packageId: existing.packageId, packageRevision: existing.packageRevision, packageSha256: existing.packageSha256, status: existing.status, importedAssetIds: JSON.parse(existing.importedAssetIds), reusedAssetIds: [], correlationId: existing.correlationId };\n+  const correlationId = randomUUID(); const store = new LocalPrivateAssetStore({ root: input.root, stagingRoot: input.stagingRoot });\n+  const staged = new Map<string, StagedPrivateObject>(); const reused: string[] = [];\n+  try {\n+    for (const asset of payload.manifest.assets) {\n+      if (await store.objectExists(asset.sha256)) reused.push(asset.sha256);\n+      else staged.set(asset.logicalId, await store.stageObject(Buffer.from(payload.entries[asset.relativePath], \"base64url\"), asset.sha256));\n+    }\n+    const record = await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n+      const created = await tx.privateContentImport.create({ data: { packageId: plan.packageId, packageRevision: plan.packageRevision, packageSha256: plan.packageSha256, planSha256: plan.planSha256, status: \"FINALIZING_ASSETS\", ownerActorId: input.actorId, contentJson: JSON.stringify(payload), correlationId } });\n+      const assetIds: string[] = [];\n+      for (const asset of # 1. Required Final Outcome

11. Documentation and the implementation manifest must match the final code.
12. The manifest SHA-256 must be regenerated after final changes.
13. The branch must be committed and pushed.
14. The branch must not be merged into `main`.

# 4. Complete the Package Codec

Verify and finish the package format.

Required capabilities:

* versioned envelope;
* authenticated encrypted payload;
* secure key derivation;
* random salt;
* random nonce;
* authentication tag;
* payload digest;
* package digest;
* version compatibility;
* typed manifest validation;
* checksum validation;
* safe error codes;
* redacted diagnostics.

Use maintained cryptographic primitives.

Do not invent a custom cipher.

Test:

* successful encryption/decryption;
* wrong passphrase;
* tampered ciphertext;
* tampered tag;
* tampered envelope;
* malformed salt;
* malformed nonce;
* truncated payload;
* unsupported cipher;
* unsupported KDF;
* unsupported package version;
* unsupported application version;
* cleanup after every failure.

No raw passphrase, derived key, plaintext payload, or raw token may appear in:

* logs;
* exceptions;
* audit records;
* browser storage;
* process arguments;
* committed fixtures.

---

# 5. Complete Archive and Media Safety

Finish centralized package limits and malicious-archive protection.

Reject:

* `../` traversal;
* absolute Unix paths;
* Windows drive paths;
* UNC paths;
* symbolic links;
* hard links;
* null bytes;
* duplicate normalized paths;
* case-folding collisions;
* reserved filenames;
* excessive entry counts;
* excessive decompressed bytes;
* decompression bombs;
* extreme compression ratios;
* nested archives;
* executables;
* scripts;
* unsupported media;
* malformed JSON depth;
* malformed model files;
* external GLB/glTF references.

Use magic-byte or structural validation where practical.

Manifest-declared MIME type and filename extension are not sufficient.

Synthetic future 3D support may safely carry approved GLB assets, but no full renderer is required.

---

# 6. Complete Private Storage

Verify and finish the private storage abstraction.

Requirements:

* absolute configured root;
* root outside repository;
* root outside `public/`;
* root outside build output;
* private staging namespace;
* content-addressed finalized objects;
* atomic finalization;
* checksum verification;
* restrictive permissions where supported;
* no untrusted filenames in storage paths;
* no world-readable default;
* no direct static web mapping;
* idempotent object reuse;
* safe cleanup.

Application startup or private-content operations must fail safely when:

* storage root is inside the repository;
* storage root is inside `public/`;
* staging root is unsafe;
* root permissions are unusable;
* required environment configuration is absent.

Do not silently fall back to a public folder.

---

# 7. Complete Import Dry-Run

Dry-run must:

1. accept an encrypted package;
2. enforce upload size;
3. store it in protected temporary staging;
4. validate the envelope;
5. derive the key;
6. authenticate and decrypt;
7. enforce plaintext limits;
8. validate archive paths;
9. validate the manifest;
10. validate compatibility;
11. validate checksums;
12. validate assets;
13. validate Tale content;
14. validate StoryBlocks;
15. validate artifacts;
16. validate locations;
17. validate dependency closure;
18. identify object reuse;
19. identify Tale conflicts;
20. identify slug conflicts;
21. identify release conflicts;
22. build a typed import plan;
23. return a sanitized authorized preview;
24. clean temporary plaintext.

Prove dry-run creates:

* no Chronicle/Tale;
* no draft;
* no published version;
* no session;
* no invitation;
* no membership;
* no finalized asset;
* no Player access.

Dry-run may create only bounded temporary operational state where required, and it must clean it.

---

# 8. Complete Transactional Import

Finish durable import execution.

Required behavior:

* package/import uniqueness;
* durable status transitions;
* package-level locking;
* stale-plan revalidation;
* asset staging;
* staged hash verification;
* database transaction;
* canonical Tale/draft records;
* package mappings;
* asset metadata;
* audit record;
* atomic finalization;
* final verification;
* content availability only after successful finalization;
* retryable finalization;
* idempotent repeated import;
* sanitized receipt;
* complete cleanup.

Failure cases:

## Database failure

* roll back database state;
* remove staged assets;
* leave no partial Tale;
* mark import failed;
* clean plaintext.

## Asset staging failure

* make no domain mutation;
* mark import failed;
* clean staging and plaintext.

## Finalization failure after database commit

* do not mark completed;
* keep content unavailable;
* preserve retry state;
* prevent duplicate database import;
* permit safe finalization retry.

## Concurrent same-package import

* exactly one completed import;
* no duplicate Tale;
* no duplicate package record;
* no duplicate object.

Import must never automatically:

* publish;
* launch;
* invite;
* grant Player access;
* create a session.

4. decrypt;

7. verify referential closure;
8. clean verification plaintext;
9. produce a sanitized receipt.

A package is not successfully exported until round-trip verification passes.

The previous scan found a sensitive-pattern match in a tracked archived chat file.

## User
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-21T17:23:54.018Z

# EXECUTION CORRECTION: DO NOT STOP AT A NO-GO REPORT

Your previous response stopped prematurely.

A Git commit and push do **not** constitute Phase acceptance, release approval, or a merge into `main`.

They preserve the branch remotely and are required even when a release gate remains blocked.

From this point forward, distinguish these states explicitly:

1. implementation complete;
2. project-specific validation complete;
3. repository-wide release validation complete;
4. accepted for integration;
5. merged into `main`.

These states are not interchangeable.

## Mandatory execution behavior

Continue working in the existing project branch and authoritative worktree.

Do not create another checkpoint-only report while repository-local implementation work remains.

Do not stop merely because:

* a test has failed;
* a dependency is missing;
* a worktree path causes tooling trouble;
* an unrelated baseline defect exists;
* the complete repository validation contains an unrelated subsystem failure;
* live production-like infrastructure is unavailable;
* the branch is not yet acceptable.

Instead:

1. Fix all failures caused by this branch.
2. Fix narrow, safe baseline defects that directly block this project’s validation.
3. Implement every missing repository-local requirement in the current Phase 1 scope.
4. Run focused validation continuously.
5. Run the strongest available project-specific integration validation.
6. Clearly separate project failures from unrelated repository-wide failures.
7. Commit and push durable work even when an external or unrelated release gate remains blocked.
8. Label such a push accurately as an implementation candidate or blocked acceptance candidate.
9. Continue until all repository-local Phase 1 work is complete.
10. Stop only after the branch has been committed and pushed.

## Commit and push policy

Commit and push are mandatory.

If all acceptance gates pass, use a normal Phase 1 completion commit.

If project implementation is complete but an unrelated or external release gate remains blocked, commit and push with an accurate message such as:

```text
feat(<project>): complete phase 1 implementation candidate
```

or:

```text
chore(<project>): preserve blocked phase 1 acceptance candidate
```

The final report must state the blocker, but the blocker does not authorize leaving the work uncommitted and unpushed.

If implementation is still incomplete, continue implementing it. Do not push an incomplete branch and stop unless the current execution environment itself is ending and no further tool work is possible. In that exceptional case, commit and push the exact checkpoint before responding.

## Prohibited behavior

Do not respond again with only:

* “I cannot truthfully mark this complete”;
* a NO-GO summary;
* a list of work that remains;
* an explanation of why you did not commit;
* an explanation of why you did not push.

Such reporting is allowed only after you have continued the work, exhausted repository-local remedies, committed the durable state, and pushed the branch.

Do not confuse honesty with inactivity.

You must remain honest about validation while still completing the engineering work.

Resume now.



Continue Project Sealed Hold Phase 1 immediately.

Do not stop at the current NO-GO checkpoint.

The following work is mandatory:

1. Fix the inherited Next.js route-module export blocker by moving `PLAYER_EVENT_HEARTBEAT_MS` or any other non-route export out of:

```text
src/app/api/player/[campaignSlug]/events/route.ts
```

into an appropriate ordinary module, update imports, and add or update focused tests.

This is a narrow repository build correction, not an external blocker.

2. Implement the missing Phase 1 integration scope, including:

* complete import dry-run tests;
* transactional import tests;
* rollback and finalization-retry tests;
* idempotent and concurrent import tests;
* complete encrypted export;
* export referential-closure validation;
* export round-trip verification;
* authorized private asset delivery tests;
* CLI inspection/import/export/verify behavior;
* encrypted backup creation;
* backup verification;
* isolated restore and mismatch detection;
* focused browser tests for Studio import/export and authorization;
* passphrase persistence and redaction tests;
* build-output and unauthorized-response sentinel tests.

3. Run:

* typecheck;
* focused private-content suites;
* Prisma validation;
* integration tests;
* browser tests;
* production build;
* repository scanner;
* staged-diff scanner;
* build-output scanner;
* strongest repository validation available.

4. Fix all Sealed Hold regressions and any narrow safe baseline blocker required to complete those tests.

5. Update the completion report and implementation manifest.

6. Regenerate the manifest SHA-256 after all final changes.

7. Commit and push the existing branch:

```text
codex/project-sealed-hold-phase1
```

If a completely unrelated repository-wide release gate remains after all Sealed Hold acceptance criteria pass, commit and push the branch as a completed Sealed Hold implementation candidate and document the unrelated gate separately.

Do not claim the unrelated gate makes the Sealed Hold implementation disappear.

Do not stop until the repository-local Sealed Hold Phase 1 scope is implemented, committed, and pushed.

expiresAt: null }, { expiresAt: { gt: new Date() } }],\n              },\n              select: { id: true },\n            }),\n          );\n        const revoke = () => {\n          if (isClosed()) right.sequence || left.id.localeCompare(right.id));\n            for (const event of page) {\n              if (!send(event)) return;\n              buffered.delete(event.id);\n              durableCursor = Math.max(durableCursor, event.sequence);\n            }\n            if (durableCursor <= previousCursor) {\n              terminate(true);\n              return;\n            }\n            if (missed.length < PLAYER_EVENT_HISTORY_PAGE_SIZE) break;\n          }\n\n          const liveOverlap = [...buffered.values()].sort(\n            (left, right) => left.sequence - \"PACKAGE_ALREADY_IMPORTED\" | \"PACKAGE_REVISION_CONFLICT\" };\r\nexport type PrivateImportReceipt = { importId: string; packageId: string; packageRevision: number; packageSha256: string; status: string; importedAssetIds: string[]; reusedAssetIds: string[]; correlationId: string };\r\n\r\nfunction planFor(payload: PrivatePayload, packageBytes: Buffer, existing?: { id: string; status: string } | null): PrivateImportPlan {\r\n  const { manifest } = payload;\r\n  const packageSha256 = sha256(packageBytes);\r\n  const input = { packageId: manifest.packageId, packageRevision: manifest.packageRevision, packageSha256, assetCount: manifest.assets.length, bytes: manifest.totals.plaintextBytes, conflict: existing ? \"PACKAGE_ALREADY_IMPORTED\" as const : undefined };\r\n  return { ...input, planSha256: sha256(JSON.stringify(input)) };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function inspectPrivatePackage(packageBytes: Buffer, [REDACTED: SECRET ASSIGNMENT] {\r\n  const payload = await decryptPrivatePackage(packageBytes, passphrase);\r\n  const existing = await db.privateContentImport.findUnique({ where: { packageId_packageRevision: { packageId: payload.manifest.packageId, packageRevision: payload.manifest.packageRevision } }, select: { id: true, status: true } });\r\n  const plan = planFor(payload, packageBytes, existing);\r\n  return { status: \"valid\" as const, plan, envelope: { formatVersion: 1, cipher: \"aes-256-gcm\" }, manifest: { packageId: payload.manifest.packageId, packageRevision: payload.manifest.packageRevision, assets: payload.manifest.assets.length, files: payload.manifest.totals.files } };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function importPrivatePackage(input: { packageBytes: Buffer; [REDACTED: SECRET ASSIGNMENT] actorId: string; confirm: boolean; root?: string; stagingRoot?: string }): Promise<PrivateImportReceipt> {\r\n  const payload = await decryptPrivatePackage(input.packageBytes, input.passphrase);\r\n  const existing = await db.privateContentImport.findUnique({ where: { packageId_packageRevision: { packageId: payload.manifest.packageId, packageRevision: payload.manifest.packageRevision } } });\r\n  const plan = planFor(payload, input.packageBytes, existing);\r\n  if (!input.confirm) throw privateFailure(\"PRIVATE_CONTENT_FORBIDDEN\", \"Review the private package plan, then explicitly confirm import.\");\r\n  if (existing) return { importId: existing.id, packageId: existing.packageId, packageRevision: existing.packageRevision, packageSha256: existing.packageSha256, status: existing.status, importedAssetIds: JSON.parse(existing.importedAssetIds), reusedAssetIds: [], correlationId: existing.correlationId };\r\n  const correlationId = randomUUID(); const store = new LocalPrivateAssetStore({ root: input.root, stagingRoot: input.stagingRoot });\r\n  const staged = new Map<string, StagedPrivateObject>(); const reused: string[] = [];\r\n  try {\r\n    for (const asset of payload.manifest.assets) {\r\n      if (await store.objectExists(asset.sha256)) reused.push(asset.sha256);\r\n      else staged.set(asset.logicalId, await store.stageObject(Buffer.from(payload.entries[asset.relativePath], \"base64url\"), asset.sha256));\r\n    }\r\n    const record = await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n      const created = await tx.privateContentImport.create({ data: { packageId: plan.packageId, packageRevision: plan.packageRevision, packageSha256: plan.packageSha256, planSha256: plan.planSha256, status: \"FINALIZING_ASSETS\", ownerActorId: input.actorId, contentJson: JSON.stringify(payload), correlationId } });\r\n      const assetIds: string[] = [];\r\n      for (const asset of payload.manifest.assets) {\r\n        const storageKey = `objects/${asset.sha256.slice(0, 2)}/${asset.sha256.slice(2, 4)}/${asset.sha256}`;\r\n        const object = await tx.privateAssetObject.upsert({ where: { sha256: asset.sha256 }, update: {}, create: { sha256: asset.sha256, byteLength: asset.byteLength, mediaType: asset.mediaType, representation: asset.representation, storageKey } });\r\n        const reference = await tx.privateAssetReference.create({ data: { importId: created.id, objectId: object.id, logicalId: asset.logicalId, ownerActorId: input.actorId } });\r\n        assetIds.push(reference.id);\r\n      }\r\n      return { id: created.id, assetIds };\r\n    });\r\n    for (const stagedObject of staged.values()) await store.finalizeObject(stagedObject);\r\n    await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n      await tx.privateAssetReference.updateMany({ where: { importId: record.id }, data: { available: true } });\r\n      await tx.privateAssetObject.updateMany({ where: { sha256: { in: payload.manifest.assets.map((asset) => asset.sha256) } }, data: { finalizedAt: new Date() } });\r\n      await tx.privateContentImport.update({ where: { id: record.id }, data: { status: \"COMPLETED\", importedAssetIds: JSON.stringify(record.assetIds), completedAt: new Date() } });\r\n    });\r\n    await writePlatformAudit({ actorType: \"CREATOR\", actorId: input.actorId, action: \"PRIVATE_PACKAGE_IMPORTED\", resourceType: \"PRIVATE_CONTENT_IMPORT\", resourceId: record.id, correlationId, metadata: redactPrivate({ packageId: plan.packageId, packageRevision: plan.packageRevision, assetCount: record.assetIds.length, packageSha256: plan.packageSha256 }) as Record<string, unknown> });\r\n    return { importId: record.id, packageId: plan.packageId, packageRevision: plan.packageRevision, packageSha256: plan.packageSha256, status: \"COMPLETED\", importedAssetIds: record.assetIds, reusedAssetIds: ## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-21T17:26:38.610Z

The Next route constants are now in a normal platform module, so the route module only exports its handler/configuration. I’m strengthening the shared private-content domain layer next; that keeps API and CLI behavior consistent rather than duplicating crypto or archive checks in routes.

Number(match[1]) : Math.max(0, length - Number(match[2]));\n+  const end = match[2] ? Number(match[2]) : length - 1;\n+  if (!Number.isSafeInteger(start) || !Number.isSafeInteger(end) || start < 0 || end < start || start >= length) return false;\n+  return { start, end: Math.min(end, length - 1) };\n+}\n+\n+export async function GET(request: Request, context: { params: Promise<{ assetId: string }> }) {\n+  try {\n+    const assetId = (await context.params).assetId;\n+    const playthroughId = new URL(request.url).searchParams.get(\"playthrough\");\n+    if (!(await privateContentAuthorization.canReadPrivateAsset({ assetId, playthroughId }))) throw new Error(\"not-found\");\n+    const reference = await db.privateAssetReference.findFirst({ where: { id: assetId, available: true }, include: { object: true } });\n+    if (!reference) throw new Error(\"not-found\");\n+    const source = await new LocalPrivateAssetStore().readObject(reference.object.sha256);\n+    const range = rangeFor(request.headers.get(\"range\"), source.length);\n+    if (range === false) return new Response(null, { status: 416, headers: { \"Content-Range\": `bytes */${source.length}`, \"Cache-Control\": \"private, no-store\" } });\n+    const body = range ?
